You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_57.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 57: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[57]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_57.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 57: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""
2	Live retriever — HONEST-REBUILD.
3	
4	Performs REAL retrieval using Serper (web) + Semantic Scholar (academic)
5	+ OpenAlex (canonicalization) and wires every candidate URL through the
6	honest-rebuild modules in one clean path:
7	
8	    1. Serper + Semantic Scholar bulk search
9	    2. Convert hits into SearchCandidate
10	    3. scope_query_validator on amplified queries
11	    4. prefetch_offtopic_filter on candidates (if embedder available)
12	    5. Fetch each URL's content (basic http.get) with size cap
13	    6. Classify each with tier_classifier
14	    7. Return list[CorpusSource] + evidence rows
15	
16	This is the live alternative to the pre-rebuild searcher.py path,
17	which had complex dependencies into src/agents/ that we're trying
18	to archive. Keeping the live retriever in polaris_graph/retrieval/
19	means the honest-rebuild pipeline is self-contained.
20	
21	Performance: rate-limited, caps total candidates. Designed to be
22	called ONCE per research question (not repeatedly in a loop).
23	"""
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import logging
27	import math
28	import os
29	import asyncio
30	import re
31	import sqlite3
32	import threading
33	import time
34	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
35	from pathlib import Path
36	from typing import Any, Optional
37	from urllib.parse import urlparse, urlsplit
38	
39	import httpx
40	
41	from src.polaris_graph.nodes.corpus_approval_gate import CorpusSource
42	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.prefetch_offtopic_filter import (
43	    SearchCandidate,
44	    filter_search_results,
45	)
46	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.scope_query_validator import (
47	    validate_amplified_queries,
48	)
49	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.query_decomposer import distill_keywords  # FX-18 (#1122)
50	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import shell_detector  # I-beatboth-001 (#1276): single-sourced shell vocab
51	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import title_body_consistency  # I-deepfix-001 B14 (#1358)
52	from src.polaris_graph.authority.authority_model import score_source_authority
53	from src.polaris_graph.authority.source_class import AuthoritySignals
54	# I-deepfix-001 (#1344) Bug B: the SAME retraction truthiness predicate the credibility
55	# engine + the generator gate use, so `is_retracted="false"` is correctly NOT retracted
56	# (bool("false") is True in Python — the coercion bug Codex iter-1 flagged).
57	from src.polaris_graph.authority.supersession import _is_truthy as _retraction_is_truthy
58	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.tier_classifier import (
59	    ClassificationResult,
60	    ClassificationSignals,
61	    TierLevel,
62	    _classify_source_tier_rules,
63	    _m2_dt,
64	    classify_source_tier,
65	)
66	# I-deepfix-001 Wave-4 CONTAMINATION (#1344) part C: ACTIVATE the DARK publication-date
67	# resolver (grep-proven 0 call sites before this wave). Module-top import so the wiring is
68	# monkeypatchable in tests (patch ``live_retriever.resolve_publication_date``); the module is
69	# a PURE htmldate-style cascade over ALREADY-fetched content (only `re`; no heavy models, no
70	# network) so importing it here has ZERO runtime side effect — the OFF path never CALLS it.
71	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.publication_date_resolver import resolve_publication_date
72	
73	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.live_retriever")
74	
75	
76	SERPER_ENDPOINT = "https://google.serper.dev/search"
77	S2_BULK_ENDPOINT = "https://api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search/bulk"
78	OPENALEX_ENDPOINT = "https://api.openalex.org/works"
79	OPENALEX_SOURCES_ENDPOINT = "https://api.openalex.org/sources"
80	
81	# Phase 0a (GH #983, ADDENDUM C5): root-level /works select= fieldset for the
82	# authority model. OpenAlex select= is ROOT-LEVEL ONLY (rejects nested props),
83	# so summary_stats / apc_prices / is_core / is_in_doaj come from a SEPARATE
84	# /sources/{id} fetch keyed by primary_location.source.id.
85	OPENALEX_WORKS_SELECT = (
86	    "id,doi,title,display_name,type,publication_year,publication_date,"
87	    "cited_by_count,is_retracted,primary_location,authorships"
88	)
89	OPENALEX_SOURCES_SELECT = (
90	    "id,is_core,is_in_doaj,apc_prices,summary_stats"
91	)
92	
93	# Versioned local cache for the authority-enrich payload (live path). The
94	# schema version is bumped (not CREATE-IF-NOT-EXISTS no-op) on any column
95	# change, with an ALTER/rebuild migration (C5 requirement).
96	AUTHORITY_CACHE_DB = Path(
97	    os.getenv("PG_AUTHORITY_CACHE_DB", "cache/authority_enrich.sqlite")
98	)
99	# I-ready-017 #1134 (Codex diff-gate P1-2): bumped 1->2 so cached enrich payloads
100	# written before the journal_only `is_retracted` + `openalex_venue` fields are
101	# REBUILT (not served stale) — a cached retracted article must not pass the
102	# journal_only predicate via a payload that predates the retraction field.
103	# I-deepfix-001 Codex wave-2 P1: bumped 2 -> 3 so cached payloads predating the
104	# `publication_date` field are REBUILT (not served stale) — a cached boundary-year
105	# row that lacks pub_date would otherwise defeat the MONTH-precision date window.
106	# I-deepfix-001 Wave-4 CONTAMINATION (#1344) part B: bumped 3 -> 4 so every enrich
107	# payload written under the PRE-VALIDATION OpenAlex title-search match logic is
108	# INVALIDATED and re-fetched under PG_OPENALEX_MATCH_VALIDATE — a cached row whose
109	# metadata came from a WRONG title-search paper (the 2019 Dwivedi mis-attach class)
110	# must not be served stale past the validator.
111	AUTHORITY_CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION = 4
112	
113	# Hard caps
114	DEFAULT_MAX_SERPER = int(os.getenv("PG_LIVE_MAX_SERPER", "20"))
115	DEFAULT_MAX_S2 = int(os.getenv("PG_LIVE_MAX_S2", "20"))
116	# I-deepfix-001 D3 breadth (2026-06-29): the fetch BUDGET is the §-1.3 disclosed
117	# bound (the ONLY thing that limits how far down the demote-not-drop cosine-ordered
118	# candidate list we fetch). The legacy default of 40 was the dominant breadth lever
119	# on the diced-preflight drb_72 fixture (926 discovered -> 166 selected -> 149
120	# fetched: a 760-candidate count-cut). Raised to a generous 200 (5x) — cost is never
121	# the constraint, time is, and the fetch is BOUNDED-PARALLEL (worker ceiling 14, per-
122	# host cap 4) so a generous cap stays wall-time sane. Still env-overridable (LAW VI):
123	# a validation run that wants the FULL discovered pool fetched (dropped_pre_fetch==0)
124	# sets PG_LIVE_FETCH_CAP higher (and the sweep's own PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP, which
125	# overrides this default on run_live_retrieval's main lane). A CAP, not a target —
126	# billed by actual fetches, so a generous cap is free insurance.
127	DEFAULT_FETCH_CAP = int(os.getenv("PG_LIVE_FETCH_CAP", "200"))
128	# I-deepfix-001 U31 fetch-fidelity (2026-07-01): the legacy per-source extract
129	# cap of 25000 chars cut 75-87% of long clinical papers mid-body (a full
130	# systematic review / guideline PDF is ~100K-190K chars; at 25K only the
131	# abstract + intro survived, so downstream chunk/embed/retrieval never saw the
132	# methods/results/discussion that carry the dosing, contraindication and
133	# population claims). Raised to a generous 300000 (12x) so long papers are
134	# retained whole. This is the FETCH/extract fidelity cap (what is stored per
135	# source for chunking), NOT a single-prompt cap — the prompt-facing cap lives
136	# separately in frame_fetcher (`_M66_CONTENT_CAP`). A CAP, not a target: sources
137	# are stored at their real length, so a generous cap only helps the rare long
138	# doc and costs nothing on the common short one. Still env-overridable (LAW VI)
139	# via PG_LIVE_CONTENT_MAX — a memory-constrained box can lower it.
140	DEFAULT_CONTENT_MAX_CHARS = int(os.getenv("PG_LIVE_CONTENT_MAX", "300000"))
141	DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT = float(os.getenv("PG_LIVE_HTTP_TIMEOUT", "20"))
142	
143	# I-fetch-003 (#1175 / BB5-C02): parallel-fetch worker-pool sizing. When
144	# PG_LIVE_RETRIEVER_MAX_WORKERS is UNSET, the pool scales with the candidate
145	# count: min(_CEILING, max(_FLOOR, len(candidates) // _PER_CANDIDATE)). Named
146	# constants (LAW VI — no magic numbers). Floor keeps small corpora at the
147	# legacy default of 8; ceiling caps the pool so a huge corpus cannot spawn an
148	# unbounded thread count; per-candidate divisor sets the ramp.
149	_FETCH_WORKERS_FLOOR = 8
150	# I-fetch-lock (FETCH SECTION 1 LOCKED): 48 -> 14. A full 921-source fetch at 14
151	# workers achieved 846/921 = 91.9% success with ZERO crawler exceptions; the same
152	# corpus at 48 workers crashed the crawler (249 exceptions, 573 = 62% success) and
153	# blew the container cgroup pids.max via headless-browser fan-out. 14 is the locked
154	# default (band 14-16); still env-overridable via PG_LIVE_RETRIEVER_MAX_WORKERS (an
155	# operator may RAISE it). Faithfulness-neutral: fetch concurrency only.
156	_FETCH_WORKERS_CEILING = 14
157	_FETCH_WORKERS_PER_CANDIDATE = 16
158	# Mirror of parallel_fetch.DEFAULT_PER_BACKEND_LIMIT (4) used as the default
159	# per-HOST concurrency cap; imported as a named constant rather than hardcoded.
160	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.parallel_fetch import (  # noqa: E402
161	    DEFAULT_PER_BACKEND_LIMIT as _PARALLEL_FETCH_DEFAULT_PER_BACKEND_LIMIT,
162	)
163	# I-fetch-003 (#1175 / AC3): WARN floor for the new fetch_success_rate
164	# retrieval diagnostic. Env-overridable; below this, a loud warning fires.
165	_FETCH_SUCCESS_RATE_WARN_FLOOR = float(
166	    os.getenv("PG_LIVE_FETCH_SUCCESS_RATE_WARN_FLOOR", "0.5")
167	)
168	
169	# BB5-C05 (#1177): "fetched-200-but-empty-extract" thresholds. A fetch counts
170	# as this DISTINCT bucket when the backend returned a non-trivial raw body
171	# (>= _EXTRACT_NONEMPTY_RAW_FLOOR chars) yet the extractor chain (trafilatura →
172	# readability → regex) yielded fewer than _EXTRACT_EMPTY_FLOOR usable chars.
173	# Named constants (LAW VI — no magic numbers); env-overridable.
174	_EXTRACT_NONEMPTY_RAW_FLOOR = int(
175	    os.getenv("PG_FETCH_NONEMPTY_RAW_FLOOR", "200")
176	)
177	_EXTRACT_EMPTY_FLOOR = int(
178	    os.getenv("PG_FETCH_EMPTY_EXTRACT_FLOOR", "50")
179	)
180	
181	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
182	# F14 (GH #1245 / D9, D10): paywall-stub min-body gate.
183	#
184	# A fetch that returns a short body from a demanded paywalled journal was
185	# logged status="ok" with an empty refetch ledger — a DEAD fetch masquerading
186	# as a good source. This gate makes a sub-floor body a `stub` (fail-LOUD,
187	# ok=False), NOT `ok`, so the refetch ladder / down-weight semantics kick in
188	# instead of admitting an empty shell to the evidence pool.
189	#
190	# DEFAULT 0 = OFF = byte-identical to prior behavior (every existing fetch that
191	# returned non-empty content stays ok=True). The live cert sweep sets this to
192	# 1000 (a real journal article is tens of thousands of chars; a paywall shell
193	# is a few hundred). A CAP-not-target: it never drops a long body, only flags a
194	# short one. Set PG_FETCH_MIN_BODY_CHARS=1000 on the run slate. The OA resolver
195	# (Unpaywall / PMC-BioC / Zyte) gets first chance to upgrade the body ABOVE the
196	# floor before the stub verdict is rendered — so this STRENGTHENS honesty,
197	# never relaxes it (LAW II fail-loud; §-1.3 the faithfulness path is untouched).
198	# Read at CALL time (LAW VI — env-overridable per run; not frozen at import).
199	def _fetch_min_body_chars() -> int:
200	    try:
201	        return int(os.getenv("PG_FETCH_MIN_BODY_CHARS", "0"))
202	    except ValueError:
203	        return 0
204	
205	# F14: publisher hosts that overwhelmingly serve paywalled article bodies via
206	# the free fetch chain (the free backends 403 / return a few-hundred-char
207	# abstract shell). When ZYTE_API_KEY is present, these are routed to Zyte FIRST
208	# (a real body instead of a shell); when it is ABSENT, a LOUD warning fires so
209	# a Zyte-blind run is auditable instead of a silent no-op (the access_bypass
210	# Zyte fallback was a SILENT no-op without the key). Substring match on the
211	# lowercased netloc; env-extendable via PG_PAYWALL_PUBLISHER_HOSTS (comma-sep,
212	# additive). Never a hard DROP — only a routing + loud-warning signal.
213	_PAYWALL_PUBLISHER_HOSTS_DEFAULT = (
214	    "sciencedirect.com",
215	    "elsevier.com",
216	    "linkinghub.elsevier.com",
217	    "onlinelibrary.wiley.com",
218	    "link.springer.com",
219	    "nature.com",
220	    "tandfonline.com",
221	    "journals.sagepub.com",
222	    "academic.oup.com",
223	    "nejm.org",
224	    "thelancet.com",
225	    "jamanetwork.com",
226	    "bmj.com",
227	    "cell.com",
228	    "ahajournals.org",
229	    "annualreviews.org",
230	)
231	
232	
233	def _paywall_publisher_hosts() -> tuple[str, ...]:
234	    """F14: the paywalled-publisher host list (default + env-additive). Read at
235	    call time so PG_PAYWALL_PUBLISHER_HOSTS can extend it per run (LAW VI)."""
236	    extra = os.getenv("PG_PAYWALL_PUBLISHER_HOSTS", "").strip()
237	    hosts = list(_PAYWALL_PUBLISHER_HOSTS_DEFAULT)
238	    if extra:
239	        hosts.extend(
240	            h.strip().lower() for h in extra.split(",") if h.strip()
241	        )
242	    return tuple(hosts)
243	
244	
245	def _is_paywall_publisher_url(url: str) -> bool:
246	    """F14: True iff the URL's host is a known paywalled publisher (substring
247	    match on the lowercased netloc). Pure, no network."""
248	    if not url:
249	        return False
250	    try:
251	        netloc = (urlparse(url).netloc or "").lower()
252	    except Exception:
253	        return False
254	    if not netloc:
255	        return False
256	    return any(h in netloc for h in _paywall_publisher_hosts())
257	
258	
259	# F30 (GH #1245): repository / landing / abstract-page markers. A source whose
260	# fetched body is a publication-record / landing / abstract page (NOT the full
261	# article text) cannot ground a methods/results claim — methods do not live on
262	# a landing page. These are the literal markers the free fetch backends emit
263	# when they land on a repository record page (Jina's "URL Source:" header, an
264	# APA-style citation block, a "Publication status:" record field). Detection
265	# DOWN-WEIGHTS + FLAGS the row (it stays in the pool at low weight per §-1.3),
266	# it NEVER hard-drops. Matched case-insensitively on the body head only.
267	_LANDING_PAGE_MARKERS = (
268	    "url source:",
269	    "## apa style",
270	    "apa style",
271	    "publication status:",
272	    "this record",
273	    "view record",
274	    "full text not available",
275	    "request full text",
276	    "abstract only",
277	)
278	# Defaults for the landing-marker head window + max body length. Read at CALL
279	# time (Codex diff-gate P2 iter-2 — env-overridable per run, not frozen at
280	# import). A real full-text body may quote a marker deep in its references, so we
281	# only inspect the head; and a landing/abstract page is short AND marker-bearing,
282	# so a long body with a head-of-body citation is NOT flagged unless it is short.
283	_LANDING_MARKER_HEAD_CHARS_DEFAULT = 1500
284	_LANDING_PAGE_MAX_CHARS_DEFAULT = 3000
285	
286	
287	def _is_landing_or_abstract_page(content: str) -> bool:
288	    """F30: True iff the fetched body looks like a repository/landing/abstract
289	    RECORD page rather than full article text. Keys on SPECIFIC landing markers
290	    AND a short body so a long full-text article that merely cites one of these
291	    phrases is never false-flagged. Pure heuristic; no network. Thresholds are
292	    read at CALL time via `_env_int` (Codex diff-gate P2 iter-2 — env-overridable
293	    per run, not frozen at import)."""
294	    if not content:
295	        return False
296	    stripped = content.strip()
297	    if len(stripped) > _env_int(
298	        "PG_LANDING_PAGE_MAX_CHARS", _LANDING_PAGE_MAX_CHARS_DEFAULT
299	    ):
300	        return False
301	    head = stripped[
302	        : _env_int("PG_LANDING_MARKER_HEAD_CHARS", _LANDING_MARKER_HEAD_CHARS_DEFAULT)
303	    ].lower()
304	    return any(marker in head for marker in _LANDING_PAGE_MARKERS)
305	
306	
307	# FIX 2 (I-deepfix-001 composition-collapse plan, GH #1344): citation-metadata
308	# "SHELL" markers. A 200-OK fetch whose VISIBLE body is a citation-EXPORT widget
309	# (Frey-Osborne ora.ox.ac.uk "[BibTeX][EndNote]"), site/episode NAVIGATION
310	# (youreverydayai "Episode Categories:" / "Related Episodes:" / "Join the
311	# discussion"), or a bare title followed by a raw "@article{...}" BibTeX entry
312	# (a Semantic-Scholar record stub) is captured VERBATIM as the grounding span
313	# even though it carries NO article prose — starving otherwise-clean baskets.
314	# classify_block_page and _is_landing_or_abstract_page are both BLIND to this
315	# class. These are HIGH-PRECISION literals: each is an export-tool / nav / raw-
316	# BibTeX artefact, never rendered article language, so a full-text article that
317	# merely quotes a BibTeX block deep in its own references is NEVER flagged (the
318	# short-body gate below is the primary guard: a real article is > the landing
319	# max). Matched case-insensitively on the body head only.
320	_CITATION_METADATA_SHELL_MARKERS = (
321	    "[bibtex]",
322	    "[endnote]",
323	    "export_record",
324	    "export record",
325	    "episode categories:",
326	    "related episodes:",
327	    "join the discussion",
328	    "@article{",
329	)
330	# Head window for the citation-shell scan. Read at CALL time (LAW VI). Distinct
331	# knob from the landing-marker window so the two signals stay independently
332	# tunable.
333	_CITATION_SHELL_HEAD_CHARS_DEFAULT = 1500
334	# Master flag env name for the citation-metadata-shell signal (default OFF).
335	_ENV_CITATION_SHELL_REFETCH = "PG_CITATION_SHELL_REFETCH"
336	
337	
338	def _citation_shell_refetch_enabled() -> bool:
339	    """FIX 2: master switch for the citation-metadata-shell signal. Default OFF —
340	    when ``PG_CITATION_SHELL_REFETCH`` is unset the signal is never computed by
341	    the caller and the fetch path is BYTE-IDENTICAL. When ON, a flagged shell
342	    joins the EXISTING forced-Zyte re-fetch + §-1.3 down-weight disposition
343	    (identical to how F30 landing pages are handled: down-weight + disclose,
344	    NEVER hard-drop). LAW VI: env-overridable; recognized truthy 1/true/on/yes."""
345	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_CITATION_SHELL_REFETCH, "").strip().lower() in (
346	        "1", "true", "on", "yes",
347	    )

349	
350	def _is_citation_metadata_shell(content: str) -> bool:
351	    """FIX 2 (I-deepfix-001 composition-collapse plan): True iff the fetched body
352	    is a citation-EXPORT / site-NAV / bare-BibTeX SHELL rather than article text.
353	
354	    Structurally mirrors ``_is_landing_or_abstract_page``: SHORT-body-gated
355	    (reuses ``PG_LANDING_PAGE_MAX_CHARS`` <= 3000 so a full-text article that
356	    quotes a BibTeX block in its references is NEVER flagged) + head-window scan
357	    + HIGH-PRECISION export-tool / nav literals only. Pure heuristic; no network.
358	    Thresholds read at CALL time via ``_env_int`` (LAW VI — env-overridable per
359	    run, not frozen at import). This predicate is PURE and always defined; the
360	    ``_citation_shell_refetch_enabled`` master flag gates whether the CALLER
361	    computes + acts on it, so the OFF path stays byte-identical."""
362	    if not content:
363	        return False
364	    stripped = content.strip()
365	    if len(stripped) > _env_int(
366	        "PG_LANDING_PAGE_MAX_CHARS", _LANDING_PAGE_MAX_CHARS_DEFAULT
367	    ):
368	        return False
369	    head = stripped[
370	        : _env_int(
371	            "PG_CITATION_SHELL_HEAD_CHARS", _CITATION_SHELL_HEAD_CHARS_DEFAULT
372	        )
373	    ].lower()
374	    return any(marker in head for marker in _CITATION_METADATA_SHELL_MARKERS)

377	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
378	# F15 (GH #1245 / D11): per-URL refetch cap + negative cache.
379	#
380	# A DEAD DOI / URL was refetched ~5.5x per URL across the expansion / deepener /
381	# agentic stages because `refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics` had NO memory
382	# of prior failures — every section that wanted to ground a claim re-paid the
383	# full AccessBypass cascade (Crawl4AI / Jina / Firecrawl / proxy) for the SAME
384	# dead URL. This adds:
385	#   - a per-URL attempt counter so a FAILING URL is fetched at most _REFETCH_CAP
386	#     times (cap=2 => up to two real attempts before a permanent skip — a
387	#     transient failure still gets its retry budget), and
388	#   - a negative cache that a URL enters ONLY once it has both FAILED and reached
389	#     the cap, so subsequent requests short-circuit with NO network call and the
390	#     skip is honestly attributable to the cap.
391	# Codex diff-gate P1 (#1245): the attempt budget — NOT a mark-dead-on-first-
392	# failure — is the cap. A URL is refetched up to `cap` times; only at exhaustion
393	# is it cached. This preserves the retry budget the spec requires ("refetched at
394	# most the cap") while still killing the ~5.5x re-fetch storm.
395	#
396	# Codex diff-gate P1 iter-2 + iter-3 (#1245): the SKIP decision gates ONLY on
397	# SETTLED FAILURES reaching the cap (the negative cache). It does NOT gate on
398	# in-flight/concurrent reservations. Rationale (iter-3 P1): gating on in-flight
399	# would HARD-SKIP a concurrent caller of a LIVE URL whose reserved fetches will
400	# SUCCEED — wrongly suppressing a fetch that could succeed, which violates the
401	# §-1.3 "never suppress a fetch that could succeed" / successes-uncapped
402	# constraint. A SUCCESS never counts toward the cap. Only FAILED fetches count;
403	# once a URL has FAILED `cap` times it is cached and permanently short-circuited.
404	#
405	# Concurrency honesty: in the real call topology the refetch loop is SERIAL (the
406	# `for anchor in primary_trial_anchors` grounding loop), so a dead URL is fetched
407	# exactly `cap` times. Under hypothetical TRUE concurrency a dead URL could see a
408	# small bounded OVERSHOOT (callers already in-flight when the cap-th failure
409	# populates the cache) — that is an acceptable perf trade-off; it NEVER over-
410	# suppresses (the iter-3 constraint), and it still kills the ~5.5x storm. A
411	# threading lock guards every read/write of the shared dicts.
412	#
413	# The cache is process-local + bounded; it is a PERFORMANCE de-dup, NOT a
414	# faithfulness gate — it only stops repeating a fetch that ALREADY failed cap
415	# times, never suppresses a fetch that could succeed. Default cap 2; env-tunable.
416	_REFETCH_CAP_DEFAULT = 2
417	# Per-URL count of SETTLED failed attempts (a success never counts toward the cap).
418	_refetch_failures: dict[str, int] = {}
419	# URLs whose SETTLED failures have reached the cap (the permanent-skip set).
420	_refetch_negative_cache: set[str] = set()
421	_refetch_cache_lock = threading.Lock()
422	
423	
424	def _refetch_cap() -> int:
425	    """F15: per-URL refetch cap, read at CALL time (LAW VI — env-overridable per
426	    run). <= 0 disables the cap (a failing URL may be retried without bound — the
427	    legacy pre-F15 behavior)."""
428	    try:
429	        return int(os.getenv("PG_REFETCH_PER_URL_CAP", str(_REFETCH_CAP_DEFAULT)))
430	    except ValueError:
431	        return _REFETCH_CAP_DEFAULT
432	
433	
434	def reset_refetch_cache() -> None:
435	    """F15: clear the per-URL failure counters + negative cache. Called at the
436	    start of each retrieval run (and by tests) so the bounded cache does not leak
437	    across independent runs/vectors in a long-lived process."""
438	    with _refetch_cache_lock:
439	        _refetch_failures.clear()
440	        _refetch_negative_cache.clear()
441	
442	
443	def _refetch_try_acquire(url: str) -> bool:
444	    """F15: True => caller MAY fetch; False => skip because the URL has FAILED the
445	    cap number of times (it is in the negative cache). Gates ONLY on settled
446	    failures — NEVER on in-flight/concurrent reservations — so a concurrent caller
447	    of a LIVE URL is never wrongly skipped (Codex diff-gate iter-3 P1: never
448	    suppress a fetch that could succeed). Thread-safe (single lock)."""
449	    if not url:
450	        return True
451	    with _refetch_cache_lock:
452	        return url not in _refetch_negative_cache
453	
454	
455	def _refetch_settle(url: str, *, succeeded: bool) -> None:
456	    """F15: settle the outcome of a fetch. A SUCCESS is a no-op (a live URL never
457	    counts toward the cap and may be re-grounded by many sections). A FAILURE
458	    increments the settled-failure count and, at the cap, caches the URL so
459	    further requests short-circuit. Thread-safe (single lock)."""
460	    if not url or succeeded:
461	        return
462	    _cap = _refetch_cap()
463	    with _refetch_cache_lock:
464	        n = _refetch_failures.get(url, 0) + 1
465	        _refetch_failures[url] = n
466	        if _cap > 0 and n >= _cap:
467	            _refetch_negative_cache.add(url)
468	
469	
470	def _refetch_should_skip(url: str) -> bool:
471	    """F15: True iff this URL has SETTLED-failed to the cap (permanently cached).
472	    Equivalent to `not _refetch_try_acquire(url)`; kept as a pure read for tests /
473	    diagnostics. Thread-safe."""
474	    if not url:
475	        return False
476	    with _refetch_cache_lock:
477	        return url in _refetch_negative_cache
478	
479	
480	def _refetch_record_failure(url: str) -> None:
481	    """F15: record a failure for a URL acquired OUTSIDE the try/settle protocol
482	    (e.g. a test or a non-reserved path). Increments the settled-failure count and
483	    caches at the cap. Thread-safe."""
484	    if not url:
485	        return
486	    _cap = _refetch_cap()
487	    with _refetch_cache_lock:
488	        n = _refetch_failures.get(url, 0) + 1
489	        _refetch_failures[url] = n
490	        if _cap > 0 and n >= _cap:
491	            _refetch_negative_cache.add(url)
492	
493	
494	def _credibility_redesign_enabled() -> bool:
495	    """F15/F30: master switch for the WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE redesign (§-1.3 /
496	    I-arch-002 #1246), mirroring ``evidence_selector._credibility_redesign_enabled``.
497	    Default OFF — when ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN`` is unset, the
498	    content-starved / landing-page rows stay on the legacy HARD-DROP path so the
499	    OFF path is byte-identical. When ON, those rows are DOWN-WEIGHTED (kept in the
500	    pool at low weight) instead of dropped."""
501	    return os.environ.get("PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN", "").strip().lower() in (
502	        "1", "true", "yes", "on",
503	    )

506	# F15/F30: the low retrieval weight stamped on a down-weighted (content-starved
507	# or landing-page) row so the composition layer can rank it last while still
508	# carrying it (§-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER). A small positive float so it never ties
509	# with a real full-text row but is never zero (zero would let a falsy `or`
510	# launder it back to a default). Read at CALL time (Codex diff-gate P2 iter-2 —
511	# env-overridable per run, not frozen at import). LAW VI — no magic number.
512	_DOWN_WEIGHT_RETRIEVAL_DEFAULT = 0.05
513	
514	
515	def _down_weight_retrieval() -> float:
516	    try:
517	        return float(os.getenv("PG_DOWN_WEIGHT_RETRIEVAL", str(_DOWN_WEIGHT_RETRIEVAL_DEFAULT)))
518	    except (TypeError, ValueError):

522	def _row_degraded_flags(tier_result: Any, *, recovered: bool = False) -> dict[str, Any]:
523	    """I-deepfix-001 (Codex P1 #2 — no-laundering): the degraded-flag keys to merge onto a
524	    grounded evidence row so the corpus-adequacy grounded-content count
525	    (``corpus_adequacy_gate.count_grounded_rows``) EXCLUDES a source the tier layer already
526	    flagged ``tier_result.fetch_degraded`` (a short KNOWN-scholarly-venue / DOI stub — it keeps
527	    its venue-authority TIER weight but must not count as grounded content, so venue authority can
528	    never launder a contentless stub into "adequate"; see ``tier_classifier`` B17).
529	
530	    The tier classifier sets ``fetch_degraded`` but the live row previously copied only
531	    ``tier_result.tier.value``, so a stub that is NOT otherwise ``content_starved`` /
532	    ``landing_page`` / ``fetch_failed`` still counted as grounded. Returns ``{'fetch_degraded':
533	    True}`` only when the tier layer flagged it; ``{}`` otherwise, so a normal (non-degraded) row is
534	    BYTE-IDENTICAL.
535	
536	    ``recovered`` (BUG-B02/B04 forced-refetch): the tier ``fetch_degraded`` is a
537	    CLASSIFICATION-TIME signal computed on the ORIGINAL stub body. When a forced Zyte re-fetch later
538	    UPGRADED the stub to full text, the row proceeds as a NORMAL full-text row and that stale flag
539	    MUST NOT be propagated (the existing design contract — "recovered rows have degraded flags never
540	    set"). Returns ``{}`` when ``recovered`` regardless of the stale tier flag.
541	
542	    Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: adds a LABEL only — never a claim/span/citation, never the faithfulness
543	    engine, and the row keeps its tier WEIGHT (§-1.3 WEIGHT-AND-LABEL, never a drop)."""
544	    if recovered:
545	        return {}
546	    if getattr(tier_result, "fetch_degraded", False):
547	        return {"fetch_degraded": True}
548	    return {}
549

551	# I-deepfix-001 U10 (Codex P1): the rule name the tier classifier fires for BOTH the
552	# OpenAlex retraction flag AND a title-only retraction / withdrawal marker
553	# (tier_classifier._detect_retraction_marker -> matched_rules "R0_retracted").
554	_TIER_RETRACTION_RULE = "R0_retracted"
555	
556	
557	def _row_is_retracted(oa: Any, tier_result: Any) -> bool:
558	    """True iff the grounded evidence row must carry ``is_retracted=True`` for the
559	    generator retraction grounding gate (``retraction_gate.partition_pool`` keys on
560	    exactly this row flag).
561	
562	    Fires when EITHER:
563	      * the OpenAlex enrichment flags the paper retracted (the legacy leg), OR
564	      * the tier classifier fired its ``R0_retracted`` rule — which ALSO catches a
565	        TITLE-ONLY retraction / withdrawal marker whose OpenAlex flag was UNSET
566	        (I-deepfix-001 U10: a retracted paper re-deposited on a preprint host). Before
567	        this fix the row was flagged only from the OpenAlex leg, so a title-marker
568	        retracted paper entered the grounding pool with a non-empty ``direct_quote``.
569	
570	    Placement / credibility metadata only. The FROZEN faithfulness engine (strict_verify /
571	    NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance span-grounding) is UNTOUCHED — this feeds the SEPARATE
572	    clinical-safety retraction gate, not any faithfulness check. Fail-open: on any missing
573	    signal the row is NOT flagged (a source with no retraction info grounds normally)."""
574	    if _retraction_is_truthy(oa, "is_retracted"):
575	        return True
576	    matched = getattr(tier_result, "matched_rules", None) or ()
577	    return _TIER_RETRACTION_RULE in matched

580	@dataclass
581	class LiveRetrievalResult:
582	    classified_sources: list[CorpusSource]
583	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]]
584	    total_candidates_pre_filter: int
585	    candidates_kept_by_scope: int
586	    candidates_kept_by_offtopic: int
587	    candidates_fetched: int
588	    candidates_failed_fetch: int
589	    api_calls: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
590	    notes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
591	    # #958 (S2): fail-loud corpus-truncation signal. True when the post-fetch
592	    # loop budget (PG_POST_FETCH_LOOP_BUDGET) broke mid-corpus, leaving later
593	    # candidates unclassified. Defaults keep existing constructors valid.
594	    corpus_truncated: bool = False
595	    candidates_total: int = 0
596	    candidates_processed: int = 0
597	    # I-deepfix-001 item 4 (#1344): RETRIEVAL-PHASE wall telemetry (render-PASS
598	    # partial, DISTINCT from corpus_truncated). True iff the per-question retrieval
599	    # deadline (PG_RETRIEVAL_WALL_SECONDS) tripped in EITHER the search fan-out or
600	    # the post-fetch classify loop, so the run handed off a PARTIAL corpus and
601	    # rendered (it did NOT die on a bare timeout, and it is NOT gated out the way
602	    # corpus_truncated is). `retrieval_queries_skipped` = planned sub-queries never
603	    # fired; `retrieval_candidates_unclassified` = fetched bodies not reached before
604	    # the wall. Defaults keep existing constructors valid + byte-identical OFF path
605	    # (the wall never trips within budget => all False/0).
606	    retrieval_wall_hit: bool = False
607	    retrieval_queries_skipped: int = 0
608	    retrieval_candidates_unclassified: int = 0
609	    # I-deepfix-001 P1-2 (#1344): True iff the B4 semantic relevance scorer was
610	    # REQUESTED (PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE=1) but the semantic embedder was
611	    # unavailable so it fell back LOUDLY to the legacy lexical cut (live_retriever
612	    # ~4423). The winner-firing gate reads this as a SECOND W6/B4 dark signal
613	    # (independent of evidence_selector._SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_CACHE): a requested
614	    # semantic winner that silently degraded to lexical is NOT firing. Default
615	    # False keeps existing constructors valid + the byte-identical OFF path (the
616	    # gate is never requested when the B4 gate is OFF).
617	    semantic_relevance_fell_back: bool = False
618	    # I-ready-017 #1134: journal_only metadata sidecar, keyed by canonical URL.
619	    # Populated ONLY on the journal_only ON path (None = OFF = byte-identical).
620	    # Carries the per-source journal-article signals (openalex pub_type /
621	    # source_type / is_peer_reviewed / is_retracted / doi / venue) that the
622	    # citeability predicate needs; merged across retrieval stages by the sweep.
623	    journal_metadata_sidecar: dict[str, Any] | None = None
624	    # I-fetch-003 (#1175 / AC3): NEW retrieval-throughput diagnostics, emitted
625	    # as SIBLING fields (NOT folded into api_calls: dict[str, int] — that
626	    # contract stays unwidened). None when the parallel-fetch path did not run
627	    # (serial fallback or no candidates). fetch_success_rate = usable / (usable
628	    # + failed); parallel_completion_rate = completed-in-deadline / submitted.
629	    fetch_success_rate: float | None = None
630	    parallel_completion_rate: float | None = None
631	    fetch_workers: int | None = None
632	    distinct_hosts: int | None = None
633	    # I-ready-017 Task 2a (#1204): ADDITIVE source-funnel telemetry (read-only).
634	    # Persists existing-but-unsaved retrieval counts so the ~90% pre-fetch
635	    # source loss is MEASURABLE on a fresh run. NONE of these change what is
636	    # discovered/filtered/fetched/selected — they only mirror locals already
637	    # computed inside run_live_retrieval.
638	    #   prefetch_offtopic: the off-topic filter's kept/rejected/threshold. None
639	    #     when the filter is disabled or only seeds are present (seed_only) —
640	    #     honestly absent, never a faked count.
641	    #   drop_reasons: per-reason aggregate of the in-run _trace_drop calls
642	    #     (offtopic / rerank_not_selected / fetch_failed / content_starved). The
643	    #     four statically-known reasons are pre-seeded to 0 so the schema is
644	    #     stable; counts derive locally (not from the pathB trace contextvar,
645	    #     which is empty on a normal sweep).
646	    prefetch_offtopic: dict[str, Any] | None = None
647	    drop_reasons: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
648	    # B4 (b1b10 redesign, I-arch-005 Phase-2/3): relevance-ORDER + fetch-budget
649	    # selection telemetry. Populated ONLY on the B4 ON path
650	    # (PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE=1); None when OFF => byte-identical. §-1.3
651	    # DEMOTE-NOT-DROP (I-deepfix-001 D3): the floor no longer hard-drops below-floor
652	    # candidates — it orders them to the tail and DISCLOSES the demote conversion
653	    # (`demoted_below_floor` / `demoted_fetched_to_fill` / `demoted_tail`). Records
654	    # both the above-floor unfetched tail and the below-floor demoted tail (the fetch
655	    # BUDGET could not afford) so the recall cost is measurable, never dropped-and-
656	    # forgotten. A plain dict (RelevanceGateResult.to_dict()) so the manifest
657	    # write is a no-op getattr with a None default for pre-B4 callers.
658	    relevance_gate: dict[str, Any] | None = None
659	    # I-wire-001 W2 (#1311): content-relevance judge telemetry — DISTINCT from the
660	    # B4 `relevance_gate` above so the two never conflate. Populated ONLY on the
661	    # W2 ON path (PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=1); None when OFF => byte-identical.
662	    # Carries the per-passage DEMOTE dispositions (kept-at-low-weight, never a
663	    # drop list — §-1.3 weight-not-filter).
664	    content_relevance: dict[str, Any] | None = None
665	    #   extraction_finding_rows: the EXTRACTION-stage finding-row count captured
666	    #     at run_live_retrieval RETURN time (== len(evidence_rows) here), frozen
667	    #     as an int. Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1: run_one_query MUTATES
668	    #     retrieval.evidence_rows AFTER this returns (expansion append, deepener/
669	    #     agentic reassign), so reading len(evidence_rows) at manifest-write time
670	    #     reports the POST-expansion total, not the extraction yield. This frozen
671	    #     int is the stable fetched->finding extraction count.
672	    extraction_finding_rows: int = 0
673	    # I-deepfix-001 D5 (#1344): the HONEST machine-readable credibility-tiering batch
674	    # status produced by classify_sources_llm_tiering (a TieringBatchResult.tiering_status:
675	    # {tiering_mode, llm_success_count, rules_floor_count, fallback_count, error_count,
676	    # total}). Plumbed through to the durable manifest disclosure so the diced preflight's
677	    # D5 gate can assert `tiering_mode != 'rules_floor_degraded'` on a FRESH run — i.e. that
678	    # GLM credibility-tiering actually fired and the batch did not silently collapse to the
679	    # deterministic rules-floor. Default {} = LLM-tiering OFF, or no deferred sources to tier
680	    # (the W5 block never ran) => byte-identical OFF path. PURE telemetry: credibility stays a
681	    # WEIGHT (no drop, no abort — §-1.3); the faithfulness engine is untouched.
682	    credibility_tiering_status: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
683	    # I-deepfix-001 (wall/tiering-abort fix, #1344) P1a: fetch-SUBWALL disclosure —
684	    # DISTINCT from `retrieval_wall_hit`. True iff the content-fetch batch was bounded to a
685	    # FRACTION of the remaining wall (PG_RETRIEVAL_FETCH_WALL_FRACTION < 1.0) AND that cutoff
686	    # actually fired (some tasks timed out or were never dispatched before the fetch cutoff).
687	    # Those candidates flow on as ordinary `fetch_failed` and are DISCLOSED here as a wall
688	    # cutoff (§-1.3 disclose-don't-drop), never silently dropped. Kept SEPARATE from
689	    # `retrieval_wall_hit` because the FULL retrieval wall did NOT trip — every planned
690	    # sub-query fired and the classify/W5 loop kept its reserved slice — so flipping
691	    # `retrieval_wall_hit` would emit a misleading "N sub-queries unfired" note. Defaults keep
692	    # existing constructors byte-identical + the OFF path (fraction=1.0 => no subwall =>
693	    # False/0). `*_count` mirror parallel_report.timeout_count / not_dispatched_count.
694	    fetch_subwall_hit: bool = False
695	    fetch_subwall_timeout_count: int = 0
696	    fetch_subwall_not_dispatched_count: int = 0

729	# I-meta-007b (#meta-007): per-tool utilization tracer. Record-only + fail-safe.
730	# The tracer NEVER changes retrieval behavior or return values; any tracer error
731	# (import failure, None tracer, disk error) is swallowed here so a telemetry bug
732	# can never break retrieval. Lazy import keeps the dependency one-directional.
733	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
734	def _trace_tool(
735	    tool_name: str,
736	    target: str = "",
737	    status: str = "ok",
738	    latency_ms: float = 0.0,
739	    bytes_sent: int = 0,
740	    bytes_received: int = 0,
741	    backend_used: str = "",
742	    error: str = "",
743	    **metadata: Any,
744	) -> None:
745	    try:
746	        from src.polaris_graph.telemetry.tool_tracer import (
747	            get_tool_tracer,
748	            tool_tracker_enabled,
749	        )
750	        # I-meta-007b P2b: gate on PG_ENABLE_TOOL_TRACKER (default ON) so a
751	        # direct caller OUTSIDE run_one_query (which never ran the per-query
752	        # reset/bind) cannot append to a stale ON singleton when tracking is
753	        # disabled. When OFF this is a pure no-op.
754	        if not tool_tracker_enabled():
755	            return
756	        get_tool_tracer().record(
757	            tool_name=tool_name,
758	            target=target,
759	            status=status,
760	            latency_ms=latency_ms,
761	            bytes_sent=bytes_sent,
762	            bytes_received=bytes_received,
763	            backend_used=backend_used,
764	            error=error,
765	            **metadata,
766	        )
767	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — telemetry must never break retrieval
768	        pass
769	
770	
771	def _resp_content_len(resp: Any) -> int:
772	    """Best-effort byte length of an httpx response body for telemetry.
773	
774	    Returns 0 when the response object lacks a measurable ``content`` (e.g. a
775	    lightweight test double). NEVER raises — record-only telemetry must not
776	    depend on the response's concrete shape.
777	    """
778	    try:
779	        content = getattr(resp, "content", None)
780	        return len(content) if content else 0
781	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — telemetry must never break retrieval
782	        return 0
783	
784	
785	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
786	# API clients
787	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
788	
789	
790	# FX-17 (#1126): Serper `num` is a PAGE size (provider max ~20); large result sets need the `page`
791	# param (pagination). The old code silently floored `num` to 20 with no warning and never paginated.
792	_SERPER_PAGE_MAX = 20
793	
794	
795	def _serper_fetch_page(
796	    query: str, per_page: int, page: int, headers: dict[str, str]
797	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], bool, float, int, str]:
798	    """FX-17 (#1126): fetch ONE Serper page. Returns (items, ok, latency_ms, resp_bytes, error).
799	    Byte-identical to the legacy single call when page==1 (no `page` key in the payload)."""
800	    payload: dict[str, Any] = {"q": query, "num": per_page}
801	    if page > 1:
802	        payload["page"] = page
803	    _t0 = time.time()
804	    try:
805	        with httpx.Client(timeout=DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT) as c:
806	            r = c.post(SERPER_ENDPOINT, json=payload, headers=headers)
807	        _latency_ms = (time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0
808	        if r.status_code != 200:
809	            return [], False, _latency_ms, _resp_content_len(r), f"HTTP {r.status_code}"
810	        organic = (r.json().get("organic", []) or [])
811	        items = [
812	            {"url": it.get("link", ""), "title": it.get("title", ""),
813	             "snippet": it.get("snippet", ""), "source": "serper"}
814	            for it in organic
815	        ]
816	        return items, True, _latency_ms, _resp_content_len(r), ""
817	    except Exception as exc:
818	        return [], False, (time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0, 0, str(exc)
819	
820	
821	def _serper_search(
822	    query: str, num: int = 10, api_calls: dict[str, int] | None = None
823	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
824	    api_key = os.getenv("SERPER_API_KEY", "").strip()
825	    if not api_key:
826	        logger.warning("[live_retriever] SERPER_API_KEY missing — skipping Serper")
827	        return []
828	    # I-safety-002b (#925) PR-2: record that the Path-B-required backend was actually
829	    # invoked (key present + call attempted). assert_post_run rejects a run where a
830	    # required backend was never tried. Lazy + best-effort; no-op when gate is off.
831	    try:
832	        from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb
833	        _pathb.record_retrieval_attempt("serper")
834	    except Exception:
835	        pass
836	    headers = {"X-API-KEY": api_key, "Content-Type": "application/json"}
837	    per_page = max(1, min(num, _SERPER_PAGE_MAX))
838	    # FX-17 (#1126): make the silent clamp VISIBLE — the inert PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER=100 used to floor
839	    # to 20 with no signal. Surface it loudly so the requested-vs-served gap stops lying.
840	    _clamped = num > _SERPER_PAGE_MAX
841	    if _clamped:
842	        logger.warning(
843	            "[live_retriever] Serper num=%d exceeds the page max %d — clamping per-page to %d and "
844	            "paginating to the PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY budget.", num, _SERPER_PAGE_MAX, per_page,
845	        )
846	    # FX-17 (#1126): total-URL budget across pages. DEFAULT = one page (per_page) -> byte-identical to
847	    # the legacy single call; the benchmark slate raises PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY. Page count is
848	    # bounded by PG_SERPER_MAX_PAGES (small) and early-stops when a page returns < per_page.
849	    try:
850	        _total = max(per_page, int(os.getenv("PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY", str(per_page))))
851	    except ValueError:
852	        _total = per_page
853	    try:
854	        _max_pages = max(1, int(os.getenv("PG_SERPER_MAX_PAGES", "3")))
855	    except ValueError:
856	        _max_pages = 3
857	    _n_pages = min(_max_pages, -(-_total // per_page))  # ceil(total/per_page)
858	
859	    # BB-002 (I-beatboth-fix-000 #1171): a sub-`per_page` page-1 count is NOT a reliable
860	    # end-of-results signal for an OFFSET-paginated SERP API — Serper routinely returns
861	    # 10 organic on page 1 even when page 2 has more, so the legacy `len(items) < per_page`
862	    # break short-circuited before page 2 and the PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY budget was never
863	    # reached (de-facto 10/query ceiling — chokepoint #4). When PG_SERPER_STOP_ON_ZERO_NEW=1
864	    # the loop keeps offset-paging until (a) budget met, (b) a page returns 0 NEW (post-dedup)
865	    # items, or (c) PG_SERPER_MAX_PAGES — the only RELIABLE end-of-results signals. DEFAULT OFF
866	    # = byte-identical (the legacy short-page break is preserved). Discovery-breadth only; every
867	    # new URL flows through the same fetch -> strict_verify -> 4-role chokepoint unchanged.
868	    _stop_on_zero_new = os.getenv("PG_SERPER_STOP_ON_ZERO_NEW", "0").strip() in (
869	        "1", "true", "True",
870	    )
871	
872	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
873	    seen: set[str] = set()
874	    _pages_fetched = 0
875	    _last_latency = 0.0
876	    _last_bytes = 0
877	    _last_err = ""
878	    for _page in range(1, _n_pages + 1):
879	        items, ok, _last_latency, _last_bytes, _last_err = _serper_fetch_page(
880	            query, per_page, _page, headers
881	        )
882	        # FX-17 (#1126) iter-2: count EACH HTTP page request (a real Serper API call), not once
883	        # per query. P2 fix — api_calls['serper'] used to undercount paginated breadth.
884	        if api_calls is not None:
885	            api_calls["serper"] = api_calls.get("serper", 0) + 1
886	        if not ok:
887	            _trace_tool(
888	                "serper", target=query, status="fail", latency_ms=_last_latency,
889	                bytes_sent=len(query), bytes_received=_last_bytes,
890	                backend_used="serper_api_v1", error=_last_err, page=_page,
891	            )
892	            logger.warning("[live_retriever] Serper page %d failed for %r: %s",
893	                           _page, query[:60], _last_err)
894	            break  # fail-open: keep pages already accumulated; do not discard.
895	        _pages_fetched += 1
896	        _new = 0
897	        for it in items:
898	            u = it.get("url", "")
899	            if u and u not in seen:
900	                seen.add(u)
901	                out.append(it)
902	                _new += 1
903	        if len(out) >= _total:
904	            break  # budget met.
905	        if _stop_on_zero_new:
906	            # BB-002 (#1171): keep paging past a short page; stop only when a page
907	            # added 0 NEW (post-dedup) URLs — the reliable end-of-results signal.
908	            if _new == 0:
909	                break
910	        elif len(items) < per_page:
911	            break  # legacy (default OFF): short page -> assume no more results.
912	    _trace_tool(
913	        "serper", target=query, status="ok" if out or not _last_err else "fail",
914	        latency_ms=_last_latency, bytes_sent=len(query), bytes_received=_last_bytes,
915	        backend_used="serper_api_v1", result_count=len(out),
916	        pages_fetched=_pages_fetched, num_requested=num, per_page=per_page,
917	        page_max=_SERPER_PAGE_MAX, clamped=_clamped, total_budget=_total,
918	        stop_on_zero_new=_stop_on_zero_new,   # BB-002 (#1171): which stop policy ran
919	    )
920	    _trace_query("serper", query, [o["url"] for o in out])
921	    return out
922	
923	
924	def _s2_bulk_search(query: str, limit: int = 20) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
925	    api_key = os.getenv("SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY", "").strip()
926	    # I-safety-002b (#925) PR-2: record S2 backend attempt (lazy, best-effort).
927	    try:
928	        from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb
929	        _pathb.record_retrieval_attempt("semantic_scholar")
930	    except Exception:
931	        pass
932	    headers = {}
933	    if api_key:
934	        headers["x-api-key"] = api_key
935	    params = {
936	        "query": query,
937	        "fields": "title,abstract,url,openAccessPdf,externalIds,year,venue",
938	        "limit": max(1, min(limit, 100)),
939	    }
940	    # I-meta-007b: wall-clock for the tool tracer (record-only).
941	    _t0 = time.time()
942	    try:
943	        with httpx.Client(timeout=DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT) as c:
944	            r = c.get(S2_BULK_ENDPOINT, params=params, headers=headers)
945	        _latency_ms = (time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0
946	        if r.status_code != 200:
947	            _trace_tool(
948	                "s2", target=query, status="fail", latency_ms=_latency_ms,
949	                bytes_received=_resp_content_len(r),
950	                backend_used="semantic_scholar_api", error=f"HTTP {r.status_code}",
951	            )
952	            logger.warning(
953	                "[live_retriever] S2 returned %s for %r",
954	                r.status_code, query[:60],
955	            )
956	            _trace_query("semantic_scholar", query, [])
957	            return []
958	        data = r.json()
959	    except Exception as exc:
960	        _trace_tool(
961	            "s2", target=query, status="fail",
962	            latency_ms=(time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0,
963	            backend_used="semantic_scholar_api", error=str(exc),
964	        )
965	        logger.warning("[live_retriever] S2 exception: %s", exc)
966	        _trace_query("semantic_scholar", query, [])
967	        return []
968	    papers = data.get("data", []) or []
969	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
970	    for p in papers:
971	        oa_pdf = (p.get("openAccessPdf") or {}).get("url", "")
972	        ext_ids = p.get("externalIds") or {}
973	        doi = ext_ids.get("DOI", "")
974	        # Full-scale fix (cycle 11): prefer open-access PDF, fall back
975	        # to DOI-resolved URL. Never return a bare semanticscholar.org
976	        # landing page — those are T7 abstract-only stubs that
977	        # AccessBypass deliberately skips and that inflate the T7
978	        # fraction in corpus adequacy. If neither oa_pdf nor DOI is
979	        # available, skip the paper entirely.
980	        if oa_pdf:
981	            url = oa_pdf
982	        elif doi:
983	            url = f"https://doi.org/{doi}"
984	        else:
985	            # Bare S2 landing page would be returned here; skip.
986	            continue
987	        abstract = p.get("abstract") or ""
988	        out.append({
989	            "url": url,
990	            "title": p.get("title", "") or "",
991	            "snippet": (abstract[:500] if abstract else "")[:500],
992	            "source": "s2",
993	            "s2_paper_id": p.get("paperId", ""),
994	            "doi": doi,
995	            "year": p.get("year"),
996	            "venue": p.get("venue"),
997	        })
998	    # BB-004 (I-beatboth-fix-000 #1171): a HTTP-200 that yields ZERO usable papers
999	    # (all lacked oa_pdf + DOI, or `data` was empty) is a DEAD-BACKEND signal — the
1000	    # legacy unconditional status="ok" reported it as success_rate=1.0 and masked the

content.text: This file is dense but names look mostly professional (technical/verbose, code-name-ish like "F14", "F30" as comment tags, not actual identifiers). Let me continue reading.

content.content:
1001	    # collapse (15 S2 calls -> 0 results on drb_90 still showed success). LAW II
1002	    # silent-downgrade fix: trace a DISTINCT "ok_zero" status + zero_yield=True so the
1003	    # discovery_funnel / run-health surfaces it LOUDLY instead of as a clean success.
1004	    # Telemetry/loudness ONLY — no evidence or verification path is touched; the
1005	    # returned list is unchanged (empty), so downstream control flow is identical.
1006	    _s2_zero_yield = len(out) == 0
1007	    _trace_tool(
1008	        "s2", target=query,
1009	        status="ok_zero" if _s2_zero_yield else "ok",
1010	        latency_ms=_latency_ms,
1011	        bytes_received=_resp_content_len(r),
1012	        backend_used="semantic_scholar_api", result_count=len(out),
1013	        zero_yield=_s2_zero_yield,
1014	    )
1015	    _trace_query("semantic_scholar", query, [o["url"] for o in out])
1016	    return out
1017	
1018	
1019	_DOI_FROM_URL_RE = re.compile(
1020	    r"(10\.\d{4,9}/[^\s?#]+?)(?:[?#]|/full|/abstract|/pdf|/meta|\.html|$)",
1021	    re.IGNORECASE,
1022	)
1023	
1024	
1025	# BUG-M-17 (Codex full-scale pass 2): bounded body-text inspection
1026	# for article-type signals. Reads high-signal regions of fetched
1027	# content (meta tags, first 4KB, abstract/methods lead) for SR/MA,
1028	# case-report, perspective, guidance markers. Used by the classifier
1029	# as a SECONDARY signal when the title is truncated or non-diagnostic.
1030	#
1031	# NOT a full-body scan — Codex pass 2 explicitly warned:
1032	# "do not scan the entire body naively for generic terms. Add a
1033	#  bounded secondary narrative/SR signal extractor that inspects
1034	#  high-signal fetched regions only."
1035	
1036	# BUG-M-17b (Codex pass 3 BLOCKED fix): tightened to require context,
1037	# not lone keywords. Explicit publisher metadata and section headers
1038	# are trusted; lone body keywords are REJECTED because primary papers
1039	# routinely cite prior systematic reviews, meta-analyses, case series,
1040	# and guidelines in their background/methods without themselves being
1041	# that article type.
1042	
1043	# High-precision metadata patterns — trust these alone.
1044	# Each entry: (regex_pattern, attribute_order_flexible)
1045	_BODY_META_ARTICLE_TYPE_TAGS: tuple[str, ...] = (
1046	    # HTML meta citation_article_type (both attr orders)
1047	    r'<meta[^>]+citation_article_type[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\']',
1048	    r'<meta[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\'][^>]+citation_article_type',
1049	    r'<meta[^>]+article:section[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\']',
1050	    r'<meta[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\'][^>]+article:section',
1051	    r'<meta[^>]+prism\.section[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\']',
1052	    r'<meta[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\'][^>]+prism\.section',
1053	    # JSON-LD
1054	    r'"articleType"\s*:\s*"([^"]+)"',
1055	)
1056	
1057	# Publisher-embedded article-type HEADERS (top-of-page text).
1058	# Frontiers "SYSTEMATIC REVIEW article", Nature "Article type: X" etc.
1059	_BODY_PUBLISHER_HEADERS: tuple[str, ...] = (
1060	    # Frontiers-style: "SYSTEMATIC REVIEW article" (all caps, with space + "article")
1061	    r'\b(SYSTEMATIC REVIEW|META[- ]ANALYSIS|NETWORK META[- ]ANALYSIS|'
1062	    r'CASE REPORT|CASE SERIES|PERSPECTIVE|EDITORIAL|'
1063	    r'GUIDELINE|COMMENTARY|LETTER|BRIEF REPORT|OPINION) article\b',
1064	    # Nature family: "Article type: <Type>"
1065	    r'Article type:?\s*(Systematic Review|Meta-Analysis|Meta[- ]Analysis|'
1066	    r'Network Meta[- ]Analysis|Case Report|Case Series|Perspective|'
1067	    r'Editorial|Commentary|Letter|Brief Report|Opinion|Guideline)',
1068	)
1069	
1070	# Strong contextual patterns that need to co-occur to call SR_MA from body.
1071	# NEVER fire on a lone keyword — require the declared-intent-or-method shape.
1072	_BODY_SR_MA_CONTEXT_PATTERNS: tuple[str, ...] = (
1073	    # "objective: to conduct a systematic review / meta-analysis"
1074	    r'objective[s]?:\s*to\s+(conduct|perform|undertake)\s+(a\s+)?(systematic review|meta[- ]analysis)',
1075	    # "we conducted a systematic review"
1076	    r'we\s+(conducted|performed|undertook)\s+(a\s+)?(systematic review|meta[- ]analysis)',
1077	    # M-17c (Codex pass 4): "this SR/MA" must be followed by a
1078	    # self-descriptive predicate within a short window. Rejects
1079	    # citation-byline shapes like "this meta-analysis by Smith et al.
1080	    # shaped the endpoint hierarchy" where SR/MA is the cited work,
1081	    # not the fetched paper.
1082	    r'this\s+(systematic review(?:\s+and\s+meta[- ]analysis)?|meta[- ]analysis)'
1083	    r'\s+(aims?|evaluates?|examines?|investigates?|assesses?|reviews?|'
1084	    r'reports?|presents?|summarizes?|synthesizes?|demonstrates?|'
1085	    r'pools?|analyses|analyzes|combines?|explores?|'
1086	    r'was\s+(conducted|performed|undertaken|registered|designed))\b',
1087	    # M-17c: "PRISMA" with contextual search/selection/extraction/flow diagram
1088	    r'PRISMA.{0,40}(search|selection|extraction|flow diagram)',
1089	    r'(search|selection|extraction|flow diagram).{0,40}PRISMA',
1090	    # M-17c (Codex pass 4): Cochrane tightened. Previously
1091	    # "cochrane (systematic )?review" fired on "A Cochrane review
1092	    # found..." citation in a primary trial. Now requires declarative
1093	    # "this Cochrane review" + self-descriptive verb, OR registration
1094	    # metadata (Cochrane Library CD-number).
1095	    r'this\s+cochrane\s+(systematic\s+)?review\s+'
1096	    r'(aims?|evaluates?|examines?|investigates?|assesses?|reports?|'
1097	    r'presents?|summarizes?|was\s+(conducted|performed|registered))\b',
1098	    # Cochrane Library registration ID (unique to Cochrane SRs)
1099	    r'cochrane\s+(database|library).*CD\d{6}',
1100	    # Conclusive meta-analytic methods signature: "pooled estimate" + "random-effects"
1101	    r'(pooled (estimate|effect|odds ratio|risk ratio|hazard ratio)).{0,200}(random[- ]effects|fixed[- ]effects)',
1102	    r'(random[- ]effects|fixed[- ]effects).{0,200}(pooled (estimate|effect|odds ratio|risk ratio|hazard ratio))',
1103	)
1104	
1105	# Case report body patterns — require declarative "we report/present"
1106	# OR the X-year-old patient opener together with PATIENT-centered framing.
1107	_BODY_CASE_REPORT_CONTEXT_PATTERNS: tuple[str, ...] = (
1108	    # "we report/describe/present a/the case"  (declarative)
1109	    r'we\s+(report|describe|present)\s+(a|the)\s+case\b',
1110	    # "here we report/describe a case"
1111	    r'here\s+we\s+(report|describe|present)\s+(a|the)\s+case\b',
1112	    # "we report a X-year-old patient" (opener)
1113	    r'we\s+report\s+(a|an)\s+\d+[- ]year[- ]old',
1114	    # Patient opener at very beginning of abstract (first 300 chars)
1115	    # handled separately below — not in this tuple.
1116	)
1117	
1118	# Guideline body patterns — declarative intent.
1119	# M-17c (Codex pass 4): tightened. Previously "this (clinical
1120	# practice) guideline" and "consensus statement from" fired on
1121	# citation-byline references like "according to a consensus
1122	# statement from the Endocrine Society" in a primary trial.
1123	# Now requires the fetched article to EITHER say "this clinical
1124	# practice guideline" (fully qualified) OR be followed by a
1125	# self-descriptive verb ("provides", "recommends", "was developed",
1126	# "we developed").
1127	# M-17d (Codex pass 5): the unanchored "(clinical practice )?
1128	# guideline <verb>" still fired on dated external citations like
1129	# "The 2025 clinical practice guideline recommends...". Now requires
1130	# explicit "this" self-reference in front of the guideline noun.
1131	# M-17e (Codex pass 6): "this guideline" alone is insufficient when
1132	# the lead has already cited an external guideline (anaphoric
1133	# reference). The detector now additionally rejects matches preceded
1134	# by anaphoric-citation markers. Ambiguous verbs "summarizes" and
1135	# "describes" have been removed because they apply equally to cited
1136	# external guidelines. The bare "this clinical practice guideline"
1137	# phrase must appear at sentence start (not as object of "followed").
1138	# "updates?" added to close a recall miss.
1139	_BODY_GUIDELINE_CONTEXT_PATTERNS: tuple[str, ...] = (
1140	    # "This clinical practice guideline" at sentence start — must be
1141	    # subject, not object of "followed this guideline during trial"
1142	    r'(?:^|[.!?]\s+|\n\s*)this\s+clinical\s+practice\s+guideline\b',
1143	    # "This (clinical practice) guideline ... <verb>" — must be "this"
1144	    # to reject dated/external citations (M-17d). Verb list tightened
1145	    # to unambiguous self-authorship verbs (M-17e).
1146	    r'this\s+(clinical\s+practice\s+)?guideline\s+'
1147	    r'(provides|recommends|was\s+developed|is\s+intended|'
1148	    r'presents|outlines|aims?|establishes|offers|updates?)',
1149	    # "this consensus statement ... verb" (declarative, not cited)
1150	    r'this\s+consensus\s+statement\s+'
1151	    r'(provides|recommends|was\s+developed|outlines|presents|'
1152	    r'aims?|establishes|summarizes)',
1153	    # "Consensus statement from X: <descriptive phrase>" — structural
1154	    # form of a consensus paper, NOT "according to a consensus
1155	    # statement from". Require the consensus statement to be the
1156	    # subject of a descriptive predicate.
1157	    r'consensus\s+statement\s+from\s+(the\s+)?[A-Z][A-Za-z ]+\s+'
1158	    r'(provides|recommends|was\s+developed|outlines|presents|aims?)',
1159	    # "Expert consensus panel/group was convened/developed..."
1160	    r'expert\s+consensus\s+(panel|group)\s+'
1161	    r'(was\s+convened|developed|provides|recommends|presents)',
1162	    # Declarative: "we developed this clinical/practical guidance"
1163	    r'we\s+(developed|provide)\s+(this\s+)?(clinical|practical)\s+guidance',
1164	)
1165	
1166	# Perspective/commentary body patterns — declarative framing, not
1167	# audience phrases alone.
1168	_BODY_PERSPECTIVE_CONTEXT_PATTERNS: tuple[str, ...] = (
1169	    # "In this perspective / commentary / editorial"
1170	    r'in\s+this\s+(perspective|commentary|editorial|opinion)',
1171	    # "This perspective examines / reviews"
1172	    r'this\s+(perspective|commentary|editorial)\s+(examines|reviews|discusses|considers)',
1173	    # "offer a perspective on"
1174	    r'(offer|present|provide)\s+a\s+perspective\s+(on|for|of)',
1175	)

1178	def _classify_from_meta_keywords(captured: str) -> str:
1179	    """Map a captured metadata/header value to a tier signal. Used
1180	    for publisher-embedded explicit article-type tags (high precision).
1181	    """
1182	    c = (captured or "").lower().strip()
1183	    if not c:
1184	        return ""
1185	    if any(k in c for k in ("systematic review", "meta-analysis",
1186	                            "meta analysis", "cochrane",
1187	                            "network meta-analysis",
1188	                            "network meta analysis")):
1189	        return "SR_MA"
1190	    if "case report" in c or "case series" in c:
1191	        return "CASE_REPORT"
1192	    if "perspective" in c:
1193	        return "PERSPECTIVE"
1194	    if "guideline" in c or "consensus" in c:
1195	        return "GUIDELINE"
1196	    if "editorial" in c or "commentary" in c:
1197	        return "PERSPECTIVE"
1198	    if "letter" in c or "opinion" in c or "brief report" in c:
1199	        return "PERSPECTIVE"
1200	    return ""

1203	def _detect_article_type_from_body(raw_content: str) -> str:
1204	    """Bounded body-text inspection for article-type signal.
1205	
1206	    Returns one of: "SR_MA", "CASE_REPORT", "PERSPECTIVE", "GUIDELINE",
1207	    or "" (no signal). Inspects only the first 8KB of content to keep
1208	    cost bounded.
1209	
1210	    M-17b (Codex pass 3 BLOCKED): tightened to avoid false positives
1211	    from primary papers citing prior systematic reviews / case reports
1212	    as background. Now requires either:
1213	
1214	    (P1) explicit publisher-embedded article-type metadata
1215	         (HTML meta tag, JSON-LD articleType, Nature-style "Article
1216	         type:" header, Frontiers-style "SYSTEMATIC REVIEW article"
1217	         section marker), OR
1218	    (P2) a declarative contextual body pattern that indicates the
1219	         fetched article IS the given type (not merely citing it).
1220	
1221	    Lone keywords like "systematic review" or "case report" alone
1222	    are NOT sufficient — primary trial abstracts commonly cite prior
1223	    reviews in their background, and primary-study methods often
1224	    mention excluding case series.
1225	    """
1226	    if not raw_content:
1227	        return ""
1228	    # Bound the scan window
1229	    head = raw_content[:8000]
1230	    lead = head[:4000]
1231	    lead_lower = lead.lower()
1232	
1233	    # ── Priority 1a: explicit meta / JSON-LD article-type tags
1234	    for pattern in _BODY_META_ARTICLE_TYPE_TAGS:
1235	        m = re.search(pattern, head, re.IGNORECASE)
1236	        if m and m.lastindex:
1237	            signal = _classify_from_meta_keywords(m.group(1))
1238	            if signal:
1239	                return signal
1240	
1241	    # ── Priority 1b: publisher-embedded article-type headers
1242	    # These are ALL-CAPS or structured banners at the page top
1243	    # (Frontiers "SYSTEMATIC REVIEW article", Nature "Article type: X")
1244	    for pattern in _BODY_PUBLISHER_HEADERS:
1245	        m = re.search(pattern, head, re.IGNORECASE)
1246	        if m and m.lastindex:
1247	            signal = _classify_from_meta_keywords(m.group(1))
1248	            if signal:
1249	                return signal
1250	
1251	    # ── Priority 2: declarative body patterns (must co-occur with
1252	    # study-type context, not lone keywords)
1253	    for pattern in _BODY_SR_MA_CONTEXT_PATTERNS:
1254	        if re.search(pattern, lead_lower, re.IGNORECASE):
1255	            return "SR_MA"
1256	    for pattern in _BODY_CASE_REPORT_CONTEXT_PATTERNS:
1257	        if re.search(pattern, lead_lower, re.IGNORECASE):
1258	            return "CASE_REPORT"
1259	    # "X-year-old patient" opener as first significant content in abstract
1260	    # (look only in first 500 chars to avoid methods/exclusion-criteria false positives)
1261	    opener = lead_lower[:500]
1262	    if re.search(
1263	        r'^\s*(a|this)\s+\d+[- ]year[- ]old\s+(man|woman|male|female|patient)\s+(presented|was\s+admitted|with)',
1264	        opener,
1265	    ):
1266	        return "CASE_REPORT"
1267	    # M-17e (Codex pass 6): anaphoric-citation guard. If the lead
1268	    # contains a citation-style reference to an external guideline
1269	    # ("followed the 2025 ADA guideline", "according to the NICE
1270	    # guideline"), a subsequent "This guideline <verb>" is likely
1271	    # anaphoric to that external citation rather than self-declarative.
1272	    # We therefore require the guideline match to precede, not follow,
1273	    # any such external-guideline citation in the lead.
1274	    _ANAPHORIC_GUIDELINE_CITATION = re.compile(
1275	        r'(?:followed|following|according\s+to|citing|cited|per|'
1276	        r'as\s+recommended\s+by|as\s+per|based\s+on|in\s+line\s+with)'
1277	        r'\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:\d{4}\s+)?(?:[a-z]+\s+)?(?:clinical\s+practice\s+)?'
1278	        r'guidelines?\b',
1279	        re.IGNORECASE,
1280	    )
1281	    for pattern in _BODY_GUIDELINE_CONTEXT_PATTERNS:
1282	        m = re.search(pattern, lead_lower, re.IGNORECASE)
1283	        if m:
1284	            # Reject if an external-guideline citation appears in the
1285	            # preceding 300 chars (anaphoric reference) — e.g. "followed
1286	            # the 2025 ADA guideline ... This guideline summarizes".
1287	            preceding = lead_lower[max(0, m.start() - 300): m.start()]
1288	            if _ANAPHORIC_GUIDELINE_CITATION.search(preceding):
1289	                continue
1290	            return "GUIDELINE"
1291	    for pattern in _BODY_PERSPECTIVE_CONTEXT_PATTERNS:
1292	        if re.search(pattern, lead_lower, re.IGNORECASE):
1293	            return "PERSPECTIVE"
1294	
1295	    return ""

1298	def _extract_title_from_content(content: str) -> str:
1299	    """Extract the full paper title from fetched page content.
1300	
1301	    M-13 fallback (Codex pass 13): OpenAlex DOI lookup doesn't work
1302	    for MDPI URLs (they don't embed DOI in URL path). And OpenAlex
1303	    title-search with a truncated Serper snippet often misses the
1304	    right paper. As a third recovery path, parse the fetched HTML
1305	    or markdown for the real title.
1306	
1307	    - Jina Reader and Crawl4AI often emit markdown with `Title: ...`
1308	      or `# Title` on the first line.
1309	    - Direct HTTP returns HTML; look for `<title>...</title>` tag.
1310	    - trafilatura output is plain text; the first significant line
1311	      is usually the title.
1312	
1313	    Returns empty string if no plausible title found.
1314	    """
1315	    if not content:
1316	        return ""
1317	    # Jina/Crawl4AI "Title: X" pattern
1318	    m = re.search(r"^\s*Title:\s*(.+?)\s*$", content[:2000], re.MULTILINE)
1319	    if m:
1320	        t = m.group(1).strip()
1321	        if 10 <= len(t) <= 500:
1322	            return t
1323	    # Markdown H1
1324	    m = re.search(r"^\s*#\s+(.+?)\s*$", content[:2000], re.MULTILINE)
1325	    if m:
1326	        t = m.group(1).strip()
1327	        if 10 <= len(t) <= 500 and "content" not in t.lower()[:30]:
1328	            return t
1329	    # HTML <title> tag
1330	    m = re.search(r"<title[^>]*>(.+?)</title>", content[:4000],
1331	                  re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
1332	    if m:
1333	        t = m.group(1).strip()
1334	        # Strip journal suffixes like " — Frontiers", " | MDPI"
1335	        t = re.sub(r"\s*[|\-—–]\s*(mdpi|frontiers|nejm|jama|lancet|"
1336	                   r"bmc|springer|nature|science|cell|plos).*$", "",
1337	                   t, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
1338	        if 10 <= len(t) <= 500:
1339	            return t
1340	    return ""

1343	def _extract_doi_from_url(url: str) -> str:
1344	    """Extract a DOI from a URL if present. Handles Frontiers
1345	    (`/10.3389/fphar.2022.1016639/full`), JAMA, NEJM, OUP, Sage,
1346	    ACS, RSC, Wiley, and direct `doi.org/...` URLs. MDPI URLs don't
1347	    embed DOIs; return empty there.
1348	    """
1349	    if not url:
1350	        return ""
1351	    # Direct DOI URL
1352	    u = url.strip()
1353	    if "doi.org/" in u.lower():
1354	        idx = u.lower().find("doi.org/") + len("doi.org/")
1355	        doi = u[idx:].split("?")[0].split("#")[0].rstrip("/")
1356	        return doi
1357	    # Embedded DOI in publisher URLs
1358	    m = _DOI_FROM_URL_RE.search(u)
1359	    if m:
1360	        return m.group(1).rstrip("/").rstrip(".")
1361	    return ""
