1364	def _candidate_oa_hints(metadata: Any) -> tuple[str, str]:
1365	    """I-meta-007c: pull (doi, pmid) hints from a candidate's metadata dict.
1366	
1367	    S2 candidates carry ``metadata["doi"]`` (line ~2001); PMIDs are not in the
1368	    S2 dict today but the slot is honoured for forward-compat / testability.
1369	    Returns ("", "") for non-dict / missing metadata. NEVER raises.
1370	
1371	    Diff-gate P2a: the whole body is wrapped in a fail-open try/except returning
1372	    ("", "") on ANY error, matching the resolver helpers' fail-open contract —
1373	    a malformed metadata object must never break the retrieval loop."""
1374	    try:
1375	        if not isinstance(metadata, dict):
1376	            return "", ""
1377	        doi = str(metadata.get("doi") or "").strip()
1378	        pmid = str(metadata.get("pmid") or "").strip()
1379	        return doi, pmid
1380	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-OPEN, never break retrieval.
1381	        return "", ""

1384	def _authority_cache_init() -> None:
1385	    """Create the authority-enrich cache with a VERSIONED migration (C5).
1386	
1387	    Not a CREATE-IF-NOT-EXISTS no-op: a stale cache whose recorded schema
1388	    version is older than AUTHORITY_CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION is rebuilt rather than
1389	    silently used with missing columns.
1390	    """
1391	    AUTHORITY_CACHE_DB.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
1392	    conn = sqlite3.connect(AUTHORITY_CACHE_DB)
1393	    try:
1394	        conn.execute(
1395	            "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_meta ("
1396	            " id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1), version INTEGER NOT NULL)"
1397	        )
1398	        row = conn.execute("SELECT version FROM schema_meta WHERE id = 1").fetchone()
1399	        existing = row[0] if row else 0
1400	        table_present = conn.execute(
1401	            "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='authority_enrich'"
1402	        ).fetchone() is not None
1403	        # Migrate whenever the recorded version is older than the current one
1404	        # (existing=0 means an unversioned/legacy cache). Drop + rebuild the
1405	        # payload table (the cache is a rebuildable accelerator, never the
1406	        # source of truth) — NOT a CREATE-IF-NOT-EXISTS no-op (C5).
1407	        if table_present and existing < AUTHORITY_CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION:
1408	            conn.execute("DROP TABLE IF EXISTS authority_enrich")
1409	            logger.warning(
1410	                "[live_retriever] authority cache schema %d < %d — migrating "
1411	                "(rebuild)", existing, AUTHORITY_CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION,
1412	            )
1413	        conn.execute(
1414	            "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS authority_enrich ("
1415	            " key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,"
1416	            " payload_json TEXT NOT NULL,"
1417	            " fetched_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)"
1418	        )
1419	        conn.execute(
1420	            "INSERT INTO schema_meta (id, version) VALUES (1, ?)"
1421	            " ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET version = excluded.version",
1422	            (AUTHORITY_CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION,),
1423	        )
1424	        conn.commit()
1425	    finally:
1426	        conn.close()
1427	
1428	
1429	# I-ready-017 #1134 (Codex diff-gate P1): run the versioned migration once before
1430	# the FIRST cache access (read OR write), so a stale v1 payload — which predates
1431	# the journal_only is_retracted/openalex_venue fields — is rebuilt away on the
1432	# READ path too, never served unchanged. Previously the migration ran only on
1433	# _authority_cache_put, so a get-before-put returned a stale payload and a cached
1434	# retracted journal article could pass the journal_only predicate.
1435	_authority_cache_migrated = False
1436	
1437	
1438	def _ensure_authority_cache_migrated() -> None:
1439	    global _authority_cache_migrated
1440	    if not _authority_cache_migrated:
1441	        _authority_cache_init()
1442	        _authority_cache_migrated = True
1443	
1444	
1445	def _authority_cache_get(key: str) -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
1446	    _ensure_authority_cache_migrated()
1447	    if not AUTHORITY_CACHE_DB.exists():
1448	        return None
1449	    conn = sqlite3.connect(AUTHORITY_CACHE_DB)
1450	    try:
1451	        row = conn.execute(
1452	            "SELECT payload_json FROM authority_enrich WHERE key = ?", (key,),
1453	        ).fetchone()
1454	    except sqlite3.OperationalError:
1455	        return None
1456	    finally:
1457	        conn.close()
1458	    if not row:
1459	        return None
1460	    import json
1461	    try:
1462	        return json.loads(row[0])
1463	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1464	        return None
1465	
1466	
1467	def _authority_cache_put(key: str, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
1468	    import json
1469	    _ensure_authority_cache_migrated()
1470	    conn = sqlite3.connect(AUTHORITY_CACHE_DB)
1471	    try:
1472	        conn.execute(
1473	            "INSERT INTO authority_enrich (key, payload_json) VALUES (?, ?)"
1474	            " ON CONFLICT(key) DO UPDATE SET payload_json = excluded.payload_json,"
1475	            " fetched_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP",
1476	            (key, json.dumps(payload)),
1477	        )
1478	        conn.commit()
1479	    finally:
1480	        conn.close()

1484	    client: httpx.Client, source_id: str

1486	    """Separate /sources/{id} fetch for venue-level authority fields (C5).
1487

1490	    """
1491	    if not source_id:

1502	    src = resp.json()

1504	        return {}
1505	    return {
1506	        "is_core": src.get("is_core"),
1507	        "is_in_doaj": src.get("is_in_doaj"),

1513	def _build_authority_signals_dict(
1514	    work: dict[str, Any], source_fields: dict[str, Any]
1515	) -> dict[str, Any]:
1516	    """Build the additive AuthoritySignals payload (C1/C5) as a plain dict.
1517	
1518	    Stored as a dict so the live-path enrich return value stays JSON-cacheable;
1519	    live_retriever reconstructs the AuthoritySignals dataclass at classify time.
1520	    Missing fields stay absent/None -> the authority model returns LOW
1521	    confidence (fail-honest, never fabricate).
1522	    """
1523	    primary = work.get("primary_location") or {}
1524	    source = primary.get("source") or {}
1525	    # First resolved institution (ROR) from authorships.
1526	    ror_id = ""
1527	    inst_type = ""
1528	    country_code = ""
1529	    for authorship in work.get("authorships") or []:
1530	        for inst in authorship.get("institutions") or []:
1531	            if inst.get("ror"):
1532	                ror_id = inst.get("ror") or ""
1533	                inst_type = inst.get("type") or ""
1534	                country_code = inst.get("country_code") or ""
1535	                break
1536	        if ror_id:
1537	            break
1538	
1539	    stats = source_fields.get("summary_stats") or {}
1540	    venue_summary_stats: dict[str, Any] = {}
1541	    if isinstance(stats, dict):
1542	        if "h_index" in stats:
1543	            venue_summary_stats["h_index"] = stats.get("h_index")
1544	        if "2yr_mean_citedness" in stats:
1545	            venue_summary_stats["2yr_mean_citedness"] = stats.get("2yr_mean_citedness")
1546	
1547	    return {
1548	        "cited_by_count": work.get("cited_by_count"),
1549	        "source_id": source.get("id", "") or "",
1550	        "venue_summary_stats": venue_summary_stats or None,
1551	        "is_core": source_fields.get("is_core"),
1552	        "is_in_doaj": source_fields.get("is_in_doaj"),
1553	        "apc_prices": source_fields.get("apc_prices"),
1554	        "publication_year": work.get("publication_year"),
1555	        # I-deepfix-001 Codex wave-2 P1: full publication_date (YYYY-MM-DD) so the
1556	        # selector can enforce a MONTH-precision date ceiling ("before June 2023").
1557	        "publication_date": work.get("publication_date"),
1558	        "ror_id": ror_id,
1559	        "institution_type": inst_type,
1560	        "country_code": country_code,
1561	    }

1564	# ── I-deepfix-001 Wave-4 CONTAMINATION (#1344) ─────────────────────────────────────────
1565	# The drb_72 audit found a SCOPE/DATE contamination: an OpenAlex title-SEARCH lookup returned a
1566	# DIFFERENT paper (a 2019 Dwivedi record) whose title/year/authors were then attached to OUR
1567	# source, fooling the publication-date/window screen. Two additive, faithfulness-NEUTRAL,
1568	# default-OFF levers below:
1569	#   (A) PG_OPENALEX_MATCH_VALIDATE — validate the title-SEARCH work actually matches our source
1570	#       (DOI agreement OR title-token overlap) BEFORE attaching its metadata. On mismatch WITHHOLD
1571	#       the OpenAlex enrichment (return {}) — the source KEEPS its own weight (§-1.3 demote the bad
1572	#       metadata's trust, NEVER drop the source). The exact /works/doi path stays TRUSTED.
1573	#   (C) PG_RESOLVE_PUBDATE_FROM_HTML — see the wiring at the row-build seam in run_live_retrieval.
1574	# Both flags default OFF and are byte-identical when OFF. Both emit an HONEST realized-effect
1575	# ``[activation]`` marker (checked/rejected, resolved/unresolved) the run_gate_b canary reads; a
1576	# distinct ``unavailable_failopen`` degrade marker (canary-rejected) fires only on a fault path.
1577	_ON_TOKENS = ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
1578	
1579	# Deliberately-generic title tokens dropped before overlap scoring so a shared "review of the ..."
1580	# boilerplate cannot inflate the match (a wrong paper must not pass on stopwords alone).
1581	_TITLE_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
1582	    "the", "and", "for", "with", "from", "into", "study", "review", "analysis",
1583	    "using", "based", "toward", "towards", "case", "report", "paper", "article",
1584	    "meta", "systematic", "randomized", "randomised", "trial", "effect", "effects",
1585	    "impact", "role", "among", "between", "versus", "over", "under", "about",
1586	})
1587	
1588	# Module-level THREAD-SAFE match-validate telemetry — `_openalex_enrich` runs both serially in the
1589	# post-fetch loop AND in the bounded-parallel enrich pre-batch (worker threads), so the counters are
1590	# guarded by a Lock. run_live_retrieval SNAPSHOTS this at the top of the enrich window and computes
1591	# the per-run delta after the loop (re-entrancy-tolerant: honest totals, no reset race). Mirrors the
1592	# established module-telemetry pattern (e.g. provenance reanchor telemetry).
1593	_MATCH_VALIDATE_TELEMETRY: dict[str, int] = {"checked": 0, "rejected": 0, "failopen": 0}
1594	_MATCH_VALIDATE_LOCK = threading.Lock()
1595	
1596	
1597	def _openalex_match_validate_enabled() -> bool:
1598	    """PG_OPENALEX_MATCH_VALIDATE opt-in kill-switch (default OFF; read at CALL time, LAW VI).
1599	    OFF => the title-search match is trusted exactly as before (byte-identical)."""
1600	    return os.getenv("PG_OPENALEX_MATCH_VALIDATE", "0").strip().lower() in _ON_TOKENS

1606	    return os.getenv("PG_RESOLVE_PUBDATE_FROM_HTML", "0").strip().lower() in _ON_TOKENS

1610	    """Phase C: tracks PG_GATE (default OFF; read at CALL time, LAW VI). OFF => the
1611	    additive venue / is_peer_reviewed row keys are NEVER written and the evidence
1612	    row is byte-identical to champion. These keys feed ONLY the deterministic
1613	    quality-eligibility scorer at the citable-menu seam (upstream of strict_verify)."""
1614	    try:
1615	        from src.polaris_graph.planning.gate_flags import gate_enabled  # noqa: PLC0415
1616	        return bool(gate_enabled())

1622	    """Thread-safe increment of one match-validate counter (checked / rejected / failopen)."""

1633	def _title_overlap_tokens(title: str) -> set[str]:
1634	    """Lowercased content tokens (>=3 chars, non-stopword, alnum) of a title, for overlap scoring."""
1635	    if not title:
1636	        return set()
1637	    toks = re.findall(r"[a-z0-9]+", str(title).lower())
1638	    return {t for t in toks if len(t) >= 3 and t not in _TITLE_STOPWORDS}

1641	def _normalize_doi(raw: "str | None") -> str:
1642	    """Bare lowercase DOI (scheme + doi.org host stripped) for exact DOI-agreement comparison."""
1643	    s = str(raw or "").strip().lower()
1644	    for pfx in ("https://doi.org/", "http://doi.org/", "doi:"):
1645	        if s.startswith(pfx):
1646	            s = s[len(pfx):]
1647	    return s.strip()

1650	def _openalex_search_match_ok(
1651	    *, our_title: str, our_doi: str, work: dict[str, Any], min_overlap: "float | None" = None,
1652	) -> bool:
1653	    """(A) TRUE iff the OpenAlex title-SEARCH ``work`` plausibly matches OUR source.
1654	
1655	    A search query returns the single best hit REGARDLESS of relevance, so a truncated / variant /
1656	    unindexed title can surface a DIFFERENT paper. Accept the match on the FIRST sufficient signal:
1657	      1. DOI agreement — our extracted DOI equals the returned work's DOI (strongest; unambiguous).
1658	      2. Title-token OVERLAP-COEFFICIENT (|A∩B| / min(|A|,|B|), stopwords removed) >= threshold — a
1659	         correct truncated title is a near-subset of the full display_name (coefficient ≈ 1.0), a
1660	         wrong paper shares few content tokens (the 2019 Dwivedi mis-attach class).
1661	    An EXPLICIT DOI CONFLICT (our DOI and the returned work's DOI BOTH present and DIFFERENT) is a HARD
1662	    NEGATIVE that short-circuits to False BEFORE signal 2 — a known-different DOI must never be overridden
1663	    by title overlap, else a DOI-fallback search attaches a different paper's metadata (Wave-4 Issue-2).
1664	    When NEITHER can be established (no DOI agreement AND no comparable title on either side) the
1665	    match is UNVALIDATABLE => return False so the caller WITHHOLDS the metadata (§-1.3 demote its
1666	    trust, keep the source). Pure (no I/O); unit-testable. Threshold is env-overridable (LAW VI)."""
1667	    work_doi = _normalize_doi(work.get("doi"))
1668	    our_doi_n = _normalize_doi(our_doi)
1669	    if our_doi_n and work_doi and our_doi_n == work_doi:
1670	        return True
1671	    # Wave-4 Issue-2 (#1344): an EXPLICIT DOI CONFLICT — both DOIs present and DIFFERENT — is a hard
1672	    # negative for this contamination class. Reject it BEFORE title-overlap validation so a DOI-fallback
1673	    # title search cannot attach a different paper's date/authority metadata on token overlap alone.
1674	    if our_doi_n and work_doi and our_doi_n != work_doi:
1675	        return False
1676	    our_toks = _title_overlap_tokens(our_title)
1677	    work_toks = _title_overlap_tokens(work.get("display_name") or work.get("title") or "")
1678	    if not our_toks or not work_toks:
1679	        # No DOI agreement and no comparable title => cannot confirm the match => withhold.
1680	        return False
1681	    threshold = min_overlap if min_overlap is not None else _env_float(
1682	        "PG_OPENALEX_MATCH_MIN_TITLE_OVERLAP", 0.5
1683	    )
1684	    overlap = len(our_toks & work_toks) / min(len(our_toks), len(work_toks))
1685	    return overlap >= threshold

1688	def _resolve_row_pubdate_backfill(
1689	    *,
1690	    content: "str | None",
1691	    jsonld: "str | None",
1692	    url: "str | None",
1693	    metadata: "dict | None",
1694	    pub_date: "str | None",
1695	    pub_year: "int | None",
1696	) -> "tuple[str | None, int | None, bool, bool]":
1697	    """(C) ACTIVATE the DARK resolver at the row-build seam: fill a MONTH/year date from ALREADY-fetched
1698	    content when the source's OpenAlex date is missing/unreliable.
1699	
1700	    Returns ``(pub_date, pub_year, resolved, failopen)``: the (possibly back-filled) date fields plus two
1701	    flags for the honest activation marker. Only its EXISTING pure function ``resolve_publication_date``
1702	    is called (ACTIVATE-not-rebuild). A month-precision iso ("YYYY-MM") back-fills ``pub_date`` (only when
1703	    it was None); a year is back-filled onto ``pub_year`` when that was None (band-guarded 1900-2100).
1704	    FAIL-OPEN: any resolver fault leaves BOTH date fields UNCHANGED and returns ``failopen=True`` — an
1705	    undated source is NEVER masked (§-1.3), so a resolver bug can never wrongly drop an in-window source.
1706	    Pure w.r.t. its args (the resolver itself does no network); unit-testable with a stubbed resolver."""
1707	    try:
1708	        r_iso, r_year = resolve_publication_date(
1709	            raw_html=content, jsonld=jsonld, url=url, search_metadata=metadata,
1710	        )
1711	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open: a resolver fault must never mask a source
1712	        return (pub_date, pub_year, False, True)
1713	    if r_iso is None:
1714	        return (pub_date, pub_year, False, False)
1715	    if "-" in r_iso and pub_date is None:
1716	        pub_date = r_iso  # month precision -> the field the timeline screen prefers
1717	    if pub_year is None and r_year is not None and 1900 <= r_year <= 2100:
1718	        pub_year = r_year
1719	    return (pub_date, pub_year, True, False)

1726	    title search. When the URL embeds a DOI (Frontiers, JAMA, NEJM,
1727	    OUP, etc.), OpenAlex's /works/doi:<doi> endpoint is exact and

1729	    fails when Serper truncated the title or returned a variant

1743	            # pre-validation) run can cache UNvalidated title-search metadata under schema v4; a later
1744	            # force-ON run must not serve that cache hit blind — doing so emits checked=0/rejected=0 and
1745	            # PASSES the activation canary while a WRONG paper's date/authority metadata stays attached
1746	            # (false-green liveness). When PG_OPENALEX_MATCH_VALIDATE is ON we REVALIDATE the cached
1747	            # payload — PURE, no network, reconstructed from the frozen openalex_full_title + doi — UNLESS
1748	            # the url-embedded DOI AGREES with the cached DOI (the exact-DOI path stays TRUSTED, never
1749	            # validated, mirroring the fetch path). A mismatch WITHHOLDS the enrichment (return {} — the
1750	            # source keeps its own weight downstream, §-1.3 demote-not-drop). A validator FAULT fails OPEN
1751	            # (serve the cache as before) and emits the DISTINCT unavailable_failopen degrade the canary
1752	            # rejects. OFF => this whole block is skipped => the cache read is byte-identical.
1753	            if _openalex_match_validate_enabled():

1929	def _env_float(name: str, default: float) -> float:
1930	    """Positive-*finite*-float env knob with a safe fallback (LAW VI).
1931	
1932	    Non-finite overrides (``inf``/``-inf``/``nan``) fall back to ``default``:
1933	    ``float("inf")`` parses fine and is ``> 0``, but feeding it to e.g.
1934	    ``threading.Thread.join(timeout=...)`` raises ``OverflowError`` on
1935	    Windows (I-bug-116 / #556).
1936	    """
1937	    try:
1938	        value = float(os.getenv(name, str(default)))
1939	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
1940	        return default
1941	    return value if math.isfinite(value) and value > 0 else default
1942

1947	        value = int(os.getenv(name, str(default)))
1948	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
1949	        return default
1950	    return value if value > 0 else default

1953	def _post_fetch_loop_budget(fetch_cap: int) -> float:
1954	    """I-ready-003 (#1074) P1: the post-fetch loop wall-clock budget, SCALED with ``fetch_cap``.
1955	
1956	    The loop classifies all ~``fetch_cap`` candidates serially (each paying an OpenAlex enrich
1957	    round-trip). A FIXED budget that does not scale with ``fetch_cap`` silently truncates the corpus at
1958	    full cap (the I-cap-005 ~40-URL silent-throttle class, one layer deeper). Returns
1959	    ``max(PG_POST_FETCH_LOOP_BUDGET floor, fetch_cap * PG_POST_FETCH_PER_URL_BUDGET)`` — byte-identical
1960	    for small caps (the 900s floor wins), and a conservative operator env can no longer silently win
1961	    because the ``fetch_cap`` term floors it. Pure (env-only); unit-testable."""
1962	    return max(
1963	        _env_float("PG_POST_FETCH_LOOP_BUDGET", 900.0),
1964	        max(0, int(fetch_cap)) * _env_float("PG_POST_FETCH_PER_URL_BUDGET", 4.0),
1965	    )

1968	def _retrieval_wall_seconds() -> float:
1969	    """I-deepfix-001 item 4 (#1344): per-question RETRIEVAL-PHASE wall budget.
1970	
1971	    The proven hang-fixes (#1264/#1338) bound generate+verify, NOT retrieval. A
1972	    slow-fetch tail (the 90s-per-URL fetch-timeout storm + AccessBypass daemon
1973	    leak) can grind the retrieval phase for tens of minutes so the run never
1974	    reaches CLOSE. This is the relaunch-BLOCKING retrieval-phase deadline: on
1975	    expiry the snowball/fan-out STOPS firing further queries and the
1976	    already-fetched partial corpus is HANDED OFF to tiering/consolidation/
1977	    generation/render WITH disclosure (§-1.3 — the unfetched tail is disclosed,
1978	    NEVER silently dropped, and the run COMPLETES+RENDERS rather than dying on a
1979	    bare timeout).
1980	
1981	    Read at CALL time (LAW VI — env-overridable per run). Default 2700s (45 min)
1982	    (UNIT 6 WAVE-B: raised from 1800 so a large-corpus fetch fan-out under the
1983	    aligned-concurrency + capped-PDF-worker regime has headroom to complete
1984	    rather than tripping the wall mid-fetch): generous enough that a healthy
1985	    retrieval never trips it, tight enough that a fetch-timeout storm cannot
1986	    grind indefinitely. Pure (env-only); unit-testable.
1987	    """
1988	    return _env_float("PG_RETRIEVAL_WALL_SECONDS", 2700.0)

2002	    Unlike ``_env_float`` (which COERCES a malformed override back to the
2003	    default), a set-but-malformed value here FAILS LOUD (raises ``ValueError``):
2004	    this floor is a SAFETY gate, and a silently-defaulted bad knob must never
2005	    mask a starved run. An unset / blank var uses the default; a valid float is
2006	    honored verbatim (env-overridable per run).
2007	    """
2008	    raw = os.getenv(PG_MIN_FETCH_YIELD_ENV)
2009	    if raw is None or not raw.strip():
2010	        return _MIN_FETCH_YIELD_DEFAULT
2011	    value = float(raw)  # malformed -> ValueError (fail-loud, intentional)
2012	    if not math.isfinite(value):
2013	        raise ValueError(

2015	        )
2016	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1369) FIX C — the yield is a fetched/attempted FRACTION, so it MUST lie in [0.0, 1.0]
2017	    # (LAW VI). A value > 1.0 makes the floor unsatisfiable => EVERY batch HALTs; a value < 0.0 disables the
2018	    # floor entirely. Fail LOUD (never silently accept an out-of-range safety knob).
2019	    if not (0.0 <= value <= 1.0):
2020	        raise ValueError(
2021	            f"{PG_MIN_FETCH_YIELD_ENV} must be a fraction in [0.0, 1.0], got {value!r}"
2022	        )

2030	def _min_fetch_yield_min_attempts() -> int:
2031	    """I-deepfix-001 (#1369) FIX 3 — minimum fetch attempts before the yield gate
2032	    is allowed to HALT (LAW VI, FAIL-LOUD).
2033	
2034	    The yield gate fires per ``run_live_retrieval`` call. A tiny auxiliary batch
2035	    (1 URL that times out -> rate 0.0; or 1 success + 3 timeouts -> 0.25) would
2036	    otherwise hard-kill an OTHERWISE-HEALTHY paid run on statistically
2037	    meaningless counts. This floor is the primary guard against that
2038	    tiny-batch false-halt: the gate only HALTS once at least this many fetches
2039	    were actually attempted.
2040	
2041	    Read at CALL time (env-overridable per run). Unset / blank -> the default.
2042	    A set-but-malformed / negative value FAILS LOUD (raises) — a safety knob
2043	    must never silently default.

2045	    raw = os.getenv(PG_MIN_FETCH_YIELD_MIN_ATTEMPTS_ENV)
2046	    if raw is None or not raw.strip():
2047	        return _MIN_FETCH_YIELD_MIN_ATTEMPTS_DEFAULT
2048	    value = int(raw)  # malformed -> ValueError (fail-loud, intentional)
2049	    if value < 0:
2050	        raise ValueError(
2051	            f"{PG_MIN_FETCH_YIELD_MIN_ATTEMPTS_ENV} must be a non-negative int, "

2057	def _fetch_yield_gate(success: int, timeout: int, failures: int = 0) -> float:
2058	    """UNIT 6 fetch-yield HALT gate (faithfulness-neutral, §-1.3).
2059	
2060	    Compute ``success_rate = success / total_attempted`` where
2061	    ``total_attempted = success + timeout + failures``. I-deepfix-001 (#1369)
2062	    FIX 3 hardened two false-halt / false-pass holes:
2063	
2064	    (a) MIN-ATTEMPTS FLOOR: the gate only HALTS once ``total_attempted`` reaches
2065	        ``PG_MIN_FETCH_YIELD_MIN_ATTEMPTS`` (default 50). Below that floor a
2066	        tiny auxiliary batch (a single timing-out URL -> rate 0.0) can no longer
2067	        hard-kill a healthy run — we emit the ``skip`` ``[activation]`` marker
2068	        and return the rate WITHOUT raising.
2069	    (b) DENOMINATOR INCLUDES NON-TIMEOUT FAILURES: a corpus thinned by HTTP /
2070	        extraction FAILURES (``failures``), not just timeouts, now counts
2071	        against the yield, so a batch that fetched few usable sources because
2072	        most requests errored can no longer sneak past the floor.
2073	
2074	    Below the floor (and attempted) -> emit the HALT marker + RAISE
2075	    ``FetchStarvationError`` so the run halts BEFORE banking a starved corpus
2076	    (the 922/990-timeout hang-and-leak class). At/above -> emit the pass marker
2077	    and return the rate. Div0 guarded (an empty batch cannot be "starved"). The
2078	    ``[activation]`` marker fires on EVERY path (HALT / pass / skip) so the
2079	    anti-dark run-log canary sees the gate ran.
2080	
2081	    Pure except for the env reads + logging; unit-testable with synthetic
2082	    counts.
2083	    """
2084	    floor = _min_fetch_yield()
2085	    min_attempts = _min_fetch_yield_min_attempts()
2086	    total_attempted = success + timeout + failures
2087	    rate = (success / total_attempted) if total_attempted > 0 else 1.0
2088	    if total_attempted < min_attempts:
2089	        logger.info(
2090	            # I-deepfix-001 (#1369) FIX D — carry rate= on the SKIP path too, so all 3 gate paths
2091	            # (pass / HALT / skip) are greppable by the run-day "fetch_yield_gate: rate=" needle.
2092	            "[activation] fetch_yield_gate: rate=%.3f attempts=%d < floor=%d -> skip",
2093	            rate, total_attempted, min_attempts,
2094	        )
2095	        return rate
2096	    if rate < floor:
2097	        logger.info(
2098	            "[activation] fetch_yield_gate: rate=%.3f floor=%.2f -> HALT",
2099	            rate, floor,
2100	        )
2101	        raise FetchStarvationError(
2102	            f"fetch yield {rate:.3f} < floor {floor:.2f} "
2103	            f"(success={success} timeout={timeout} failures={failures}) — "
2104	            f"halting before banking a starved corpus"
2105	        )
2106	    logger.info(
2107	        "[activation] fetch_yield_gate: rate=%.3f floor=%.2f -> pass",
2108	        rate, floor,
2109	    )
2110	    return rate

2113	def _retrieval_fetch_wall_fraction() -> float:
2114	    """I-deepfix-001 (wall/tiering-abort fix, #1344): the FRACTION of the REMAINING
2115	    retrieval wall the content-FETCH batch may consume, so slow web-fetch cannot eat the
2116	    ENTIRE ``PG_RETRIEVAL_WALL_SECONDS`` and starve the post-fetch classify loop (which
2117	    builds the corpus + defers W5 credibility-tiering). The remainder of the wall is
2118	    reserved for classification.
2119	
2120	    Read at CALL time (LAW VI — env-overridable per run). Exactly three outcomes:
2121	      * ``PG_RETRIEVAL_FETCH_WALL_FRACTION`` UNSET  -> ``0.75`` (the DEFAULT-ON cap).
2122	      * a valid finite float in ``(0.0, 1.0]``      -> that value.
2123	      * ANY set-but-invalid value (non-numeric, NaN/inf, zero, negative, or > 1.0)
2124	        -> ``1.0`` = LEGACY full-wall fetch budget, byte-for-byte.
2125	
2126	    P2 fix (Codex REQUEST_CHANGES): the prior body routed through ``_env_float`` which
2127	    COERCES a garbage / NaN / zero / negative override to the 0.75 default BEFORE the
2128	    range check ever sees it — so a bad env silently imposed a 0.75 recall cap the operator
2129	    never validly requested. Reading the raw value and fail-SAFING every invalid override to
2130	    the legacy 1.0 full-wall makes the docstring's contract TRUE (a garbage knob widens the
2131	    fetch budget, never hides a drop). ``math`` is already imported (used by ``_env_float``).
2132	    Pure (env-only); unit-testable. Faithfulness-neutral: a source not fetched before the cap
2133	    is DISCLOSED via ``fetch_subwall_hit`` / ``notes``, never silently dropped (§-1.3).
2134	    """
2135	    raw = os.getenv("PG_RETRIEVAL_FETCH_WALL_FRACTION")
2136	    if raw is None:
2137	        return 0.75
2138	    try:
2139	        frac = float(raw)
2140	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
2141	        return 1.0
2142	    if not math.isfinite(frac) or not (0.0 < frac <= 1.0):
2143	        return 1.0
2144	    return frac
2145

2147	def _retrieval_w2_wall_fraction() -> float:
2148	    """I-deepfix-001 (wall/tiering-abort fix, #1344) P1b: the FRACTION of the REMAINING
2149	    retrieval wall the default-on W2 content-relevance batch (Stage-1 reranker one-pass +
2150	    GLM escalation) may consume, so W2 cannot eat the ENTIRE remaining wall and re-starve the
2151	    post-fetch classify/W5 loop it was supposed to protect. The remainder is reserved for
2152	    classification.
2153	
2154	    Mirrors :func:`_retrieval_fetch_wall_fraction`'s fail-safe parser exactly. Three outcomes:
2155	      * ``PG_RETRIEVAL_W2_WALL_FRACTION`` UNSET     -> ``0.5`` (DEFAULT-ON: W2 gets at most
2156	        half the remaining wall; classify/W5 keeps the other half).
2157	      * a valid finite float in ``(0.0, 1.0]``      -> that value.
2158	      * ANY set-but-invalid value                   -> ``1.0`` = pass the FULL retrieval
2159	        deadline unchanged = byte-identical to the pre-P1b threading.
2160	
2161	    Read at CALL time (LAW VI). Pure (env-only); unit-testable. Faithfulness-neutral: W2 only
2162	    STOPS earlier — content_relevance_judge's always-release keeps un-scored / un-escalated
2163	    passages at FULL weight (never demote-on-timeout, never drop — §-1.3).
2164	    """
2165	    raw = os.getenv("PG_RETRIEVAL_W2_WALL_FRACTION")
2166	    if raw is None:
2167	        return 0.5
2168	    try:
2169	        frac = float(raw)
2170	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
2171	        return 1.0
2172	    if not math.isfinite(frac) or not (0.0 < frac <= 1.0):
2173	        return 1.0
2174	    return frac

2177	class CorpusTruncationError(RuntimeError):
2178	    """I-arch-004 F18b (#1255): raised when the post-fetch loop budget breaks
2179	    mid-corpus AND ``PG_CORPUS_TRUNCATION_POLICY=fail_closed``.
2180	
2181	    The post-fetch loop can hit its wall-clock deadline before classifying every
2182	    fetched candidate; the legacy behavior set a fail-loud ``corpus_truncated``
2183	    flag and BROKE, leaving a PARTIAL corpus to flow into generation (§-1.3: a
2184	    silent recall degradation — a "1000-URL" run could be composed from the first
2185	    few hundred sources with no in-run gate). ``fail_closed`` raises this BEFORE
2186	    any generator token is billed so a truncated corpus never ships. The post-hoc
2187	    scorer backstop (``score_run._check_polaris_gate``) still rejects a truncated
2188	    manifest under the default ``warn`` policy; this error is the additive in-run
2189	    gate."""
2190	
2191	
2192	class FetchStarvationError(RuntimeError):
2193	    """UNIT 6 (I-wire-001 TRACK-2 fetch robustness): raised when the parallel
2194	    fetch batch yielded too few USABLE fetches relative to timeouts — the
2195	    922/990-timeout HANG-AND-LEAK cascade signature (wedged mineru-PDF workers
2196	    exceed the abandon-join, hold their in-flight slot forever, and drain the
2197	    pool). The fetch-yield HALT gate computes ``success/(success+timeout)`` and
2198	    raises this when it falls below ``PG_MIN_FETCH_YIELD`` so the run HALTS
2199	    BEFORE banking a starved corpus into consolidation/composition.
2200	
2201	    Faithfulness-neutral (§-1.3): this HALTS the run, it never fabricates or
2202	    silently drops — a starved corpus is refused loudly, not shipped thin."""
2203

2205	def _corpus_truncation_policy() -> str:
2206	    """Read ``PG_CORPUS_TRUNCATION_POLICY`` (LAW VI). One of:
2207	
2208	      - ``warn``   (DEFAULT): legacy behavior — set ``corpus_truncated`` and BREAK.
2209	                   Byte-identical to pre-F18b. The post-hoc scorer backstop is the
2210	                   gate; the in-run report still composes over the partial corpus.
2211	      - ``repair``: do NOT break at the deadline; CONTINUE processing the remaining
2212	                   candidates so recall is preserved (§-1.3 weight-not-filter). The
2213	                   per-candidate Layer-1 bound (``_bounded_openalex_enrich``,
2214	                   PG_OPENALEX_ENRICH_DEADLINE) still prevents a single wedged
2215	                   candidate from hanging the run, so dropping the loop-level
2216	                   deadline does not reintroduce the #554 hang.
2217	      - ``fail_closed``: RAISE ``CorpusTruncationError`` at the deadline so a
2218	                   truncated corpus never reaches generation (no tokens billed).
2219	
2220	    Unknown / empty value falls back to ``warn`` (byte-identical default)."""
2221	    raw = (os.getenv("PG_CORPUS_TRUNCATION_POLICY", "") or "").strip().lower()
2222	    if raw in ("warn", "repair", "fail_closed"):
2223	        return raw
2224	    if raw:
2225	        logger.warning(
2226	            "[live_retriever] PG_CORPUS_TRUNCATION_POLICY=%r is not one of "
2227	            "warn/repair/fail_closed — using 'warn' (legacy default)",
2228	            raw,
2229	        )
2230	    return "warn"

2233	def _bounded_openalex_enrich(
2234	    url: str, title: str, stats: Optional[dict[str, int]] = None,
2235	) -> dict[str, Any]:
2236	    """Wall-clock-bounded wrapper around `_openalex_enrich` (GH #554).
2237	
2238	    `_openalex_enrich` issues `httpx.Client(timeout=DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT)`
2239	    requests. `httpx`'s timeout bounds each request *phase*
2240	    (connect/read/write/pool) but NOT total request time, so a wedged or
2241	    byte-trickling OpenAlex response (slowloris pattern) is never
2242	    hard-bounded. The post-`parallel_fetch` candidate loop in
2243	    `run_live_retrieval` is synchronous, so one wedged enrich call hangs the
2244	    whole run before it reaches any terminal verdict (#554 — demo-fatal).
2245	
2246	    Run the call in a daemon thread and abandon it past
2247	    `PG_OPENALEX_ENRICH_DEADLINE` (default 45 s = 2x the 20 s httpx phase
2248	    timeout, covering the DOI-lookup + title-search-fallback double request,
2249	    + margin). Enrichment is optional — the tier classifier degrades
2250	    gracefully to title/content signals without it. `stats["enrich_timeouts"]`
2251	    is incremented on timeout so the caller can fail-fast.
2252	    """
2253	    deadline = _env_float("PG_OPENALEX_ENRICH_DEADLINE", 45.0)
2254	    holder: dict[str, Any] = {}
2255	
2256	    def _worker() -> None:
2257	        try:
2258	            holder["value"] = _openalex_enrich(url, title)
2259	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
2260	            holder["error"] = exc
2261	
2262	    worker = threading.Thread(
2263	        target=_worker, name="openalex-enrich", daemon=True,
2264	    )
2265	    worker.start()
2266	    worker.join(timeout=deadline)
2267	    if worker.is_alive():
2268	        if stats is not None:
2269	            stats["enrich_timeouts"] = stats.get("enrich_timeouts", 0) + 1
2270	        logger.warning(
2271	            "[live_retriever] OpenAlex enrich exceeded %.0fs for %s — "
2272	            "skipping enrichment (daemon thread abandoned)",
2273	            deadline, url[:80],
2274	        )
2275	        return {}
2276	    if "error" in holder:
2277	        logger.debug(
2278	            "[live_retriever] OpenAlex enrich raised for %r: %s",
2279	            url, holder["error"],
2280	        )
2281	        return {}
2282	    return holder.get("value", {})
2283

2285	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2286	# I-deepfix-001 (#1344) WAVE 2 — fetch throughput. Two DEFAULT-OFF flags that
2287	# stop already-FETCHED bodies from being lost under the retrieval wall (the
2288	# throughput collapse behind "only 3 of ~35 queries mattered"):
2289	#   (A) PG_POST_FETCH_ENRICH_PARALLEL — pre-batch the per-candidate OpenAlex
2290	#       enrich in a BOUNDED ThreadPool BEFORE the serial classify loop, so a slow
2291	#       serial enrich tail (~13-30s/source) cannot consume the whole retrieval
2292	#       wall and leave fetched bodies unclassified.
2293	#   (B) PG_WALL_CLASSIFY_RESCUE — at the wall break, keep classifying the
2294	#       remaining already-fetched bodies RULES-ONLY at the deterministic rules-
2295	#       floor tier and KEEP them (§-1.3 keep-not-drop), so they feed the CRAG
2296	#       corrective reserve instead of being dropped.
2297	# Both are FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: enrich is credibility METADATA and the rescue
2298	# only classifies+keeps. The FROZEN faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI /
2299	# 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding) is byte-untouched — every rescued /
2300	# enriched row still re-passes it unchanged downstream.
2301	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2302	_ENV_POST_FETCH_ENRICH_PARALLEL = "PG_POST_FETCH_ENRICH_PARALLEL"
2303	_ENV_POST_FETCH_ENRICH_WORKERS = "PG_POST_FETCH_ENRICH_WORKERS"
2304	_ENV_POST_FETCH_ENRICH_WALL_FRACTION = "PG_POST_FETCH_ENRICH_WALL_FRACTION"
2305	_ENV_WALL_CLASSIFY_RESCUE = "PG_WALL_CLASSIFY_RESCUE"
2306	_ENV_WALL_RESCUE_WEIGHT = "PG_WALL_RESCUE_WEIGHT"
2307	# Bounded default worker cap for the Fix-A pre-batch (LAW VI — env-overridable):
2308	# enough concurrency to collapse the serial enrich tail, small enough to never
2309	# fan out unbounded onto OpenAlex.
2310	_POST_FETCH_ENRICH_WORKERS_DEFAULT = 8
2311	# WAVE-2 Fix B rules-floor WEIGHT for a wall-rescued body (§-1.3 keep-at-floor,
2312	# NEVER drop). Mirrors content_relevance_judge._DEFAULT_DEMOTE_WEIGHT (0.25): a
2313	# source classified RULES-ONLY past the wall never had its content-relevance
2314	# scored, so it is KEPT at the low-but-nonzero rules floor — never at full (1.0)
2315	# weight (which would falsely rank it as fully relevant) and never at zero (a
2316	# drop). Clamped to (0, 1] by `_wall_rescue_weight`.
2317	_WALL_RESCUE_WEIGHT_DEFAULT = 0.25
2318	# Disclosed, KEEP-NEUTRAL telemetry label for a wall-rescued body. Deliberately
2319	# NOT `demoted`/`escalated_demoted` (weighted_enrichment._CONFIRMED_OFFTOPIC_LABELS)
2320	# — a rescued body was NEVER judged off-topic, only left unscored past the wall, so
2321	# marking it off-topic would suppress its cite surface (a §-1.3 drop). This label
2322	# is keep-neutral everywhere it is read (never suppressed), only surfacing the
2323	# honest "kept at the rules floor because the wall was hit" disclosure.
2324	_WALL_RESCUE_LABEL = "wall_rescue_floor"
2325

2327	def _post_fetch_enrich_parallel_enabled() -> bool:
2328	    """WAVE-2 Fix A master switch (default OFF). Unset => the post-fetch loop runs
2329	    the serial per-candidate OpenAlex enrich exactly as before (BYTE-IDENTICAL).
2330	    ON => the enrich is pre-batched in a BOUNDED ThreadPool before the classify
2331	    loop so the serial enrich tail cannot burn the retrieval wall and drop
2332	    already-fetched bodies unclassified. LAW VI env-overridable; truthy
2333	    1/true/on/yes. Faithfulness-neutral (enrich is credibility metadata)."""
2334	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_POST_FETCH_ENRICH_PARALLEL, "").strip().lower() in (
2335	        "1", "true", "on", "yes",
2336	    )

2345	def _post_fetch_enrich_wall_fraction() -> float:
2346	    """WAVE-2 Fix A (Codex wave-2 P1a): the FRACTION of the REMAINING retrieval wall the
2347	    parallel OpenAlex enrich pre-batch may consume, so the batch cannot burn the ENTIRE
2348	    wall before the classify loop even starts.
2349	
2350	    The pre-batch's collection is SYNCHRONOUS (it blocks in index order until every
2351	    future resolves or the deadline passes). Handing it the FULL ``_retrieval_deadline``
2352	    let a slow early future stall the whole collection to the wall — starving the very
2353	    classify loop the fix was meant to protect: with rescue OFF the fetched-body tail is
2354	    dropped at candidate 0, and with rescue ON every candidate falls into rules-only
2355	    rescue. Reserving the remainder for classification closes that (mirrors
2356	    :func:`_retrieval_w2_wall_fraction`, the identical W2 fix).
2357	
2358	    Three outcomes (fail-safe parser identical to :func:`_retrieval_w2_wall_fraction`):
2359	      * ``PG_POST_FETCH_ENRICH_WALL_FRACTION`` UNSET -> ``0.5`` (enrich gets at most half
2360	        the remaining wall; classification keeps the other half).
2361	      * a valid finite float in ``(0.0, 1.0]``       -> that value.
2362	      * ANY set-but-invalid value                    -> ``1.0`` = the FULL retrieval
2363	        deadline unchanged (the pre-P1a behaviour; OFF path is unaffected either way).
2364	
2365	    Read at CALL time (LAW VI). Pure (env-only); unit-testable. Faithfulness-neutral:
2366	    a straggler not collected inside the reserved slice is recorded as ``{}`` — the SAME
2367	    empty result the serial per-candidate enrich returns on timeout (an unenriched row is
2368	    disclosed/undated downstream, NEVER dropped — §-1.3)."""
2369	    raw = os.getenv(_ENV_POST_FETCH_ENRICH_WALL_FRACTION)
2370	    if raw is None:
2371	        return 0.5
2372	    try:
2373	        frac = float(raw)
2374	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
2375	        return 1.0
2376	    if not math.isfinite(frac) or not (0.0 < frac <= 1.0):
2377	        return 1.0
2378	    return frac

2381	def _wall_rescue_weight() -> float:
2382	    """WAVE-2 Fix B (Codex wave-2 P1b): the rules-floor content-relevance WEIGHT a
2383	    wall-rescued body is KEPT at (§-1.3 keep-at-floor, never drop).
2384	
2385	    A body classified RULES-ONLY past the retrieval wall never had its content-relevance
2386	    scored, so it must NOT carry the default full weight (1.0) — that would falsely rank
2387	    it as fully relevant. It is instead kept at the deterministic rules floor (default
2388	    ``_WALL_RESCUE_WEIGHT_DEFAULT`` = 0.25, matching the content-relevance demote floor):
2389	    low but NON-zero, so the source still flows to composition at reduced weight and is
2390	    NEVER dropped. Clamped to ``(0, 1]`` (a zero/negative/garbage override can never
2391	    zero-drop a rescued source). Read at CALL time (LAW VI). Faithfulness-neutral — the
2392	    frozen strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance engine still re-checks the row."""
2393	    raw = os.getenv(_ENV_WALL_RESCUE_WEIGHT, "").strip()
2394	    if not raw:
2395	        return _WALL_RESCUE_WEIGHT_DEFAULT
2396	    try:
2397	        w = float(raw)
2398	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
2399	        return _WALL_RESCUE_WEIGHT_DEFAULT
2400	    if not math.isfinite(w) or not (0.0 < w <= 1.0):
2401	        return _WALL_RESCUE_WEIGHT_DEFAULT
2402	    return w

2417	def _loop_budget_truncation_active(
2418	    *, wall_rescue_mode: bool, now: float, loop_deadline: float

2463	def _openalex_full_iso(iso: str | None, *, ceiling: bool) -> str | None:
2464	    """Normalize a ``UserConstraints`` ISO bound to a full OpenAlex ``YYYY-MM-DD`` date.

2513	def _prefetch_openalex_enrich_parallel(
2514	    candidates: list[Any],
2515	    *,
2516	    workers: int,
2517	    deadline_monotonic: Optional[float] = None,
2518	    enrich_fn: Optional[Any] = None,
2519	) -> dict[int, dict[str, Any]]:
2520	    """WAVE-2 Fix A: bounded-parallel pre-batch of the per-candidate OpenAlex enrich.
2521	
2522	    Returns ``{i: enrich_dict}`` keyed by each candidate's position in
2523	    ``candidates`` — ORDER-STABLE (``result[i]`` always corresponds to
2524	    ``candidates[i]`` regardless of completion order), so the merge is identical to
2525	    the serial loop's per-candidate enrich. The batch NEVER reorders ``candidates``
2526	    and NEVER touches ``seen_urls`` (the caller's upstream first-seen-wins candidate
2527	    ordering is preserved); it only READS ``cand.url`` / ``cand.title`` (immutable)
2528	    and calls the SAME bounded enrich the serial path calls, so there is no race on
2529	    shared candidate/dedup state. The only shared write is the module authority
2530	    cache inside the enrich, whose per-call sqlite connection + top-level
2531	    try/except make a lock-contended write fail-open to ``{}`` (never corruption);
2532	    the one-time cache migration is warmed once here before fan-out.
2533	
2534	    Each candidate's enrich is hard-bounded (``enrich_fn`` defaults to
2535	    ``_bounded_openalex_enrich`` — a daemon-thread join-timeout abandon), so a
2536	    wedged OpenAlex response can never hang the pool teardown. An overall
2537	    ``deadline_monotonic`` (absolute ``time.monotonic()``) additionally caps the
2538	    whole batch: a straggler not collected by the deadline is recorded as ``{}`` —
2539	    the SAME empty result the serial per-candidate bound returns on timeout (§-1.3:
2540	    an unenriched row is disclosed/undated downstream, NEVER dropped). Codex wave-2
2541	    P0: once the deadline passes the batch STOPS waiting AND CANCELS every still-queued
2542	    future so no further OpenAlex enrich starts during the classify phase — the batch
2543	    is bounded by the WALL, not just by worker count. Already-running futures (at most
2544	    ``max_workers``) cannot be cancelled but are each self-bounded by the per-call
2545	    daemon join-timeout. Pure w.r.t. inputs; unit-testable with a 2-arg stub
2546	    ``enrich_fn`` (no heavy models).
2547	    """
2548	    results: dict[int, dict[str, Any]] = {}
2549	    n = len(candidates)
2550	    if n == 0:
2551	        return results
2552	    _enrich = enrich_fn if enrich_fn is not None else _bounded_openalex_enrich
2553	    # Warm the authority-cache migration ONCE on the main thread so the pool
2554	    # workers never race the one-time `_ensure_authority_cache_migrated` global.
2555	    try:
2556	        _ensure_authority_cache_migrated()
2557	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — cache warm is best-effort; enrich fails open
2558	        pass
2559	    from concurrent.futures import (  # noqa: E402
2560	        ThreadPoolExecutor,
2561	        TimeoutError as _FutureTimeout,
2562	    )
2563	
2564	    max_workers = max(1, min(int(workers), n))
2565	    per_call_timeout = _env_float("PG_OPENALEX_ENRICH_DEADLINE", 45.0) + 5.0
2566	    executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(
2567	        max_workers=max_workers, thread_name_prefix="oa-enrich-batch",
2568	    )
2569	    # Declared BEFORE the try so the finally can cancel every submitted future even
2570	    # if the submit loop raises partway (Codex wave-2 P0 — no leaked queued enrich).
2571	    _future_by_idx: dict[int, Any] = {}
2572	    try:
2573	        for idx, cand in enumerate(candidates):
2574	            _future_by_idx[idx] = executor.submit(
2575	                _enrich, getattr(cand, "url", ""), getattr(cand, "title", ""),
2576	            )
2577	        # Codex wave-2 P0: once the RESERVED wall slice is spent the pre-batch must
2578	        # stop consuming network/threads — it is bounded by the WALL DEADLINE, not
2579	        # just by worker count. When the deadline passes we STOP waiting on the
2580	        # remaining futures AND CANCEL each still-queued one so no further enrich
2581	        # starts during the classify phase. Every index STILL gets a value ({} for an
2582	        # un-collected straggler) — §-1.3 keep-not-drop: the merge never loses an
2583	        # index, it only records the SAME fail-open {} the serial enrich returns on
2584	        # timeout. (Running futures <= max_workers can't be cancelled but are each
2585	        # self-bounded by the per-call daemon join-timeout.)
2586	        _deadline_expired = False
2587	        for idx in range(n):  # collect in INDEX order -> order-stable merge
2588	            fut = _future_by_idx[idx]
2589	            if _deadline_expired:
2590	                fut.cancel()  # still-queued -> never runs; already-running -> no-op
2591	                results[idx] = {}
2592	                continue
2593	            if deadline_monotonic is not None:
2594	                _timeout = max(0.0, deadline_monotonic - time.monotonic())
2595	            else:
2596	                _timeout = per_call_timeout
2597	            try:
2598	                val = fut.result(timeout=_timeout)
2599	            except _FutureTimeout:
2600	                val = {}
2601	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — mirror serial enrich's {}-on-error
2602	                val = {}
2603	            results[idx] = val if isinstance(val, dict) else {}
2604	            # Past the reserved deadline: do NOT keep waiting on / letting the
2605	            # remaining futures run — cancel them on the next iterations + finally.
2606	            if deadline_monotonic is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline_monotonic:
2607	                _deadline_expired = True
2608	    finally:
2609	        # Cancel every still-pending future so a queued OpenAlex enrich cannot keep
2610	        # firing after the reserved wall slice (Codex wave-2 P0). cancel_futures also
2611	        # purges the pool's work queue; still-running workers are self-bounded by the
2612	        # per-call daemon join-timeout, so teardown never blocks on a wedged enrich.
2613	        for _f in _future_by_idx.values():
2614	            _f.cancel()
2615	        executor.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
2616	    return results
2617

2660	# I-meta-002-q1d (#954): table-aware linearization. _strip_html flattens <table> markup to running text,
2661	# so a result-table cell loses its header association ("Discontinuation due to nausea ... 3.8%" collapses to
2662	# a floating "3.8") and integer / %-without-decimal cells become unanchored — strict_verify can then only
2663	# verify the loose decimals that survive in prose, under-verifying the richest clinical data (results tables).
2664	# This no-network pass detects <table> blocks and linearizes each data row as "header: cell | header: cell"
2665	# BEFORE flattening, so the cell keeps its column header in the text the provenance window captures.
2666	_TABLE_RE = re.compile(r"<table\b[^>]*>(.*?)</table>", re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)
2667	_ROW_RE = re.compile(r"<tr\b[^>]*>(.*?)</tr>", re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)
2668	# Capture each cell WITH its tag (th vs td) so a column-header row is distinguishable from a row-header.
2669	_CELL_TAGGED_RE = re.compile(r"<(t[hd])\b[^>]*>(.*?)</t[hd]>", re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)
2670	# colspan/rowspan shift columns → index-zip would mis-align → degrade (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1).
2671	_SPAN_ATTR_RE = re.compile(r"\b(?:col|row)span\s*=", re.IGNORECASE)
2672	
2673	
2674	def _cell_text(cell_html: str) -> str:
2675	    """Strip inner tags from a single cell and collapse whitespace."""
2676	    txt = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", " ", cell_html)
2677	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", txt).strip()
2678	
2679	
2680	def _parse_row_cells(row_html: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
2681	    """Return [(tag, text)] for each <th>/<td> in the row (tag lowercased)."""
2682	    return [(m.group(1).lower(), _cell_text(m.group(2))) for m in _CELL_TAGGED_RE.finditer(row_html)]
2683	
2684	
2685	def linearize_html_tables(html: str) -> str:
2686	    """Return result-table rows linearized so table numbers survive in text WITH their column header when —
2687	    and ONLY when — the source unambiguously declares one. Pure regex, no network, fail-open ('').
2688	
2689	    Header:cell association is emitted ONLY for the CANONICAL column-header table: the first non-empty row
2690	    is ENTIRELY <th> with non-empty header text, the table has >1 column, NO colspan/rowspan anywhere, and
2691	    <th> appears in NO other row (so it is column headers, not per-row row-headers). EVERY other shape —
2692	    span tables, headerless tables, row-header / mixed <th>+<td> data rows, empty-<th>-before-data — DEGRADES
2693	    to plain ' | '-joined cells. Joined still surfaces the number next to its row label, but a cell can NEVER
2694	    receive a column header the source did not declare (no fabricated provenance). This single conservative
2695	    rule covers the Codex diff-gate P1s (colspan/rowspan, headerless-multirow, row-header, empty-th-before-
2696	    data) without enumerating each."""
2697	    try:
2698	        out_rows: list[str] = []
2699	        for table_html in _TABLE_RE.findall(html or ""):
2700	            raw_rows = _ROW_RE.findall(table_html)
2701	            if not raw_rows:
2702	                continue
2703	            rows = [_parse_row_cells(r) for r in raw_rows]
2704	            rows = [r for r in rows if any(text for _tag, text in r)]  # drop all-empty rows
2705	            if not rows:
2706	                continue
2707	
2708	            def _join(r: list[tuple[str, str]]) -> str:
2709	                return " | ".join(text for _tag, text in r if text)
2710	
2711	            first = rows[0]
2712	            canonical = (
2713	                not _SPAN_ATTR_RE.search(table_html)              # no colspan/rowspan
2714	                and len(first) > 1                                # multi-column
2715	                and all(tag == "th" for tag, _t in first)         # row 0 is ENTIRELY <th> (column headers)
2716	                and all(text for _tag, text in first)             # ... with non-empty header text
2717	                and not any(tag == "th" for r in rows[1:] for tag, _t in r)  # <th> only in row 0
2718	            )
2719	            if not canonical:
2720	                # DEGRADE: every ambiguous shape joins plainly — never a fabricated header:cell.
2721	                out_rows.extend(j for r in rows if (j := _join(r)))
2722	                continue
2723	            # CANONICAL column-header table: zip each data row to the <th> header row by column index.
2724	            headers = [text for _tag, text in first]
2725	            for row in rows[1:]:
2726	                cells = [text for _tag, text in row]
2727	                pairs = [
2728	                    f"{headers[j]}: {cells[j]}"
2729	                    if j < len(headers) and headers[j] and cells[j]
2730	                    else cells[j]
2731	                    for j in range(len(cells))
2732	                ]
2733	                joined = " | ".join(p for p in pairs if p)
2734	                if joined:
2735	                    out_rows.append(joined)
2736	        return "\n".join(r for r in out_rows if r)
2737	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive observability; never break fetch on a malformed table
2738	        return ""

2741	_JSONLD_SCRIPT_RE = re.compile(
2742	    r'<script[^>]*type\s*=\s*["\']application/ld\+json["\'][^>]*>(.*?)</script>',
2743	    re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE,
2744	)
2745	# Bound the captured JSON-LD so a pathological page cannot balloon the payload
2746	# the classifier scans (the junk patterns only need the @type marker near the top).
2747	_JSONLD_MAX_CHARS = 20000
2748	
2749	
2750	def _extract_jsonld_blocks(raw_html: str) -> str:
2751	    """Capture the raw <script type="application/ld+json"> block contents from
2752	    the RAW fetched HTML — BEFORE _strip_html() deletes every <script> block.
2753	
2754	    Diff-gate P1-C: the structural junk-detection classifier (Signal C,
2755	    news_article/login_wall/press_release classes) scans JSON-LD strings such as
2756	    `"@type":"NewsArticle"` that live ONLY inside ld+json <script> blocks. Those
2757	    blocks are destroyed by _strip_html (line removing <script>...</script>), so
2758	    the demotion never fires unless the JSON-LD is extracted FIRST and routed to
2759	    ClassificationSignals.structured_jsonld separately from the stripped body.
2760	
2761	    Returns the concatenated block contents (bounded), or "" when the input is
2762	    not raw HTML (e.g. Jina markdown / Crawl4AI cleaned text already had its
2763	    <script> blocks removed upstream) — honestly empty, never fabricated. Pure
2764	    regex, no network, fail-open ("").
2765	    """
2766	    if not raw_html or "ld+json" not in raw_html.lower():
2767	        return ""
2768	    try:
2769	        blocks = [m.group(1).strip() for m in _JSONLD_SCRIPT_RE.finditer(raw_html)]
2770	        joined = "\n".join(b for b in blocks if b)
2771	        return joined[:_JSONLD_MAX_CHARS]
2772	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive structural signal; never break fetch
2773	        return ""

2776	def _readability_extract(html: str) -> str:
2777	    """BB5-C05 (#1177): readability-lxml fallback extractor. trafilatura returns
2778	    empty trees on 30-50% of fetched pages; readability's article-detection
2779	    recovers many of them. Guarded import (skip-with-log when the optional dep
2780	    is absent — never break `_strip_html`). Returns extracted plain text or ""."""
2781	    if not html:
2782	        return ""
2783	    try:
2784	        from readability import Document  # type: ignore
2785	    except ImportError:
2786	        logger.debug(
2787	            "[live_retriever] BB5-C05 readability-lxml not installed — "
2788	            "skipping fallback extractor"
2789	        )
2790	        return ""
2791	    try:
2792	        summary_html = Document(html).summary(html_partial=True)
2793	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — readability runs lxml; a parse
2794	        # error must never break the strip path. (A C-level SIGSEGV would still
2795	        # escape, but readability only runs AFTER trafilatura already declined,
2796	        # and on the SAME doc that passed the trafilatura size gate.)
2797	        logger.debug(
2798	            "[live_retriever] BB5-C05 readability extract error (%s)",
2799	            type(exc).__name__,
2800	        )
2801	        return ""
2802	    # readability returns cleaned HTML — strip residual tags to plain text.
2803	    no_tags = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", " ", summary_html or "")
2804	    no_tags = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", no_tags)
2805	    return no_tags.strip()

2808	# I-extract-001 Layer-A (data-picked HTML main-content extractor): flag-gated
2809	# trafilatura profile at the post-fetch HTML->text seam (_strip_html). The
2810	# I-extract-001 Layer-A leaderboard measured trafilatura's favor_precision profile
2811	# trimming page furniture while holding body-anchor recall at 1.000 (no body loss).
2812	# The flag is OPT-IN: the default profile ("default" / unset) preserves the prior
2813	# trafilatura behavior so no existing run changes silently. Faithfulness-neutral:
2814	# it only changes WHICH text trafilatura returns; the readability-lxml -> regex
2815	# fallback chain and the downstream strict_verify span-grounding are untouched.
2816	PG_HTML_EXTRACTOR_ENV = "PG_HTML_EXTRACTOR"
2817	_HTML_EXTRACTOR_DEFAULT = "default"
2818	_HTML_EXTRACTOR_TRAFILATURA_PRECISION = "trafilatura_precision"

2821	def _trafilatura_extract_kwargs() -> dict[str, Any]:
2822	    """Resolve the trafilatura.extract kwargs for the configured PG_HTML_EXTRACTOR
2823	    profile. Read at CALL time (not import) so deploy-slate / test overrides apply
2824	    without re-import. Returns {} for the default profile (current behavior) and
2825	    {"favor_precision": True} for the data-picked precision profile. An UNKNOWN
2826	    value fails LOUD (warns + uses the default profile) rather than silently
2827	    no-op'ing a typo'd flag into the wrong extractor."""
2828	    mode = os.getenv(PG_HTML_EXTRACTOR_ENV, _HTML_EXTRACTOR_DEFAULT).strip().lower()
2829	    if mode in ("", _HTML_EXTRACTOR_DEFAULT):
2830	        return {}
2831	    if mode == _HTML_EXTRACTOR_TRAFILATURA_PRECISION:
2832	        return {"favor_precision": True}
2833	    logger.warning(
2834	        "[live_retriever] unknown %s=%r — expected %r or %r; using trafilatura "
2835	        "defaults",
2836	        PG_HTML_EXTRACTOR_ENV, mode, _HTML_EXTRACTOR_DEFAULT,
2837	        _HTML_EXTRACTOR_TRAFILATURA_PRECISION,
2838	    )
2839	    return {}

2842	def _strip_html(html: str) -> str:

2852	    # C-crash on a pathological doc is NOT a catchable Python exception.
2853	    try:
2854	        from src.tools.access_bypass import safe_trafilatura_extract
2855	        # I-extract-001 Layer-A: select the trafilatura profile via PG_HTML_EXTRACTOR

2857	        extract_kwargs = _trafilatura_extract_kwargs()
2858	        extracted = safe_trafilatura_extract(html, **extract_kwargs) or ""

2861	            if extract_kwargs:
2862	                # Behavioral canary: confirm the precision profile actually
2863	                # produced the extracted base text (the favor_precision kwarg
2864	                # reached trafilatura and did NOT silently fall through to the
2865	                # readability/regex tiers). Only fires when the opt-in flag is
2866	                # set, so default runs stay quiet.
2867	                logger.info(
2868	                    "[live_retriever] PG_HTML_EXTRACTOR=%s active — trafilatura "
2869	                    "favor_precision produced %d chars",
2870	                    _HTML_EXTRACTOR_TRAFILATURA_PRECISION, len(base),
2871	                )

2880	        # from. An oversized/suspect doc that skipped trafilatura must therefore
2881	        # ALSO skip readability and go straight to the regex strip, or it just
2882	        # re-enters libxml2 by another door (same SIGSEGV risk). Gate on the
2883	        # SHARED size predicate. Import the FUNCTION (not the constant value) so
2884	        # the deploy-slate PG_TRAFILATURA_MAX_HTML_CHARS override + test monkey-

3001	    """Legacy naive httpx fetcher. Kept as emergency fallback when
3002	    AccessBypass is unavailable (tests that don't want Crawl4AI browser
3003	    spawning, or sandboxes without Playwright).
3004	
3005	    Returns (content, ok, title, body_type, jsonld). Diff-gate P1-C: `jsonld`
3006	    is the raw ld+json <script> block contents extracted from the RAW HTML
3007	    BEFORE _strip_html deletes the <script> blocks (empty when the page carries
3008	    no JSON-LD)."""
3009	    try:
3010	        with httpx.Client(
3011	            timeout=DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT,
3012	            follow_redirects=True,
3013	            headers={
3014	                "User-Agent": (
3015	                    "Mozilla/5.0 (POLARIS-honest-rebuild/1.0) "
3016	                    "research-assistant"
3017	                ),
3018	                # BUG-BROTLI-R8d: httpx/aiohttp advertise `br` by default
3019	                # but can't always decode it. Forbid it so servers don't
3020	                # return Brotli-encoded bodies we can't read.
3021	                "Accept-Encoding": "gzip, deflate",
3022	            },
3023	        ) as c:
3024	            r = c.get(url)
3025	        if r.status_code != 200:
3026	            return "", False, "", "", ""
3027	        ctype = (r.headers.get("content-type", "") or "").lower()
3028	        raw = r.text if "text" in ctype or "html" in ctype or "json" in ctype else ""
3029	        if not raw and r.content:
3030	            raw = r.content.decode("utf-8", errors="ignore")
3031	        # BUG-M-13/M-14: extract title from raw HTML BEFORE stripping
3032	        # (the <title> tag is gone after _strip_html).
3033	        extracted_title = _extract_title_from_content(raw)
3034	        # BUG-M-17: detect article-type from bounded body region.
3035	        body_type = _detect_article_type_from_body(raw)
3036	        # Diff-gate P1-C: capture raw ld+json BEFORE _strip_html removes <script>.
3037	        jsonld = _extract_jsonld_blocks(raw)
3038	        content = _strip_html(raw)[:max_chars]
3039	        # BB5-C05 iter-2 (#1177, Codex P2): mirror the parallel/AccessBypass
3040	        # path's "fetched-200-but-empty-extract" bucket on the NAIVE/DIRECT path
3041	        # too. A real 200 body (>= _EXTRACT_NONEMPTY_RAW_FLOOR raw chars) whose
3042	        # extractor chain (trafilatura → readability → regex) collapses below
3043	        # _EXTRACT_EMPTY_FLOOR usable chars is the SAME distinct failure class
3044	        # here as there — previously this naive path swallowed it silently as a
3045	        # generic empty fetch. Surface it as its own auditable telemetry bucket.
3046	        # Telemetry-only: the returned tuple is unchanged (control flow is byte-
3047	        # identical). Reuses the existing floors — no new constants.
3048	        if len(raw) >= _EXTRACT_NONEMPTY_RAW_FLOOR and len(content) < _EXTRACT_EMPTY_FLOOR:
3049	            logger.info(
3050	                "[live_retriever] fetched_200_but_empty_extract %s "
3051	                "(method=httpx_naive raw_chars=%d extracted_chars=%d)",
3052	                url[:80], len(raw), len(content),
3053	            )
3054	            # BB5-C05 iter-2 (#1177, Codex P2 reconcile): record ONLY the keyed
3055	            # _m45 reason here — NOT a second _trace_tool. The production naive
3056	            # path is reached via `_fallback_naive_fetch`, which already emits the
3057	            # SINGLE per-fetch `_trace_tool` (ok/fail) for this call; adding an
3058	            # `empty_extract` _trace_tool too would double-record one fetch into
3059	            # two contradictory buckets (empty_extract AND fail), whereas the
3060	            # parallel path emits exactly one. The _m45 reason is keyed by URL +
3061	            # last-write-wins, so it carries the distinct empty-extract signal
3062	            # without duplicating the trace. (Direct callers of this function —
3063	            # e.g. the M-45 diagnostics refetch — still get the _m45 reason.)
3064	            _m45_record_fetch_telemetry(
3065	                url, "httpx_naive", "fetched_200_but_empty_extract",
3066	            )
3067	        # FIX #2 (I-deepfix-001): apply the SAME F14 min-body stub gate the
3068	        # PRIMARY branch applies. Sub-floor fallback bodies (paywall shells,
3069	        # 'Just a moment...' interstitials) are LABELED stub/ok=False (content
3070	        # KEPT — §-1.3) instead of admitted as ok=True. DEFAULT floor 0 (unset)
3071	        # => returns the tuple UNCHANGED => byte-identical.
3072	        return _apply_min_body_stub_gate(
3073	            content=content,
3074	            ok=bool(content),
3075	            url=url,
3076	            method="httpx_naive",
3077	            max_chars=max_chars,
3078	            extracted_title=extracted_title,
3079	            body_type=body_type,
3080	            jsonld=jsonld,
3081	        )
3082	    except Exception as exc:
3083	        logger.debug(
3084	            "[live_retriever] naive-httpx fetch %r failed: %s", url, exc,
3085	        )
3086	        return "", False, "", "", ""

3089	def _fallback_naive_fetch(
3090	    url: str,
3091	    max_chars: int,
3092	    t0: float,
3093	    primary_reason: str,
3094	) -> tuple[str, bool, str, str, str]:
3095	    """Run the naive-httpx fallback and record its FINAL outcome (I-meta-007b P2a).
3096	
3097	    Every content-fetch fallback in :func:`_fetch_content` (AccessBypass
3098	    disabled / unavailable / timed out / raised / produced no result) ends by
3099	    delegating to :func:`_fetch_content_httpx_naive`. The pre-fix code traced
3100	    ``fail``/``timeout`` BEFORE that delegation, so a SUCCESSFUL fallback was
3101	    still recorded as a failure. This helper traces the ACTUAL result instead:
3102	
3103	    * ``status="ok"`` + real ``bytes_received`` (length of fetched content) when
3104	      the naive fetch returned content,
3105	    * ``status="fail"`` otherwise — carrying ``primary_reason`` (the reason the
3106	      primary path fell back, e.g. ``access_bypass_timeout_90s``) in ``error``
3107	      for diagnostics.
3108	
3109	    ``backend_used="httpx_naive"`` uniformly because the naive fetcher is what
3110	    actually ran. ``t0`` is the single wall-clock start captured at the top of
3111	    :func:`_fetch_content` so latency reflects total fetch time. Record-only +
3112	    fail-safe (``_trace_tool`` swallows its own errors); returns the naive
3113	    fetcher's tuple unchanged so retrieval behavior is identical to before.
3114	    """
3115	    result = _fetch_content_httpx_naive(url, max_chars)
3116	    content, ok = result[0], result[1]
3117	    _trace_tool(
3118	        "fetch_content",
3119	        target=url,
3120	        status="ok" if ok else "fail",
3121	        latency_ms=(time.time() - t0) * 1000.0,
3122	        backend_used="httpx_naive",
3123	        bytes_received=len(content) if ok else 0,
3124	        error="" if ok else str(primary_reason),
3125	        primary_reason=str(primary_reason),
3126	    )
3127	    return result
3128

3131	    """M-42b (2026-04-22): re-fetch source content for deterministic
3132	    trial-table / timeline extraction when the evidence row's
3133	    existing `direct_quote` is thin (<100 chars) or the row was
3134	    never successfully fetched.
3135	
3136	    Returns a 2000-char extract (head + decimal-windows) via
3137	    _fetch_content + _build_provenance_quote, or empty string when
3138	    the URL cannot be fetched or returns thin content.
3139	
3140	    Caller is expected to cache the result on the evidence row for
3141	    the remainder of the run so repeat table generation within the
3142	    same sweep does not re-hit the network.
3143	
3144	    Generic wrapper — not trial/drug/domain-specific. Used by the
3145	    M-42b trial-table builder and the Trial Program Timeline builder.
3146	
3147	    M-45 (2026-04-22): see `refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics`
3148	    for a variant that returns structured per-URL diagnostics
3149	    (backend, char count, body type, eligibility, failure mode) for
3150	    the V28 preflight and downstream audits. This function is a thin
3151	    wrapper around that variant.
3152	    """
3153	    quote, _diag = refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics(url, max_chars)
3154	    return quote

3159	# before it returns, then read by `refetch_for_extraction_with_
3160	# diagnostics`. Keyed by url; overwritten on each call so memory

3185	def _refetch_full_body_enabled() -> bool:
3186	    """I-deepfix-004 A (#1375) — True (default) ⇒ the A15 re-fetch fetches the FULL
3187	    body (``PG_LIVE_CONTENT_MAX`` = ``DEFAULT_CONTENT_MAX_CHARS``) and caps only the
3188	    QUOTE at the caller's ``max_chars``, so ``_build_provenance_quote``'s decimal-window
3189	    design can reach a real number DEEP in a long body (the prior code truncated the
3190	    FETCH at ``max_chars`` = 2000 BEFORE the quote was built, so 171 A15 rows were head-
3191	    truncated and any deep number was thrown away). ``PG_REFETCH_FULL_BODY=0`` (or
3192	    off/false/no/disabled) ⇒ the FETCH is capped at ``max_chars`` exactly as before ⇒
3193	    byte-identical. Read at call time (LAW VI)."""
3194	    return os.environ.get("PG_REFETCH_FULL_BODY", "1").strip().lower() not in (

3199	def refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics(
3200	    url: str, max_chars: int = 2000,
3201	) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
3202	    """M-45 (2026-04-22): refetch with structured per-URL diagnostics.
3203	
3204	    Codex V28 plan pass-2 APPROVED this diagnostic-first approach.
3205	    V27 still produced thin quotes for paywalled PDFs despite the
3206	    AccessBypass cascade existing. This variant records WHY each
3207	    refetch landed or failed so audits can branch the fix
3208	    (explicit-wire Jina/Firecrawl, better extraction window, or
3209	    strict skip) based on real data instead of assumption.
3210	
3211	    Returns (quote, diagnostics) where:
3212	      - quote: same as `refetch_for_extraction` — non-empty iff the
3213	        refetched content is ≥100 chars and the provenance quote
3214	        was built; empty string otherwise. Strict ≥100 char contract
3215	        preserved (no statement fallback, no prose fallback).
3216	      - diagnostics: dict with these keys:
3217	          url: the input URL (truncated to 200 chars for JSON safety)
3218	          attempted: bool — did we try the fetch at all
3219	          method: str — AccessBypass method that produced content
3220	            ('crawl4ai', 'jina', 'firecrawl', 'httpx', 'archive_org',
3221	            'scihub', or 'none')
3222	          raw_char_count: int — bytes returned by _fetch_content
3223	            before provenance-quote extraction
3224	          body_type: str — 'abstract' / 'full_text' / 'paywall_shell'
3225	            / 'html_meta' per `_detect_article_type_from_body`
3226	          eligible: bool — True iff quote was emitted (≥100 chars)
3227	          failure_mode: str — one of:
3228	            '' (eligible), 'exception', 'fetch_failed',
3229	            'thin_content', 'paywall_shell', 'fetch_shell',
3230	            'wrong_content_front_matter'
3231	            ('fetch_shell' = clean_fetch_body reported the whole body is a
3232	            boilerplate/interstitial shell — not extractable, not cited;
3233	            'wrong_content_front_matter' = I-deepfix-004 D: the cleaned body is
3234	            a journal-issue cover/TOC/masthead, not the cited article — an A15
3235	            recovery must NEVER adopt a front-matter span as the cited work)
3236	          exception_type: str — class name when failure_mode=exception
3237	    """
3238	    diagnostics: dict[str, Any] = {
3239	        "url": (url or "")[:200],
3240	        "attempted": False,
3241	        "method": "none",
3242	        "raw_char_count": 0,
3243	        "body_type": "",
3244	        "eligible": False,
3245	        "failure_mode": "",
3246	        "exception_type": "",
3247	    }
3248	    if not url:
3249	        diagnostics["failure_mode"] = "empty_url"
3250	        return "", diagnostics
3251	    # F15 (GH #1245 / D11): per-URL refetch cap + negative cache. A dead URL was
3252	    # refetched ~5.5x across expansion/deepener/agentic stages, re-paying the
3253	    # full AccessBypass cascade each time. `_refetch_try_acquire` returns False
3254	    # ONLY when the URL has SETTLED-FAILED the cap number of times (it is in the
3255	    # negative cache) — it gates on settled failures, NOT in-flight reservations,
3256	    # so a concurrent caller of a LIVE URL is never wrongly skipped (Codex diff-
3257	    # gate iter-3 P1). False => short-circuit with NO network call (cap is the
3258	    # reason). This is a PERFORMANCE de-dup; it never suppresses a fetch that
3259	    # could succeed (only a URL that has already FAILED cap times is cached), so
3260	    # no faithfulness gate is touched. `attempted` stays False so the caller sees
3261	    # this was skipped. The reserved-slot settle below counts a FAILURE toward the
3262	    # cap and leaves a SUCCESS uncapped.
3263	    if not _refetch_try_acquire(url):
3264	        logger.info(
3265	            "[refetch_for_extraction] skipping refetch of %s — failed the "
3266	            "per-URL cap=%d times (negative-cached, no further refetch)",
3267	            url[:80], _refetch_cap(),
3268	        )
3269	        diagnostics["failure_mode"] = "refetch_capped"
3270	        return "", diagnostics
3271	    # A slot is reserved; it MUST be settled exactly once. `_settled` tracks the
3272	    # success/failure outcome so the finally below settles correctly on EVERY
3273	    # post-acquire return path (success counts a slot release; a failure converts
3274	    # it into a recorded failure toward the cap).
3275	    _fetch_succeeded = False
3276	    try:
3277	        diagnostics["attempted"] = True
3278	        # I-deepfix-004 A (#1375): SEPARATE the FETCH-body cap from the QUOTE cap. Fetch
3279	        # the FULL body (default ``DEFAULT_CONTENT_MAX_CHARS`` = ``PG_LIVE_CONTENT_MAX`` =
3280	        # 300000) so ``_build_provenance_quote`` below can window a decimal DEEP in the
3281	        # body; the QUOTE stays capped at the caller's ``max_chars`` via
3282	        # ``max_total_chars`` (unchanged). OFF (``PG_REFETCH_FULL_BODY=0``) ⇒ fetch cap =
3283	        # ``max_chars`` ⇒ byte-identical to the prior head-truncating behaviour.

3285	        try:

3287	        except Exception as exc:

3290	            )
3291	            diagnostics["failure_mode"] = "exception"

3293	            # M-45 pass-2: read any telemetry recorded before the exception.
3294	            te = _m45_pop_fetch_telemetry(url)
3295	            diagnostics["method"] = te.get("method", "none")
3296	            return "", diagnostics  # failure (settled in finally)
3297	
3298	        diagnostics["raw_char_count"] = len(content) if content else 0
3299	        diagnostics["body_type"] = body_type or ""
3300	        # M-45 pass-2 (Codex audit HIGH): read the winning AccessBypass
3301	        # method + failure reason that `_fetch_content` recorded. Pre-
3302	        # pass-2 the method was always "none" because `_fetch_content`

3305	        if tele:

3307	            reason = tele.get("failure_reason", "")

3313	            # (set above) instead of overwriting with generic fetch_failed.

3363	        # DEFAULT-ON by ``PG_FETCH_SHELL_VOCAB_GATE``; OFF ⇒ byte-identical. A refused

3417	# I-meta-007c: OPEN-ACCESS resolver for the LIVE retrieval loop.
3418	#
3419	# When AccessBypass returns a stub/empty/paywalled result for a candidate that
3420	# carries a DOI (from S2 metadata or embedded in the URL), resolve the
3421	# best open-access full text BEFORE giving up. Fail-OPEN: any resolver error
3422	# returns "" and the caller falls through to its existing path — retrieval is
3423	# NEVER broken by a resolver failure.
3424	#
3425	# Fallback order (exact, per `.codex/I-meta-007/_wiring_specs.txt`
3426	# LANE wire:unpaywall-live-path):
3427	#   1. Unpaywall v2 best-OA URL  -> fetch via AccessBypass
3428	#   2. PubMed EFetch abstract (only when a PMID is available)
3429	#   3. else "" (caller keeps its existing stub/fallback behaviour)
3430	#
3431	# The OA fetch REUSES the existing AccessBypass stack (already budgeted in the
3432	# per-candidate fetch timeout) and the existing frame_fetcher parsers — no new
3433	# network budget, no duplicated parsing. All helpers are module-level so tests
3434	# can monkeypatch each seam independently (no real network, no spend).
3435	#
3436	# Env gates (LAW VI):
3437	#   PG_ENABLE_LIVE_OA_RESOLVER  default "1"  — master on/off switch.
3438	#   PG_UNPAYWALL_EMAIL          default placeholder — Unpaywall ToS email.
3439	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3440	# BB-007 (I-beatboth-fix-000 #1171): the placeholder Unpaywall email. The OA resolver treats this
3441	# (or an empty value) as "resolver unavailable" and fails LOUD (traces a distinct signal) rather than
3442	# issuing a doomed request that Unpaywall ToS rejects/throttles — a hidden cause of the fetch-fail rate.
3443	_UNPAYWALL_PLACEHOLDER_EMAIL = "polaris@example.org"
3444	
3445	
3446	def _oa_resolver_enabled() -> bool:
3447	    """True iff the live OA resolver is enabled. Off iff the env var is set to
3448	    a recognized falsey value ("0" / "false" / "no"); default ON."""
3449	    raw = os.getenv("PG_ENABLE_LIVE_OA_RESOLVER", "1").strip().lower()
3450	    return raw not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", "")
