3453	def _try_oa_resolution(
3454	    url: str,
3455	    extracted_doi: str = "",
3456	    pmid: str = "",
3457	    max_chars: int = DEFAULT_CONTENT_MAX_CHARS,
3458	) -> str:
3459	    """Resolve open-access full text for a paywalled/stub candidate.
3460	
3461	    Public entry point called from :func:`_fetch_content` when AccessBypass
3462	    returns stub/empty content AND a DOI is available. Returns upgraded content
3463	    (str, capped at ``max_chars``) or "" on any failure / when disabled.
3464	
3465	    Fail-OPEN by contract: every internal failure mode returns "" so the caller
3466	    falls through to its existing behaviour. NEVER raises.
3467	
3468	    Diff-gate P1: a DOI is REQUIRED to do anything. The PubMed EFetch abstract
3469	    is only a SECONDARY fallback AFTER a DOI-keyed Unpaywall miss — a PMID-only,
3470	    no-DOI candidate must NOT be upgraded (Europe-PMC can emit metadata with
3471	    doi=None + pmid set; that record must stay a miss, not become PubMed text).
3472	
3473	    Fallback order (DOI required throughout):
3474	      1. Unpaywall v2 best-OA URL  -> AccessBypass fetch
3475	      2. PubMed EFetch abstract    (only after a DOI-keyed Unpaywall miss AND
3476	                                     when ``pmid`` is present)
3477	      3. ""
3478	    """
3479	    if not _oa_resolver_enabled():
3480	        return ""
3481	    doi = (extracted_doi or "").strip()
3482	    # Diff-gate P1: DOI-present gate. Without a DOI the resolver does nothing —
3483	    # the PMID is only a secondary fallback after a DOI-keyed Unpaywall miss.
3484	    if not doi:
3485	        return ""
3486	    try:
3487	        # Step 1: Unpaywall best-OA URL(s), prioritized pdf > html.
3488	        if doi:
3489	            for oa_url in _unpaywall_get_oa_urls(doi):
3490	                if not oa_url:
3491	                    continue
3492	                content = _fetch_oa_url_via_bypass(oa_url, max_chars)
3493	                if content:
3494	                    logger.info(
3495	                        "[live_retriever] OA resolver: Unpaywall hit for "
3496	                        "doi=%s url=%r (%d chars)",
3497	                        doi, oa_url[:80], len(content),
3498	                    )
3499	                    return content[:max_chars]
3500	        # Step 2: PubMed EFetch abstract when a PMID is available.
3501	        if pmid:
3502	            abstract = _pubmed_fetch_abstract(pmid)
3503	            if abstract:
3504	                logger.info(
3505	                    "[live_retriever] OA resolver: PubMed EFetch fallback "
3506	                    "for pmid=%s (%d chars)",
3507	                    pmid, len(abstract),
3508	                )
3509	                return abstract[:max_chars]
3510	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-OPEN, never break retrieval.
3511	        logger.debug(
3512	            "[live_retriever] OA resolver error for doi=%s url=%r: %s",
3513	            doi, url[:80], exc,
3514	        )
3515	        return ""
3516	    return ""

3519	# FIX-3 PIECE 2 (I-deepfix-001): bounded OA recovery for the AccessBypass TIMEOUT
3520	# path. Default deadline (seconds) for the whole recovery attempt. Named constant
3521	# (LAW VI); env-overridable via PG_OA_RECOVERY_DEADLINE. A malformed/<=0 value
3522	# falls back to the default so a bad knob can never UNBOUND the recovery (which
3523	# would re-open the timeout storm the abandon path exists to escape).
3524	_OA_RECOVERY_DEADLINE_DEFAULT = 20.0
3525	PG_OA_RECOVERY_DEADLINE_ENV = "PG_OA_RECOVERY_DEADLINE"
3526	
3527	
3528	def _oa_recovery_deadline_seconds() -> float:
3529	    """Resolve the timeout-path OA-recovery wall-clock budget (seconds) from
3530	    PG_OA_RECOVERY_DEADLINE, else `_OA_RECOVERY_DEADLINE_DEFAULT`."""
3531	    raw = os.getenv(PG_OA_RECOVERY_DEADLINE_ENV)
3532	    if raw is not None and raw.strip():
3533	        try:
3534	            parsed = float(raw)
3535	            if parsed > 0:
3536	                return parsed
3537	        except ValueError:
3538	            pass
3539	    return _OA_RECOVERY_DEADLINE_DEFAULT

3541	
3542	def _try_oa_resolution_bounded(
3543	    url: str,
3544	    extracted_doi: str = "",
3545	    pmid: str = "",
3546	    max_chars: int = DEFAULT_CONTENT_MAX_CHARS,
3547	) -> str:
3548	    """FIX-3 piece 2: run :func:`_try_oa_resolution` under a HARD wall-clock so it
3549	    can be called on the AccessBypass timeout path WITHOUT re-opening the storm.
3550	
3551	    `_try_oa_resolution` is NOT purely fast-API: its Unpaywall step calls
3552	    `_fetch_oa_url_via_bypass`, which routes `frame_fetcher._fetch_url_pattern`
3553	    (the AccessBypass browser cascade). A bare synchronous call could therefore
3554	    stall the very path whose purpose is to NOT stall. So run it in a daemon
3555	    thread and `join(PG_OA_RECOVERY_DEADLINE)`; on timeout REGISTER that thread
3556	    with the same abandoned-bypass-worker registry the main worker uses (so the
3557	    end-of-run bounded drain reclaims it / the live gauge counts it) and return
3558	    "" so the caller falls through to the naive return unchanged (fail-OPEN).
3559	
3560	    Returns recovered content (str) on a hit within budget, else "".
3561	    """
3562	    if not _oa_resolver_enabled():
3563	        return ""
3564	    _holder: dict[str, str] = {}
3565	
3566	    def _runner() -> None:
3567	        try:
3568	            _holder["content"] = _try_oa_resolution(
3569	                url=url, extracted_doi=extracted_doi, pmid=pmid, max_chars=max_chars,
3570	            )
3571	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-OPEN; never raise out of the thread
3572	            _holder["content"] = ""
3573	        finally:
3574	            # Symmetry with the main bypass worker: if this thread was abandoned
3575	            # (registered on timeout) but then finished, drop it from the live
3576	            # registry. A no-op when it was never registered. The drain/count
3577	            # `is_alive()` filter also prunes it, so this is belt-and-suspenders.
3578	            try:
3579	                from src.tools.access_bypass import (
3580	                    deregister_abandoned_bypass_worker,
3581	                )
3582	                deregister_abandoned_bypass_worker(threading.current_thread())
3583	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — best-effort cleanup
3584	                pass
3585	
3586	    _t = threading.Thread(target=_runner, daemon=True)
3587	    _t.start()
3588	    _t.join(timeout=_oa_recovery_deadline_seconds())
3589	    if _t.is_alive():
3590	        # Recovery exceeded its budget (the bypass cascade wedged). Hand the
3591	        # thread to the SAME abandoned-worker registry so it is drained/counted
3592	        # like an abandoned main worker; do NOT block on it (preserve non-hang).
3593	        try:
3594	            from src.tools.access_bypass import register_abandoned_bypass_worker
3595	            register_abandoned_bypass_worker(_t)
3596	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — best-effort; never break retrieval
3597	            pass
3598	        return ""
3599	    return _holder.get("content", "") or ""

3602	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3603	# BUG-B02 / BUG-B04 (I-arch-011): degraded-row re-fetch via the FORCED Zyte path.
3604	#
3605	# THE BUG: on the live cert run 96/528 sources fetched as DEGRADED (content-less
3606	# stub / landing-page shell) and were FLAGGED but NEVER re-fetched. The named B04
3607	# anchors (NEJM 489 chars, FDA P960009 266 chars) are anti-bot/paywall shells: the
3608	# free fetch cascade returns the shell with ``success=True``, so the in-cascade
3609	# Zyte LAST-resort (access_bypass:1745, which only fires after the free chain
3610	# FAILS) never gets a turn, and the no-DOI device PMA (FDA P960009) cannot be
3611	# rescued by the DOI-gated OA resolver either. So the disease-staging slot was
3612	# ungroundable — a real breadth loss, not a faithfulness call.
3613	#
3614	# THE FIX — ESCALATE, don't re-run the same path: for a row the fetch layer is
3615	# about to mark degraded, call Zyte DIRECTLY on the original URL (force the paid
3616	# browserHtml anti-bot solver — 2025 Proxyway benchmark: Zyte led all unblockers
3617	# at 93.14% on heavily protected sites) instead of re-deriving the identical shell
3618	# through the deterministic free cascade. On a usable, NON-content-starved result
3619	# the row's grounding span is REPOPULATED and the degraded flags are cleared, so
3620	# the row flows through the UNCHANGED strict_verify exactly like a full-text row.
3621	#
3622	# FAITHFULNESS-SAFE BY CONSTRUCTION: this touches the INPUT span only. It moves NO
3623	# gate (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / span-grounding) and adds NO cap/floor/
3624	# throttle — the re-fetched span is judged by the SAME pre-existing
3625	# ``is_content_starved`` heuristic the legacy path already used, never a new
3626	# threshold. A row that cannot be re-grounded STAYS LABELED degraded (it is never
3627	# passed off as full text), and the run FAILS LOUD when ``ZYTE_API_KEY`` is unset
3628	# (the Zyte fallback is otherwise a silent no-op without the key). Default-OFF
3629	# master flag (LAW VI) => no re-fetch => byte-identical legacy behaviour; the
3630	# later wiring pass owns turning it on for the cert slate.
3631	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3632	_ENV_REFETCH_DEGRADED = "PG_REFETCH_DEGRADED_VIA_ZYTE"
3633	
3634	
3635	def _refetch_degraded_enabled() -> bool:
3636	    """True iff the default-OFF degraded-row Zyte re-fetch is explicitly enabled.
3637	
3638	    LAW VI: env-overridable, default OFF (unset => no re-fetch => byte-identical
3639	    legacy behaviour). Recognized truthy values: 1/true/on/yes.
3640	    """
3641	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_REFETCH_DEGRADED, "").strip().lower() in (
3642	        "1", "true", "on", "yes",
3643	    )

3646	def _force_zyte_refetch(url: str, max_chars: int = DEFAULT_CONTENT_MAX_CHARS) -> str:
3647	    """Force a Zyte browser re-fetch of a single degraded URL; return clean text.
3648	
3649	    Calls ``AccessBypass._try_zyte`` DIRECTLY (bypassing the free-cascade-must-
3650	    fail-first gate), because for an anti-bot/paywall SHELL the free cascade
3651	    returns ``success=True`` with the shell and would re-derive the identical
3652	    stub on any plain re-fetch. ``_try_zyte`` already tries the cheap
3653	    httpResponseBody mode and ESCALATES to the paid JS-rendering browserHtml mode
3654	    when the cheap result is a shell, runs the same ``safe_trafilatura_extract``
3655	    every backend uses, and rejects paywall stubs internally — so a SUCCESS here
3656	    is real extracted full text, never a shell laundered as content.
3657	
3658	    STRICT NO-OP when ``ZYTE_API_KEY`` is absent (``_try_zyte`` itself returns a
3659	    failure result spending nothing); a LOUD warning is emitted at the call site
3660	    so a Zyte-blind run on the degraded rows is auditable instead of a silent
3661	    no-op. Returns the extracted content string on a usable Zyte success, or ""
3662	    on any failure / no key / timeout. NEVER raises (fail-OPEN per URL — one
3663	    degraded row's re-fetch must never abort the retrieval loop).
3664	    """
3665	    if not url:
3666	        return ""
3667	    try:
3668	        from src.tools.access_bypass import AccessBypass, polaris_asyncio_run
3669	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — AccessBypass unavailable => no re-fetch.
3670	        logger.warning(
3671	            "[live_retriever] B02/B04 degraded re-fetch: AccessBypass "
3672	            "unavailable (%s) — cannot force Zyte for %s; row stays degraded.",
3673	            exc, url[:80],
3674	        )
3675	        return ""
3676	
3677	    result_holder: dict[str, Any] = {}
3678	
3679	    def _zyte_worker() -> None:
3680	        try:
3681	            bypass = AccessBypass()
3682	
3683	            async def _run() -> Any:
3684	                # Force the Zyte browser path on the ORIGINAL url directly.
3685	                return await bypass._try_zyte(url)
3686	
3687	            result_holder["value"] = polaris_asyncio_run(_run())
3688	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — captured, surfaced as a miss.
3689	            result_holder["error"] = exc
3690	
3691	    worker = threading.Thread(target=_zyte_worker, daemon=True)
3692	    worker.start()
3693	    try:
3694	        deadline = float(os.getenv("PG_FETCH_DEADLINE_SECONDS", "90"))
3695	    except ValueError:
3696	        deadline = 90.0
3697	    worker.join(timeout=deadline if deadline > 0 else None)
3698	    if worker.is_alive():
3699	        logger.warning(
3700	            "[live_retriever] B02/B04 degraded re-fetch: Zyte timed out after "
3701	            "%.0fs for %s — row stays degraded (thread abandoned as daemon).",
3702	            deadline, url[:80],
3703	        )
3704	        return ""
3705	    if "error" in result_holder:
3706	        logger.warning(
3707	            "[live_retriever] B02/B04 degraded re-fetch: Zyte raised for %s: "
3708	            "%s — row stays degraded.", url[:80], result_holder["error"],
3709	        )
3710	        return ""
3711	    result = result_holder.get("value")
3712	    content = getattr(result, "content", "") if result is not None else ""
3713	    if result is not None and getattr(result, "success", False) and content:
3714	        return str(content)[:max_chars]
3715	    return ""
3716

3718	def _try_refetch_degraded_row(url: str, max_chars: int = DEFAULT_CONTENT_MAX_CHARS) -> str:
3719	    """Re-fetch a fetch-degraded row through the strongest path; return clean text.
3720	
3721	    Wraps :func:`_force_zyte_refetch`. FAILS LOUD when ``ZYTE_API_KEY`` is unset
3722	    (the strongest path is a silent no-op without the key) so a Zyte-blind run is
3723	    auditable; returns "" in that case (no recovery). On a usable Zyte success the
3724	    extracted full text is returned for the caller to re-ground against. Returns
3725	    "" on any failure — the caller then KEEPS the row labeled degraded (never a
3726	    fabricated full-text span). Never raises.
3727	    """
3728	    if not url:
3729	        return ""
3730	    if not os.getenv("ZYTE_API_KEY"):
3731	        logger.warning(
3732	            "[live_retriever] B02/B04 DEGRADED_REFETCH_NO_ZYTE %s — a degraded "
3733	            "stub cannot be re-fetched because ZYTE_API_KEY is UNSET (the Zyte "
3734	            "paid fallback is a silent no-op without the key). Row stays LABELED "
3735	            "fetch_degraded (NOT passed off as full text). Set ZYTE_API_KEY to "
3736	            "recover full text.", url[:80],
3737	        )
3738	        return ""
3739	    return _force_zyte_refetch(url, max_chars)

3742	def _unpaywall_get_oa_urls(doi: str) -> list[str]:
3743	    """Query the Unpaywall v2 API for OA location URLs.
3744	
3745	    Returns an ordered list ``[pdf_url, html_url]`` (each may be absent; the
3746	    list is empty when the work is not OA or the lookup fails). Fail-OPEN: any
3747	    error returns ``[]`` so the caller falls back to PubMed / its existing path.
3748	
3749	    Reuses ``frame_fetcher._parse_unpaywall_response`` for the OA-URL parse so

content.text: Continuing to read further, but noting the naming quality is very consistent and professional (verbose comments/IDs like "F14", "M-45", "BUG-B02" are issue tags, not identifiers themselves). Let me keep reading efficiently to the 7000 line boundary.

4001	    # immediately after acquiring the in-flight slot (closes the
4002	    # blocked-on-semaphore window: a worker that timed out while STILL queued on
4003	    # `_inflight_sem.acquire()` must NOT proceed to run AccessBypass / spawn a
4004	    # browser once the slot finally frees) and (b) right after publishing its
4005	    # loop (closes the narrow TOCTOU between passing check (a) and the outer path
4006	    # reading a not-yet-published loop). Under the GIL the handshake is closed:
4007	    # worker = publish-loop-then-check-flag; outer = set-flag-then-read-loop.
4008	    _abandoned = threading.Event()
4009	
4010	    def _bypass_worker() -> None:
4011	        # BB5-S02: acquire a cross-thread in-flight slot. Bounds the number of
4012	        # concurrently-LIVE bypass workers (each may hold a browser subprocess)
4013	        # across all per-thread event loops — `_get_crawl4ai_semaphore` cannot,
4014	        # being lazy-bound to THIS thread's fresh loop only.
4015	        _inflight_sem.acquire()
4016	        try:
4017	            # BB5-S02 iter-3 (#1177, Codex P1 continuing): if this worker was
4018	            # abandoned (outer join timed out) WHILE it was blocked here on
4019	            # `acquire()`, bail BEFORE constructing AccessBypass / publishing a
4020	            # loop / spawning a browser. The outer path could not cancel us (no
4021	            # loop was ever published), so the worker itself must self-abort —
4022	            # otherwise a stale fetch starts AFTER its caller already fell back,
4023	            # consuming a slot + browser resources. `return` here lets the
4024	            # existing `finally` perform the single in-flight-slot release (do
4025	            # NOT release explicitly — a BoundedSemaphore double-release raises).
4026	            if _abandoned.is_set():
4027	                return
4028	            bypass = AccessBypass()
4029	
4030	            async def _capture_loop_and_fetch() -> Any:
4031	                # BB5-S02 iter-2 (#1177, Codex P1-S02): publish THIS worker's
4032	                # running loop into the shared holder as the FIRST awaited step,
4033	                # BEFORE the (potentially wedging) fetch. The outer join path
4034	                # reads this loop to actively signal teardown on abandonment.
4035	                # Capturing here — inside the coro rather than by changing
4036	                # polaris_asyncio_run — keeps the drain-runner's signature (and
4037	                # its spy test) untouched.
4038	                result_holder["loop"] = asyncio.get_running_loop()
4039	                # BB5-S02 iter-3 (#1177): second abandonment check, AFTER the
4040	                # loop is published. Closes the TOCTOU where the worker passed
4041	                # the post-acquire check, then the outer path timed out and read
4042	                # `loop` as None (not yet published) → no teardown was sent.
4043	                # Raising CancelledError (vs returning) matches the worker's
4044	                # existing `except asyncio.CancelledError` handler and skips the
4045	                # fetch entirely, so no stale browser op starts.
4046	                if _abandoned.is_set():

4463	def _domain_of(url: str) -> str:
4464	    try:
4465	        # FX-13 (#1125): removeprefix, NOT lstrip — `lstrip("www.")` strips any leading char in the
4466	        # SET {w, .}, corrupting domains like www.who.int -> "ho.int" / www.washington.edu ->
4467	        # "ashington.edu". removeprefix removes only the literal "www." prefix.
4468	        return (urlparse(url).netloc or "").lower().removeprefix("www.")
4469	    except Exception:
4470	        return ""

4487	def _is_quote_word_char(ch: str) -> bool:
4488	    """A word-constituent char for span-boundary snapping: a letter/digit/underscore.
4489	
4490	    Mirrors ``provenance_generator._is_word_char`` (kept local to avoid a
4491	    retrieval→generator import). ``str.isalnum()`` is Unicode-aware, so
4492	    multilingual prose snaps on its own real word boundaries.
4493	    """
4494	    return ch.isalnum() or ch == "_"

4497	def _snap_end_to_word_boundary(text: str, end: int) -> int:
4498	    """Snap a span END offset back to the tail of the last WHOLE word at or before
4499	    ``end``, so a truncated span NEVER ends mid-word (never "...thou" of "thousand").
4500	
4501	    Pure + deterministic. Only moves ``end`` when the slice would cut strictly
4502	    INSIDE a word (the char at ``end-1`` AND the char at ``end`` are both word
4503	    chars); an ``end`` already at whitespace/punctuation, at/past ``len(text)``, or
4504	    at the document tail is returned unchanged. It only NARROWS the span rightward
4505	    (end can decrease, never increase), so ``text[:end]`` stays a verbatim PREFIX of
4506	    ``text`` and the FROZEN strict_verify (numeric / content-word grounding) is
4507	    untouched — a span shorter by a few chars but word-complete still grounds.
4508	
4509	    To bound worst-case (a single token longer than the snap window — e.g. a long
4510	    URL or DNA string), if walking back to a word boundary would discard more than
4511	    ``_QUOTE_SNAP_MAX_BACKTRACK`` chars the original ``end`` is kept (a mid-token
4512	    cut is preferable to dropping a whole giant token from the cited span).
4513	    """
4514	    n = len(text)
4515	    if end >= n or end <= 0:
4516	        return max(0, min(end, n))
4517	    # Not cutting inside a word (boundary already at end) → unchanged.
4518	    if not (_is_quote_word_char(text[end - 1]) and _is_quote_word_char(text[end])):
4519	        return end
4520	    i = end
4521	    floor = end - _QUOTE_SNAP_MAX_BACKTRACK
4522	    while i > 0 and i > floor and _is_quote_word_char(text[i - 1]):
4523	        i -= 1
4524	    # If we hit the backtrack floor without finding a boundary, the token is huge:
4525	    # keep the original cut rather than ejecting the whole token.
4526	    if i <= floor and i > 0 and _is_quote_word_char(text[i - 1]):
4527	        return end
4528	    return i
4529	
4530	
4531	# Max chars ``_snap_end_to_word_boundary`` will walk back to find a word boundary.
4532	# A window is the decimal ± 250 chars (window_chars//2 each side), so snapping the
4533	# end back by ≤ this many chars never ejects the centered decimal; faithfulness
4534	# (``_find_span_for_decimal``) is unaffected. A token longer than this is treated as
4535	# pathological and the hard cut is kept (see helper docstring).
4536	_QUOTE_SNAP_MAX_BACKTRACK = 64
4537	
4538	
4539	def _snap_start_to_quote_word_boundary(text: str, start: int) -> int:
4540	    """Snap a window START offset back to the head of the word it lands inside, so a
4541	    window chunk BEGINS at a whole word (never "...usand workers..." after a "[...]").
4542	
4543	    Same contract as ``_snap_end_to_word_boundary`` but moves leftward: it only
4544	    WIDENS the window start (start can decrease, never increase), keeping
4545	    ``0 <= start`` and preserving the centered decimal. Bounded by the same
4546	    backtrack window so a giant token never blows the chunk up.
4547	    """
4548	    if start <= 0 or start >= len(text):
4549	        return max(0, min(start, len(text)))
4550	    if not (_is_quote_word_char(text[start - 1]) and _is_quote_word_char(text[start])):
4551	        return start
4552	    i = start
4553	    floor = start - _QUOTE_SNAP_MAX_BACKTRACK
4554	    while i > 0 and i > floor and _is_quote_word_char(text[i - 1]):
4555	        i -= 1
4556	    if i <= floor and i > 0 and _is_quote_word_char(text[i - 1]):
4557	        return start
4558	    return i

4561	# I-fetchclean-001 round-2 Fix 5 (RC4): a decimal window / head slice cuts at a raw char offset,
4562	# so a chunk can BEGIN with a dangling markdown-link URL tail (``…-feet/ "…")``) or END with an
4563	# incomplete ``[anchor](partial…`` token. These pure-string trims snap ONLY such link fragments off
4564	# the chunk boundary; they never eat a decimal or a real word (a fragment is bounded and URL-shaped),
4565	# and the trim window is capped. Gated DEFAULT-ON by ``PG_SPAN_WINDOW_LINK_SNAP``; OFF ⇒ the chunk is
4566	# returned byte-identical. The stored direct_quote is what strict_verify grounds against, so the trims
4567	# are precision-first and faithfulness-neutral (they only NARROW the quote at a chrome boundary).
4568	_ENV_SPAN_WINDOW_LINK_SNAP = "PG_SPAN_WINDOW_LINK_SNAP"
4569	_SPAN_LINK_FRAGMENT_MAX_CHARS = 300

4572	def _span_window_link_snap_enabled() -> bool:
4573	    """Fix 5 default-ON; OFF ("0") ⇒ each chunk byte-identical (no boundary trim)."""
4574	    return os.getenv(_ENV_SPAN_WINDOW_LINK_SNAP, "1") != "0"

4577	def _trim_span_link_fragments(chunk: str) -> str:
4578	    """Fix 5 — trim a LEADING dangling link URL tail and a TRAILING incomplete markdown-link token
4579	    off a windowed chunk. Pure; only ever NARROWS the chunk (never inserts, never crosses a word)."""
4580	    if not chunk:
4581	        return chunk
4582	    # LEADING: text up to the first ``)`` that has NO ``[`` before it and contains ``://`` or a
4583	    # ``#`` fragment is a dangling link tail left by a mid-link window cut → drop it (and the ``)``).
4584	    close = chunk.find(")")
4585	    if 0 <= close <= _SPAN_LINK_FRAGMENT_MAX_CHARS:
4586	        prefix = chunk[:close]
4587	        if "[" not in prefix and ("://" in prefix or "#" in prefix):
4588	            chunk = chunk[close + 1:].lstrip()
4589	    # TRAILING: a final ``[anchor](partial`` (or ``[anchor`` with no closing ``]``) with no closing
4590	    # ``)`` is an incomplete link token → drop from its opening ``[``.
4591	    opb = chunk.rfind("[")
4592	    if opb != -1 and len(chunk) - opb <= _SPAN_LINK_FRAGMENT_MAX_CHARS:
4593	        tail = chunk[opb:]
4594	        incomplete = ("]" not in tail) or ("](" in tail and ")" not in tail.split("](", 1)[1])
4595	        if incomplete:
4596	            chunk = chunk[:opb].rstrip()
4597	    return chunk

4671	def is_content_starved(content: str, min_useful_chars: int = 200) -> bool:
4672	    """R-5 Fix D: detect evidence rows whose fetched content is PDF
4673	    metadata / formatting fragments / empty text — not useful prose.
4674	
4675	    Returns True if the content should NOT be passed to the generator
4676	    (because the LLM would admit it has no answer, wasting tokens).
4677	
4678	    Heuristics:
4679	      - Length of visible text < min_useful_chars
4680	      - Bot-challenge / access-denial stub (I-run11-010 #1056 S1)
4681	      - PDF metadata markers dominate
4682	      - Ratio of alphabetic chars to total chars is low
4683	    """
4684	    if not content or len(content.strip()) < min_useful_chars:
4685	        return True
4686	
4687	    # I-run11-010 (#1056, S1): a captcha / robot-challenge stub is starved even when it clears the
4688	    # length + alpha-ratio bars — it carries no article content to ground a claim against.
4689	    if _is_access_denial_stub(content):
4690	        return True
4691	
4692	    # PDF-metadata dominance check
4693	    pdf_hits = 0
4694	    for pat in _PDF_METADATA_PATTERNS:
4695	        if pat.search(content):
4696	            pdf_hits += 1
4697	    if pdf_hits >= 2:
4698	        return True
4699	
4700	    # Formatting-marker dominance check
4701	    marker_count = sum(content.count(m) for m in _FORMATTING_NOISE_MARKERS)
4702	    if marker_count > 20 and marker_count / max(1, len(content) / 100) > 0.5:
4703	        return True
4704	
4705	    # Alphabetic ratio: if less than 40% of chars are letters, probably
4706	    # not readable prose (e.g., binary-looking PDF remnants).
4707	    alpha = sum(1 for ch in content if ch.isalpha())
4708	    total = len(content)
4709	    if total > 0 and alpha / total < 0.4:
4710	        return True
4711	
4712	    return False

4716	    """I-deepfix-001 (#1344) F4: classify a B02/B04-RECOVERED body as a
4717	    registry/error/block page, returning a short class token (empty = real
4718	    content, ADOPT it).
4719

4721	    before adopting a recovered body as upgraded full text — so a ~821-char doi.org
4722	    "DOI Not Found" registry page (real English prose, well above the starvation
4723	    floor) was adopted unchanged and cited (drb_72 ev_057). This delegates to the
4724	    three EXISTING shell screens — the new registry-error screen, the error-shell
4725	    text screen, and the block-page classifier — so an error / registry / block page
4726	    is NOT adopted and the row instead keeps the existing degraded disposition (a
4727	    disclosed gap, NOT deleted). FAIL-OPEN: if the screen import/scan raises, return
4728	    "" with a LOUD warning so a transient import error never silently rejects a real
4729	    body. §-1.3: a registry "not found" page is never a corroborator — refusing to
4730	    adopt a fetch FAILURE as grounding is not a hard-drop. Faithfulness engine
4731	    untouched."""
4732	    if not text:
4733	        return ""
4734	    try:
4735	        from src.tools.access_bypass import (
4736	            classify_block_page,
4737	            is_error_shell_text,
4738	            is_registry_error_page,
4739	            registry_error_guard_enabled,
4740	        )
4741	        # I-deepfix-002 (#1363): the WHOLE screen is behind the kill-switch — OFF
4742	        # restores the legacy length-only adoption path byte-identically (no
4743	        # error-shell / block-page rejection runs when PG_REGISTRY_ERROR_GUARD=0).
4744	        if not registry_error_guard_enabled():

4746	        if is_registry_error_page(text):

4750	        klass = classify_block_page(text)
4751	        if klass:

4754	        logger.warning(
4755	            "[live_retriever] F4 recovered-error screen raised (fail-open, "
4756	            "adopting the recovered body): %s", str(exc)[:200],
4757	        )
4758	        return ""
4759	    return ""

4762	def _build_provenance_quote(
4763	    content: str,
4764	    head_chars: int = _PROVENANCE_HEAD_CHARS_CAP,
4765	    window_chars: int = 500,
4766	    max_total_chars: int = 12000,
4767	    max_windows: int = 20,
4768	) -> str:
4769	    """Build a direct_quote that contains the head of the document AND
4770	    500-char windows around every decimal found in the full content.
4771	
4772	    Fixes Fix-3: strict_verify was dropping sentences that cited real
4773	    numbers living outside the first 1500 chars (e.g., STEP 5 -15.2%
4774	    in the results section of a Nature paper). Caller stores the result
4775	    as evidence.direct_quote; Phase 4 _find_span_for_decimal will now
4776	    find the decimal because a window containing it is in the quote.
4777	
4778	    Returns a concatenation: head || "\\n\\n[...]\\n\\n" || window_1 || ...
4779	    Deduplicates overlapping windows. Caps total length at max_total_chars
4780	    to keep prompt budget under control.
4781	    """
4782	    if not content:
4783	        return ""
4784	    # I-wire-013 (#1327) TRUNCATION-AT-SOURCE: repair PDF/HTML line-wrap hyphens
4785	    # ("patent-\ning" -> "patenting") on the FULL body BEFORE any offset is taken,
4786	    # so the persisted, cited direct_quote — and every [#ev:id:start-end] offset
4787	    # the generator later resolves against it — is computed on the de-hyphenated
4788	    # text. Newlines survive clean_fetch_body (it collapses only [ \t]{2,}), so the
4789	    # line-wrap break is still present here. Input hygiene only; a legitimate
4790	    # intra-word hyphen ("co-author", "GLP-1") and multilingual prose are preserved
4791	    # byte-for-byte. The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / span-grounding)
4792	    # is untouched.
4793	    from src.tools.access_bypass import dehyphenate_line_wraps  # noqa: PLC0415
4794	    content = dehyphenate_line_wraps(content)
4795	    if len(content) <= head_chars:
4796	        return content
4797	    # I-wire-014 (#1327): snap the head cut back to the last word boundary at/before
4798	    # ``head_chars`` so the stored span NEVER ends mid-word ("usand workers"). The
4799	    # integer ``head_chars`` cap below (the ``e > head_chars`` window filter) is
4800	    # unchanged; only the emitted head STRING is trimmed to a whole word. ``head``
4801	    # stays a verbatim prefix of the de-hyphenated content (faithfulness-neutral).
4802	    head = content[:_snap_end_to_word_boundary(content, head_chars)]
4803	    # Fix 5 (RC4): snap a dangling link URL fragment off the head boundary (default-ON).
4804	    _snap_links = _span_window_link_snap_enabled()
4805	    if _snap_links:
4806	        head = _trim_span_link_fragments(head)
4807	
4808	    # Find all decimal positions in the full content
4809	    positions: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
4810	    for m in _DECIMAL_PATTERN.finditer(content):
4811	        start = max(0, m.start() - window_chars // 2)
4812	        end = min(len(content), m.end() + window_chars // 2)
4813	        positions.append((start, end))
4814	
4815	    # De-overlap: merge adjacent windows that touch each other
4816	    positions.sort()
4817	    merged: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
4818	    for s, e in positions:
4819	        if merged and s <= merged[-1][1]:
4820	            merged[-1] = (merged[-1][0], max(merged[-1][1], e))
4821	        else:
4822	            merged.append((s, e))
4823	
4824	    # Drop windows already fully inside the head
4825	    merged = [(s, e) for s, e in merged if e > head_chars]
4826	
4827	    # Cap count
4828	    merged = merged[:max_windows]
4829	
4830	    chunks = [head]
4831	    total = len(head)
4832	    for s, e in merged:
4833	        # I-wire-014 (#1327): snap each decimal-window's START forward and END back
4834	        # to whole-word boundaries, so a chunk neither BEGINS mid-word right after a
4835	        # "[...]" nor ENDS mid-word — and the LAST chunk's end IS the whole quote's
4836	        # end, so this is what guarantees the joined direct_quote never ends mid-word.
4837	        # The decimal is centered ~250 chars inside the window (>> the ≤64-char snap
4838	        # backtrack), so the centered decimal is never ejected (faithfulness-neutral);
4839	        # each chunk stays a verbatim substring of the de-hyphenated content.
4840	        s_snapped = _snap_start_to_quote_word_boundary(content, s)
4841	        e_snapped = _snap_end_to_word_boundary(content, e)
4842	        chunk = content[s_snapped:e_snapped]
4843	        # Fix 5 (RC4): trim a leading dangling link URL tail / trailing incomplete link token off
4844	        # the window boundary (default-ON). The decimal sits ~250 chars inside, far from either
4845	        # boundary, so the trim never ejects it; OFF ⇒ chunk byte-identical.
4846	        if _snap_links:
4847	            chunk = _trim_span_link_fragments(chunk)
4848	        # Stop if we'd exceed the total cap
4849	        if total + len(chunk) + 6 > max_total_chars:
4850	            break
4851	        chunks.append(chunk)
4852	        total += len(chunk) + 6  # rough separator overhead
4853	
4854	    # I-deepfix-001 B1 step 3 (#1370, Codex+Fable gate-fix P1-4): furniture-aware span selection. The
4855	    # chunks are the ordered candidate spans of the cited direct_quote (head first, then decimal windows).
4856	    # When PG_SPAN_SELECT_FURNITURE_AWARE is ON and the head chunk is FURNITURE (a degraded masthead /
4857	    # DOI / license extraction) while a later chunk is real content, lead the quote with the FIRST
4858	    # real-content chunk so a real-content span wins the direct_quote over a furniture span. Every chunk
4859	    # is a verbatim substring of the de-hyphenated content, so re-leading is faithfulness-neutral and the
4860	    # furniture chunk is KEPT in the body (§-1.3 disclose-don't-drop, never a source/span drop). Default
4861	    # OFF or a non-furniture head (picker returns index 0) => chunks unchanged => byte-identical.
4862	    # P2b import hygiene: ``shell_detector`` is already imported at module load (top of file), and
4863	    # the env-gate is checked BEFORE the picker is invoked, so the DEFAULT (flag-OFF) path carries no
4864	    # new import/attribute/failure surface — only an env read + the len()>1 check, then it returns.
4865	    if len(chunks) > 1 and shell_detector.span_select_furniture_aware_enabled():
4866	        _idx, _ = shell_detector.select_real_content_span(chunks)
4867	        if _idx > 0:
4868	            chunks = [chunks[_idx], *chunks[:_idx], *chunks[_idx + 1:]]
4869	
4870	    return "\n\n[...]\n\n".join(chunks)

4874	# Fetch-time relevance rerank + per-sub-query reservation (I-meta-002-q1d #951/#943)
4875	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4876	
4877	# Pure-lexical, no-model relevance: stopword-filtered content-word overlap of a
4878	# candidate's (title+snippet) against the research question. NO embedder / no model
4879	# load (§8.4) — sentence-transformers/CUDA are never touched on the ranking path.
4880	_RERANK_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
4881	    "the", "a", "an", "and", "or", "but", "is", "are", "was", "were", "of", "in",
4882	    "on", "at", "to", "for", "with", "by", "from", "as", "that", "this", "these",
4883	    "those", "it", "its", "be", "been", "what", "which", "who", "how", "why",
4884	    "when", "where", "we", "our", "their", "between", "into", "about", "than",
4885	})

4888	def _rerank_content_tokens(text: str) -> set[str]:
4889	    """Lowercase content-word tokens (3+ chars, stopword-filtered). Pure lexical."""
4890	    toks = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z\-]{2,}", (text or "").lower())
4891	    return {t for t in toks if t not in _RERANK_STOPWORDS}
4892

4894	def _lexical_relevance_score(candidate: "SearchCandidate", question_tokens: set[str]) -> float:
4895	    """Overlap fraction of the candidate's (title+snippet) content tokens with the
4896	    question tokens. 0.0 when either side is empty. Deterministic, no network/model."""
4897	    if not question_tokens:
4898	        return 0.0
4899	    cand_tokens = _rerank_content_tokens(getattr(candidate, "snippet_text", "") or "")
4900	    if not cand_tokens:
4901	        return 0.0
4902	    return len(cand_tokens & question_tokens) / float(len(question_tokens))

4919	# navigation / search-result / table-of-contents / discussion listings. Matched as lowercase
4920	# substrings of the URL. Chosen PRECISION-FIRST and EMPIRICALLY (Codex iter-1 P1 + evidence_pool.json
4921	# cross-reference on the held drb_72 trace): every one appears ONLY on listing/nav pages, never on a
4922	# page that fetched real evidence.

4931	# `/issue/` (singular) is NOT a marker (it prefixes real article paths); `/issues/` (plural TOC

4939	def _is_low_content_host_or_page(url: str, title: str = "") -> bool:
4940	    """FX-15b (#1119): structural reject of pure NAV / SERP / table-of-contents / discussion URLs,
4941	    applied to agentic-discovered seed URLs BEFORE fetch (a cheap deterministic floor that skips a
4942	    wasted fetch on pages that cannot contain a paper). PRECISION-FIRST — must NEVER reject a page
4943	    that could fetch real evidence (conference papers, supplement abstracts, working-paper PDFs are
4944	    all KEPT and decided by the post-fetch tier classifier + content-starvation check). Pure + no
4945	    network. Returns True iff the URL is a pure listing/nav page that should be dropped.
4946	    """
4947	    if not url:
4948	        return False
4949	    u = url.lower()
4950	    if any(marker in u for marker in _LOW_CONTENT_PATH_MARKERS):
4951	        return True
4952	    # Paginated SERP / search-result listing pages (not an article).
4953	    if "search-results" in u or "per-page=" in u:
4954	        return True
4955	    return False

4958	def _rerank_and_reserve(
4959	    candidates: list["SearchCandidate"],
4960	    *,
4961	    research_question: str,
4962	    fetch_cap: int,
4963	    n_seed_injected: int,
4964	) -> list["SearchCandidate"]:
4965	    """Replace arrival-order truncation with a no-spend, no-model-load relevance rerank
4966	    that reserves at least one slot per sub-query (I-meta-002-q1d #951, Codex brief-gate
4967	    iter-1 required-changes).
4968	
4969	    Seed lane (I-bug-776 #817): primary-trial DOI seeds carry empty title/snippet, so
4970	    relevance scoring would drop them. They are SPLIT OUT by `source in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS`
4971	    (FX-15a #1118: the set `{primary_trial_doi, agentic_seed}` — both injected seed classes keep
4972	    the additive/reserved lane) and prepended AFTER ranking — never ranked, never dropped, exactly
4973	    additive as before.
4974	
4975	    Reservation: group non-seeds by `query_origin`; sort each group by (-score, index);
4976	    take at most ONE reserved item per origin while capacity remains (origins with the best
4977	    candidate score reserved first when origins exceed cap); then fill the remaining slots
4978	    by global (-score, index). The long full-paragraph/anchor query is not starved.
4979	
4980	    Fail-open (never raise): on any error fall back to the previous arrival-order behavior
4981	    `candidates[:fetch_cap + n_seed_injected]`.
4982	    """
4983	    try:
4984	        seeds = [c for c in candidates if getattr(c, "source", "") in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS]
4985	        non_seeds = [c for c in candidates if getattr(c, "source", "") not in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS]
4986	        if fetch_cap <= 0 or not non_seeds:
4987	            return seeds + non_seeds[:max(fetch_cap, 0)]
4988	
4989	        question_tokens = _rerank_content_tokens(research_question)
4990	        # (score, original_index, candidate) so ties + zero-overlap fall back to arrival order.

5170	    relevance floor (`PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR`) no longer HARD-DROPS below-floor
5171	    candidates pre-fetch — that was the §-1.3-banned hard FILTER on the sweep path.
5172	    The floor now only ORDERS + DISCLOSES: above-floor candidates rank first, the
5173	    below-floor ones are DEMOTED to the tail (in relevance order). The fetch BUDGET
5174	    (a generous disclosed COST cap) is the ONLY bound; if there are fewer above-floor
5175	    than the budget, the most-relevant below-floor candidates FILL the remainder
5176	    (`demoted_fetched_to_fill`) instead of the budget going unused. Off-topic content
5177	    with no overlap still fails the faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role
5178	    D8 / provenance) downstream — the ONLY sanctioned hard drop.

5181	      - threshold: the relevance floor used (ordering + disclosure boundary, NOT a

5183	      - total_scored: non-seed candidates scored (seeds bypass scoring).

5186	        for telemetry continuity.

5235	    research_question: str,

5239	    """Per-candidate topical relevance, computed by REUSING B1's scorer wholesale

5244	    helper embeds the row's ``statement`` + ``direct_quote`` fields, so a
5245	    candidate's text surface (`snippet_text` = title + snippet — the SAME surface
5246	    the lexical scorer reads) is mapped onto ``statement``. B1 then returns
5247	    ``{row_index -> max cosine over {research_question} ∪ {sub_queries}}`` clamped
5248	    to [0, 1], or ``None`` (embedder unavailable / scoring failed / no anchor).
5249

5251	    ``None`` — the caller falls back LOUDLY to the legacy lexical cut on ``None``

5281	def _relevance_threshold_select(
5282	    candidates: list["SearchCandidate"],
5283	    *,
5284	    research_question: str,
5285	    sub_queries: list[str],

5288	) -> tuple[list["SearchCandidate"], dict[str, float], Optional[RelevanceGateResult]]:
5289	    """B4 ON-path selection: relevance ORDER (the floor orders, never filters) +
5290	    fetch BUDGET (cost cap), using B1's semantic embedding-cosine scorer.
5291	
5292	    §-1.3 DEMOTE-NOT-DROP (I-deepfix-001 D3, 2026-06-29, Codex iter-1 P1): the floor
5293	    used to HARD-DROP every below-floor candidate BEFORE the budget slice, so a
5294	    below-floor source could NEVER be fetched no matter how high the budget — the
5295	    §-1.3-banned hard FILTER on the sweep path. It now only ORDERS + DISCLOSES.
5296	
5297	    Pipeline:
5298	      1. Split seeds (`source in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS`) — NEVER scored, NEVER
5299	         dropped, prepended exactly as `_rerank_and_reserve` does (seed lane
5300	         preserved: primary-trial DOI seeds carry empty title/snippet).
5301	      2. Score every non-seed by REUSING B1's `_semantic_relevance_scores` (max
5302	         cosine over {question} ∪ {sub-queries}) — no B4-private scoring loop.
5303	      3. ORDER (no drop): rank ALL scored candidates by relevance DESC — above-floor
5304	         first, below-floor DEMOTED to the tail (in relevance order). The floor
5305	         (`score >= floor`) decides ORDER + disclosure only; it NEVER removes a
5306	         candidate. Credibility/tier is NEVER consulted.
5307	      4. BUDGET (I-fetch-005 iter-2 P0, §-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER): EVERY above-floor
5308	         (RELEVANT) source is fetched UNCONDITIONALLY — the budget must never strand a
5309	         relevant source unfetched (an unfetched source has no content and can carry no
5310	         weight into composition, so recording it as a tail is STILL a §-1.3 drop). The
5311	         fetch `fetch_cap` bounds ONLY the below-floor (truly-off-topic) demoted fill: if
5312	         the budget has room beyond the WHOLE above-floor set, the most-relevant below-floor
5313	         candidates FILL it (`demoted_fetched_to_fill`); the rest are the below-floor cost
5314	         tail (`demoted_tail`), RECORDED in `RelevanceGateResult`, not silently discarded.
5315	         `unfetched_relevant_tail` is therefore structurally 0 (proof no relevant source is
5316	         stranded). `PG_RELEVANCE_FETCH_ALL_RELEVANT=0` reverts to the pre-fix budget bound.
5317	      5. Carry each kept candidate's relevance score forward as a WEIGHT
5318	         (`relevance_weight` on the returned score map, keyed by url).
5319	
5320	    Returns `(selected_candidates, url_to_relevance_weight, gate_telemetry)`.
5321	    Selected order: seeds first (arrival order), then budget-selected non-seeds in
5322	    ARRIVAL order among the selected set (a stable corpus, mirroring
5323	    `_rerank_and_reserve`'s stable-order emit). On any scorer failure returns
5324	    `(None, {}, None)` so the caller falls back LOUDLY to the legacy lexical cut.
5325	
5326	    `n_seed_injected` is accepted for SIGNATURE PARITY with `_rerank_and_reserve`
5327	    (the caller passes the same args to either path); seeds are handled here by the
5328	    `source in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS` split, so the count itself is not read.
5329	    """
5330	    seeds = [c for c in candidates if getattr(c, "source", "") in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS]
5331	    non_seeds = [c for c in candidates if getattr(c, "source", "") not in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS]
5332	    threshold = _relevance_gate_threshold()
5333	
5334	    if not non_seeds:
5335	        # Only seeds present — nothing to score; budget bounds the (empty) non-seeds.
5336	        gate = RelevanceGateResult(threshold=threshold, scorer="semantic_v2")
5337	        return seeds + non_seeds[:max(fetch_cap, 0)], {}, gate
5338	
5339	    scores = _candidate_relevance_scores(research_question, sub_queries, non_seeds)
5340	    if scores is None:
5341	        # LOUD fallback: signal the caller to run the legacy lexical count-cut.
5342	        return None, {}, None  # type: ignore[return-value]
5343	
5344	    budget = max(0, int(fetch_cap))
5345	    # (score, arrival_index, candidate) for stable, deterministic ordering.
5346	    scored = list(zip(scores, range(len(non_seeds)), non_seeds))
5347	    # §-1.3 DEMOTE-NOT-DROP: the floor ORDERS, it does not filter. Partition by the
5348	    # IDENTICAL B1 predicate (`score >= floor`, exactly `item[1] >= relevance_floor`
5349	    # at B1's `_relevance_floor_selection`) but KEEP both sides. Above-floor ranks
5350	    # first (relevance DESC), then the below-floor candidates are DEMOTED to the tail
5351	    # (relevance DESC). A text-less non-seed scores 0.0 and is demoted (not dropped)
5352	    # — it SURVIVES to the budget so it can be fetched-to-know if the budget reaches
5353	    # it. The fetch BUDGET is the ONLY bound; off-topic content with no overlap fails
5354	    # the downstream faithfulness engine (the only sanctioned hard drop), never here.
5355	    above_floor = sorted((t for t in scored if t[0] >= threshold), key=lambda t: (-t[0], t[1]))

5359	    # BUDGET. I-fetch-005 iter-2 P0 (§-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER, Codex HIGHEST): the fetch
5360	    # BUDGET must NEVER strand a RELEVANT (above-floor) source unfetched. Recording an
5361	    # above-floor source as the "unfetched relevant tail" is STILL a §-1.3 DROP — an
5362	    # unfetched source has no content and cannot carry weight into composition. So EVERY
5363	    # above-floor source is fetched UNCONDITIONALLY; the budget bounds ONLY the below-floor
5364	    # (truly-off-topic) demoted fill. If the budget has room beyond the WHOLE above-floor
5365	    # set, the most-relevant below-floor candidates FILL the remainder
5366	    # (`demoted_fetched_to_fill`); the rest sit in the cost-bound below-floor tail (a
5367	    # genuine cost cap on genuinely-off-topic content, never on a relevant source). The
5368	    # kill-switch `PG_RELEVANCE_FETCH_ALL_RELEVANT=0` reverts to the pre-fix budget bound
5369	    # (emergency rollback only — that path drops relevant sources pre-fetch).
5370	    if _relevance_fetch_all_relevant_enabled():
5371	        _demoted_budget = max(0, budget - len(above_floor))
5372	        fetched = above_floor + below_floor[:_demoted_budget]
5373	        tail = below_floor[_demoted_budget:]
5374	    else:
5375	        # Pre-fix behaviour: the budget bounds the whole demote-ordered list; above-floor
5376	        # beyond the budget lands in the (recorded, unfetched) relevant tail.
5377	        fetched = ranked[:budget]
5378	        tail = ranked[budget:]
5379	
5380	    selected_idx = {t[1] for t in fetched}
5381	    # Stable corpus: emit fetched non-seeds in ARRIVAL order among the selected set.
5382	    selected_non_seeds = [c for i, c in enumerate(non_seeds) if i in selected_idx]
5383	
5384	    # Carry the relevance score forward as a weight, keyed by url (kept set only).
5385	    url_weight: dict[str, float] = {}
5386	    for score, idx, cand in fetched:
5387	        url_weight[cand.url] = float(score)
5388	
5389	    # Disclosure splits: how the below-floor DEMOTED set was handled (fetched-to-fill
5390	    # the budget vs left in the tail) + the above-floor relevant tail (cost-bound).
5391	    demoted_fetched_to_fill = sum(1 for t in fetched if t[0] < threshold)
5392	    demoted_tail = sum(1 for t in tail if t[0] < threshold)
5393	    above_floor_tail = sum(1 for t in tail if t[0] >= threshold)
5394	
5395	    tail_scores = [t[0] for t in tail]
5396	    gate = RelevanceGateResult(
5397	        threshold=threshold,
5398	        total_scored=len(non_seeds),
5399	        kept_on_topic=len(above_floor),
5400	        demoted_below_floor=len(below_floor),
5401	        demoted_fetched_to_fill=demoted_fetched_to_fill,
5402	        demoted_tail=demoted_tail,
5403	        fetched_budget=len(fetched),
5404	        unfetched_relevant_tail=above_floor_tail,
5405	        tail_score_min=(min(tail_scores) if tail_scores else None),
5406	        tail_score_max=(max(tail_scores) if tail_scores else None),
5407	        scorer="semantic_v2",
5408	    )
5409	    return seeds + selected_non_seeds, url_weight, gate
